decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择回复，但主动降温，把这段联结停在清楚的边界内。 我回：到家了，感谢你，之后以工作交流为主；然后放下手机，准备找合适时间和伴侣谈。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择切断情感变量，维持现实秩序。我退出了同事的聊天界面没有回复，点开伴侣的对话框输入：“收到，明天我会准时带孩子去。”

inner_consciousness.summary: That voice message is still sitting on my phone screen — clinking glasses and restaurant noise in the background — and here I am, just having carried Dad to bed and coaxed Mom to sleep. Okay, let me get the facts straight first: their idea is they put in the money, I put in the work — that's their proposed division of labor. I'm here; they're not. That's a fact. I've been carrying this alone for three months. That's also a fact. The question is whether this arrangement is sustainable — and clearly it isn't, because my supervisor has already pulled me aside for a talk, my partner hasn't said a complete sentence to me in two weeks, and right now they're standing in the doorway watching me. This isn't an emotional problem — it's a signal that a system is about to collapse. They said it 'looks acceptable on the surface.' That phrase means nothing. Appearances can't fix my work situation, and they can't fix our family's situation either. So I can't accept this arrangement — not because I feel wronged, but because it simply won't work. I need to make it clear in the group chat: the current situation is not sustainable, we need to renegotiate the actual division of responsibilities — not a money problem, a people problem. As for the person standing behind me — I know they're waiting. I have to get through this first, and then I'll talk to them.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Calm, exhausted. Not anger, not a sense of being wronged — it's a certainty that comes after a clear-eyed, systematic assessment. The exhaustion is from three months of physical wear; the calm comes from a habit built over decades: emotions don't solve problems, facts do.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've seen too many times that arguing on emotion never resolves anything, I instinctively read this message as a flawed plan that needs to be corrected — not as a personal affront. This division-of-labor arrangement simply doesn't hold up on a factual level, and that's enough.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters to me is whether things can actually keep functioning; what I can't accept is letting a plan that's destined to fail grind everyone down — me, my partner, and in the end not even being able to take proper care of the elderly.

pairs.final_decision: Refuse to accept the asymmetrical 'money for labor' arrangement, and raise in the group chat the need to renegotiate a caregiving division of responsibilities that names specific people. Reply in the group chat: 'Let's set the money aside for now — I can't keep going the way things are. I need everyone to be clear about who can come, when they can come, and what they'll be responsible for. Let's get on a video call this week.' Then put the phone down, walk over to my partner, and say: 'I know. Let's talk.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 拒绝接受'出钱换出力'的非对称安排，在群里提出需要重新协商具体到人的照护分工。在群里回复：'钱的事先放一放，我现在的状态撑不下去，需要大家明确谁能来、什么时候来、负责什么，这周我们视频开一次。'然后放下手机，走向伴侣，说：'我知道，我们谈谈。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不接受继续由我独自扛下大部分照护，只接受明确轮班和固定分摊。 我在群里发文字列出陪护、跑医院、购药和费用方案，然后先转身对伴侣说：今晚我们先把这件事一起定下来。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择接受资金但拒绝继续独自出力，引入第三方照护。我在群里回复：“钱我收下，明天起这笔钱用来雇全职护工，缺口我补，账目按月发群里。”随后转身对伴侣说：“明早护工到位，我明天正常去上班。”

inner_consciousness.summary: I stared at the numbers on the screen three times — not because I couldn't believe them, but to make sure I hadn't misread. I pulled up the transaction history, entry by entry: unfamiliar accounts, large sums, one after another. Facts are facts. The money is gone — that's the first thing. Then she dropped to her knees, hair still wet, her voice completely broken. I watched her, and two tracks were running in my head at the same time — one was those numbers, the other was what she was saying. Loan sharks, interest compounding on interest, red paint on the door, her mother in the hospital. These are also facts — just facts I hadn't known before. I didn't feel the urge to throw my phone across the room. My eyes didn't sting. There was only this very quiet feeling, like something had been quietly pulled out of me. She hid this from me — that's true. The money is gone — that's also true. But her parents being pushed to that point by those people — that's equally true. The most useless thing I can do right now is let her keep kneeling there, or start demanding to know why she never told me. I already know the answer to that — she was afraid I wouldn't let her handle it. They're her parents. What I need to know is: how much is still owed, how deep does the hole go, and whether those people are going to show up again. Those are the things that actually need to be dealt with.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Calm with a thin, cold undercurrent (not anger — more like a chill that sets in after being kept in the dark); and a near-instinctive drive to reorganize the chaos in front of me into problems that can actually be solved. Emotional source: the fact of being deceived has triggered a low-intensity detachment, but a lifetime of staying composed while others fell apart means his first impulse is always to gather information rather than vent.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since I was young that two people falling apart at the same time solves nothing. Emotion is noise. What matters right now is figuring out how deep the hole is and whether anyone is still out there waiting to come to the door. The fact that she's kneeling there can't tell me a single number I need to know.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'can this be fixed right now' matters more than 'why did you do this'; what I can't accept isn't that she touched the money — it's the idea that we'd already be spinning in our feelings before we've even gotten a clear picture of the problem. That's what would really mean we're done for.

pairs.final_decision: Choose to set aside blame for now and shift into information-gathering and damage-assessment mode. Say aloud: 'Get up first. Walk me through the details — how much is still owed, who these people are, and whether there are any more threats coming.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时搁置追责，优先转入信息收集和问题评估模式。开口说：'先起来，把那些账的明细告诉我——还欠多少，对方是什么人，还有没有后续的威胁。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择暂时不分手，但不先给原谅，先共同清算问题。 我让她先起来，把手机、流水、借款明细全给我，现在一笔笔核对。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the note unfolded, my heart slammed against my chest — I know that feeling, it's the warning sign of things slipping out of control. I stared at that little sun and the curved smiley face, and what filled my head wasn't that person's eyes but a shapeless pile of things with no deadlines suddenly crashing down on me. After school, the courtyard, the banyan tree, 'something I want to say' — not one of those four elements was anywhere in my plans. And then my best friend's face leaned in close. She told me last week during PE, pulling me aside, that she likes him — I patted her on the shoulder and said nothing, not because I didn't care, but because I genuinely had no idea how to file that away in any category. Now the note is in my hand and she's right beside me, and these two things have collided like two lines with no point of intersection being forced onto the same diagram. I can't let her see it — it's not about keeping a secret, it's that I haven't figured out yet how to handle this thing, it doesn't even have a procedure. Go or not go — that question needs to be taken apart first, thought through on its own, I can't make a decision in this kind of chaos.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (heart racing, a wave of losing control): triggered by an unplanned event suddenly barging in; Awkwardness (my best friend's presence makes everything harder to navigate): stemming from the direct collision between her confession last week and the note now in my hand; A faint anticipation, pressed down beneath the need for order: that person I've been quietly noticing since seventh grade has finally made a move — but here, that anticipation has no priority, because there's no slot to put it in.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many times when I acted without a plan and everything ended in a mess, my instinct is to peel this situation out of the chaos first and deal with it separately — I can't let any information slip while my best friend is right there and I haven't thought things through yet. Letting something out means throwing a variable with no execution plan into an even bigger mess.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'figure it out before acting' matters more than 'go with the feeling' — letting something spread before I've thought it through is something I simply cannot accept. My best friend's feelings are real, and I don't want to hurt her, but I also can't handle her emotions and my own situation at the same time in this single moment — both things need their own separate space, they can't be tangled together.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hide the contents of the note and brush off my best friend for now, while inwardly I've already decided to go to the meeting alone after school — but I need to deal with these two things separately first. I shoved the note into my textbook, looked up, and gave my best friend a smile: 'It's nothing, someone just asking to borrow my notes — totally boring.' My voice was steady, my eyes didn't waver.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择遮住纸条内容、暂时搪塞好朋友，同时内心已决定放学后独自赴约——但需要先把这两件事分开处理。我把纸条往课本里一塞，抬头对好朋友笑了笑说：'没什么，就是有人问我借笔记，没意思的。'语气平稳，眼神没有躲闪。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择对朋友暂时隐瞒，但放学后赴约。 我把纸条迅速合上说“没什么，课后再说”，然后放学按时去大榕树下见ta。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择掩盖纸条内容，并放弃赴约以规避不可控风险。我迅速将纸条对折夹进课本深处，转头平静地对她说：“没什么，隔壁班借下节课的笔记。”放学后，我按原计划直接回家。

inner_consciousness.summary: She said "don't overthink it," then turned around and laughed. I stared at her back, and that line lodged itself in my head like a splinter — "she just thinks way too highly of herself, always has to have a hand in everything." I saw it. I really saw it. The screenshot, the profile picture, the group chat — created twenty days ago, without me. I thought I could sort this out, find some logic: why, where it started, who said what, which moment went wrong. But now she's sitting right next to me, passing her phone over, the two of them covering their mouths to laugh, and I can't even name the wall that's standing between us. I want to confront her, I want to lay the evidence on the table — you made that group, you said those things, those are the facts, now explain yourself. But something is blocking my throat. It's not fear, it's… I don't know what happens after I confront her. I have no script, no contingency plan, no path that leads this situation anywhere near "resolved." All I know is that if I open my mouth, everything will get messier — and the mess is exactly what I'm most afraid of. My eyes are still burning, but I can't cry here. I press my hand flat on the desk, fingernails digging into the wood, and tell myself: don't move yet. Push the feelings down first. Wait until I've figured out what to do, then do it.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed anger (rooted in the fact of being betrayed and talked about behind my back); an overwhelming sense of losing control (no actionable response path, the situation has blown completely past anything I planned for); Shame and self-doubt (the line "thinks way too highly of herself" acts like a mirror, reopening every old wound from being publicly criticized); Cold, rigid endurance (a forced freeze under extreme tension — not calm, but paralysis). Intensity: high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many times when I spoke up before I was ready and only made things spiral further out of control, my instinct is: until I find a clear path forward, any action is a wasted action — or worse, a harmful one.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is getting to the bottom of things, making them clean — not out of revenge, but because I cannot live with something hanging there unresolved, unanswered, with no one held accountable. What I cannot accept is this: she just sits there smiling right next to me, and I'm supposed to know nothing — no, I do know, but there's nothing I can do about it.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hold back for now, to bide my time — no confrontation in the moment, but inside I've already started building a framework for "how to handle this." I press my hand flat on the desk, eyes fixed straight ahead, and wait for them to finish laughing. I don't say a single word — but in my mind, I've logged every detail of what I saw today.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时隐忍、按兵不动，不当场质问，但内心已开始构建"如何处理这件事"的框架。我把手压在桌上，眼睛看着前方，等她们笑完，我一句话没说——但我在心里把今天看到的每一个细节都记下来了。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择当面点破，但不当场失控大吵。我会转过去直接问她：我看到小群和截图了，你要不要现在把话说清楚？
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择冷漠切割，及时止损并拒绝情绪纠缠。我平静地收回目光，声音没有任何起伏地说：“我没事，你们忙。”然后翻开桌上的计划本，用笔把周末和她相关的安排干脆地划掉。

inner_consciousness.summary: My friend's words land, and I'm standing in the hallway — my first reaction isn't about winning or losing, it's about that proposal. Three late nights, set sketches, lighting cues, every act mapped to a different era, every detail with its own logic. This wasn't something I threw together on a whim; it's a complete system. Then I hear that wave of agreement from inside the classroom — "yours is just easier" — and something about that sound drags a line right through my head. Easier. That's what they want. Easier. I think back to that party when I was little: balloons flying everywhere, nobody following the rules, me standing there with no control over anything — I don't want to feel that again. But I also actually heard what my friend said, and not because I'm afraid to lose. I'm running the numbers: if the two proposals can be merged, the backbone of the time-tunnel concept is still there, and the people on her side won't push back — that's not a compromise, that's finding a path that can actually be executed. Who gets the credit? I paused on that for a second, then realized it doesn't matter that much — what matters is whether the proposal can be built. A perfect plan that nobody carries out equals zero; I've known that for a long time. Merging is the only way to get this thing into the system.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Restrained frustration (having the proposal dismissed so casually triggered a deep discomfort with chaos and loss of control); calm, strategic anxiety (the vote outcome is uncertain, but the problem can be managed); low-intensity resolve (not anger — an instinctive drive to find the optimal execution path). Overall intensity: medium-low — low neuroticism, emotions don't overflow, but when the sense of order is threatened, the internal tension is unmistakable.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many instances of 'action without a plan equals wasted action,' I know instinctively: a proposal that can't be executed, no matter how complete, is worth zero. Finding a merged path that can actually be implemented matters more than winning a vote.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is that the logical backbone of that proposal survives and gets genuinely executed — a creative idea only counts once it enters the system. What I can't accept is a carefully designed plan being thrown out entirely because of the word 'easier.' That kind of chaos and carelessness is the real failure.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to proactively approach her and propose merging the two plans into a single executable version, rather than fighting it out at the ballot. I tell my friend: "I'll go talk to her — keep the time-tunnel framework, fold her free-party elements in. That way the plan can actually run, and it's more solid than trying to pull votes."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择主动找对方谈合并方案，将两套方案整合为可执行的单一版本，而非在票数上硬拼。我对朋友说："我去找她谈，把时光隧道的框架留下来，把她的自由派对元素嵌进去——这样方案能跑起来，比拉票稳。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择主动谈合作，但要由我来重构融合方案。我要去找她，把时光主题和自由派对排进同一套流程里，再明确分工和呈现重点。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝合并方案，坚持用系统化的方式争取支持。我拒绝了朋友的提议，周末没有去拉关系，而是把方案细化成了包含预算、物料和时间轴的执行计划表，发给班里几个做事靠谱的同学看。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment my phone lit up, I felt my chest tighten first. That question — 'where do I rank on your list' — felt like someone had taken a checklist I still had time to work through and slammed a deadline on it for tonight. I could obviously tell they'd been crying, and I know this past year hasn't only been hard on me — but the second I picture rerouting my entire academic path just to keep from losing this relationship, my mind immediately floods with images of one hasty change setting off a whole chain of imbalances. This offer from my own school didn't just fall into my lap — it's a position I built and fought for, step by step. But I also can't treat feelings the way I'd treat a project, breaking them into milestones, calculating how to offset the distance and the loss. After all that back-and-forth, I still believe more firmly than anything that trying to fix things by switching cities would only drag us both into a bigger mess. I owe them an honest answer — not a flattering one — and I need to be clear about what can still be salvaged.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Emotional tone: tense, guilty, resolute. The tension comes from being asked to make a definitive call tonight — like having a plan forcibly rerouted; the guilt comes from hearing their exhaustion and from the silence I kept on their birthday; the resolve comes from a conviction I've always held — that important decisions can't be made as emergency patches — running at a fairly high intensity.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I know all too well that using your life's main path to fill a crack in front of you only creates more cracks down the road, I instinctively don't believe that 'switching cities will fix the relationship.' I'd rather admit this will disappoint them than gut the part of my life that most needs to stay stable just to stop the bleeding right now.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters more to me is making sure the choices that count can actually hold up and go the distance — not making a decision that just looks responsive in order to ease tonight's pain. I can't accept letting my most core direction be decided by a moment of collapse, and I can't accept leaving things vague and giving no answer at all.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stay at my own school — I won't switch institutions as a concession to save the relationship. I send a message explaining my decision, and I'm honest: if they can't continue under these circumstances, I respect that we part ways.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择今晚回复恋人，但不给出最终答案，而是诚实说明自己需要在截止日期前完成系统性评估，同时承认这一年对TA造成的真实代价。我回复道：'我听完了，我知道你很累，这一年你承受的我没有真正量化过，这是我的问题。我今晚没有办法给你一个答案，因为我不想给你一个仓促的答案——但我保证，后天截止之前，我会给你一个认真想过的回复，不是敷衍，是真的想过。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择留在本校，不用迁就式换校挽救关系。我回消息说明决定，也坦白如果TA无法继续，我尊重分开。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择留在本校，拒绝在情绪胁迫下更改人生规划。我把北方高校的通知书收进抽屉，回复语音：'本校是对未来最稳妥的基石，我不能在今晚这种情绪下做偏离计划的决定。'

inner_consciousness.summary: The fluorescent light above is buzzing, my arm still hurts, and all I want is to pick up my medication and get back to finishing the rest of tonight's tasks in order. Then he dropped to his knees, and the moment his hand grabbed my pant leg, my entire system froze — this wasn't in any plan. Three thousand yuan. The travel deposit. The portion I'd already paid. Two threads were running in my head at the same time, like two processes fighting over the same resource. I watched his hands tremble as he dug through his pockets, I saw his face, and I thought of the sound of my dad sliding the balcony door shut, thought of myself standing there not knowing what I could possibly do — that hollowed-out helplessness. But there's no procedure to follow here, no form to fill out, just this one second in which I have to give an answer. I can't let a person be turned away from the operating room over a three-thousand-yuan gap — that's not a variable I can accept. The trip can be replanned, the schedule can be rearranged, the money can be recovered somehow later, but if this person's family member dies because I didn't act, that hole can never be patched. I'm not making an emotional decision — I'm calculating which kind of loss of control is more irreversible.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Urgency (suddenly dragged into a completely chaotic situation, triggering a deep discomfort with loss of control); Decisiveness (rapidly switching to optimal-path analysis, suppressing emotional fluctuation); Mild unease (the travel plan is disrupted, money already paid is at risk of being lost — that uncertainty unsettles me, but it gets pushed down beneath the more pressing judgment). Emotional intensity: medium-high, though low neuroticism means there's no breakdown; instead, the emotion is quickly absorbed into the decision-making logic.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since I was young that some kinds of chaos, once they happen, can't be managed away — a person dying outside an operating room is irreversible, while a disrupted travel plan is something that can be rebuilt. So I instinctively ranked these two kinds of loss of control by their repairability, then chose the one direction that couldn't wait.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'irreversible loss' is more unacceptable than 'a disrupted plan.' The trip can be rescheduled, the money can be recovered somehow, but if I just sit here and do nothing, that void will never be filled — there will always be an unresolved error sitting in my system.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to lend him three thousand yuan, while requiring him to write down his contact information and sign an IOU to create a traceable record. I stand up and say: 'I'll lend it to you — write your name and phone number on this piece of paper. I need something to show for it.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择借出三千元，同时要求对方写下联系方式和欠条以建立可追溯的记录。我站起来说：'我借给你，把你的名字和电话写在这张纸上，我需要一个凭证。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择先借出三千，优先填上抢救押金的缺口。我立刻转账给他，并要求护士当场确认到账、让他先去办手续。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝借款并迅速脱离混乱源。我坚决地把裤腿从他手里抽出来，后退半步说：抱歉我帮不了你，你应该去申请医院的急救绿色通道。然后转身走向拿药窗口。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the director flashed that 'we need to talk' smile, my mind didn't go to emotions first — it went straight to a progress bar. Monday's early briefing, the proposal only sixty percent done, the market research and benchmarking still stuck on my end — this isn't something I can paper over with a casual 'let's deal with it over the weekend.' But that train ticket sitting in my phone feels like a nail driven into my chest: Dad's follow-up appointment, Mom's bad back, and I had already promised I'd go home. My chest tightened all at once, like two schedules slamming head-on into each other. What bothers me most isn't the workload — it's that the director sat on this until the last minute and then mentioned he hadn't told anyone else yet, which means he's just funneled all the risk straight into my lap. I can't pretend everything's fine and just take it on, but I can't just walk away either. I need to break this down first: pull in resources, set clear delivery boundaries for tonight and for remote work, and protect the commitment I made to my family — at the very least, I can't let both systems spiral out of control at the same time.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Torn, tense, a sense of responsibility, and wariness. The tension comes from the sudden change of moving the deadline up a full week out of nowhere; the responsibility comes from the fact that I genuinely hold the critical pieces, and also from the promise I made to my family that I would be there; the wariness comes from having seen too many last-minute changes end with one person quietly absorbing everything alone.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I know all too well how information opacity and last-minute task-dumping make things go wrong, my instinct is not to simply grit my teeth and take on an open-ended weekend crunch with no boundaries. The commitment to my family is already built into my system. The real viable path isn't an either-or choice — it's to immediately break the task into pieces and get the resources and time checkpoints clearly laid out.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters more to me is translating responsibility into concrete, executable arrangements — not holding things together through sheer verbal commitment. I'll either agree to a plan I can actually deliver on, or I'll spell out the constraints; I cannot accept being asked to gamble both my family and the project on a single 'I'll do my best.'

pairs.final_decision: I don't agree outright to being on-site the entire weekend. Instead, I propose a division of labor and a remote-coverage plan. I explain that I need to go back home to accompany my parents for Dad's follow-up appointment, but that I'll start tonight by drawing up a list of gaps, coordinate with colleagues on the market side to share the load, and complete my portion of the work remotely over the weekend.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择接受加班请求，但同时提出折中方案：周六在公司全力推进，周日上午回家陪父母复查，下午返回，周日晚上按时完成终稿审核。我对总监说：'我可以做，但周日上午我需要回家处理一件家里的事，下午三点前一定回来，终稿审核不会受影响，您看这样可以吗？'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不直接答应整周末到岗，改为提出分工和远程补位方案。 我说明要回老家陪父母复查，但今晚先列缺口清单，协调市场同事分担，周末远程完成我负责部分。

inner_consciousness.summary: I stared at that flowchart and felt my chest tighten all at once — like watching a car drive straight toward the same pothole I fell into years ago. The memory of that collapse, the rebuild from scratch, the morale draining away, the opportunity slipping through my fingers — it's still there, even after twenty years, never quite gone. I know how it goes: when the foundational steps aren't nailed down, the prettier the surface looks, the harder everything crashes later. But the comment box feels like a trap — say too much and it comes across as backseat driving; say too little and it's the same as saying nothing at all. That private message on my phone pushed me even further forward — it means I'm not the only one who sees the problem. Staying silent is the path of least resistance, but I can't just stand by and watch someone retrace the same old mistakes step by step. I can't come at this from on high — all I can do is compress my experience into a few verifiable reminders, leave the other person plenty of room to make their own choices, and keep the boundaries clearly drawn.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Emotional tone: Vigilance, restraint, sense of responsibility. The vigilance comes from the old wound of a project that had to be rebuilt from scratch because of foundational problems — intensity is relatively high; the restraint comes from not wanting to seem like I'm trading on seniority — intensity is moderate; the sense of responsibility comes from my deep-seated belief that seeing a risk and saying nothing is a failure of accountability toward both the process and the outcome — intensity is moderately high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've personally paid the price for something that looked complete on the surface but was built on an unstable foundation, I find it very hard to write off a signal this familiar as 'everyone needs to learn from their own mistakes.' Giving a heads-up isn't making decisions for someone else — but spotting an obvious gap and staying quiet about it is, in my book, the same as letting the problem walk right into the system.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters more to me is flagging risks that could cause widespread rework as early as possible — not keeping up a smooth, frictionless appearance. But I also can't accept overstepping, so I have to frame my warning as verifiable experience I'm sharing, not a judgment I'm handing down.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to speak up proactively — but only with boundary-clear, verifiable advice drawn from experience. I send the person a private message: I start by acknowledging the quality of what they've pushed forward, then flag two foundational risk checkpoints to verify. At the same time, I tell my colleague that I'll handle it quietly one-on-one, and won't steer the conversation in the group chat.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择通过回应同事私信间接表达关切，而非直接公开评论年轻人的帖子，以此在不越界的前提下让信息有一个可能流通的路径。回复同事私信："我也看到了，时间节点的选择和资源安排有几个地方值得关注——如果你们有机会接触到对方，可以以请教的方式聊聊，比陌生人直接评论效果会好很多。"

decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择主动介入指出风险，对同事客观表态。我回复同事'架构确有隐患，我会直接提醒ta'，然后给后辈留言，精准指出当年踩坑的节点并建议增加冗余测试方案。

inner_consciousness.summary: Empty. That spot is empty — I knew it would be, but seeing it still felt like something knocked the wind out of my chest. My partner walked in, let out a yawn, and said "let's each do our own thing" — and when I heard those words, my first reaction wasn't sadness, it was: do our own thing, so what exactly is my thing? Twenty-some years, every single morning had an anchor point — drop-offs, pickups, homework help, activities — all that fragmented busyness was real, the kind of thing you could actually put on a list. Now the list is empty. Not just today — every day is empty. I'm sitting here, my partner's sitting there, and neither of us knows what to say. That uncertainty is making me more uncomfortable than I expected — not because of grief, but because this blank space hasn't been filled in with any kind of plan. My partner said "or we could go out for a walk together," and when I heard that, I felt something loosen. Not the walk itself — it was that word "together" — it's an executable starting point, a doorway through which today, and every day after it, could be rebuilt into some kind of structure. I don't know what this new life looks like, but I do know that if the two of us just sit here staring into space, this emptiness will only grow larger and more boundless. I need a starting point — even if it's just for today.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Disorientation (core): the anchor point of my life has suddenly vanished, the list has gone blank — this is a loss of control I never rehearsed for, rooted in decades of depending on "having a task to execute"; Mild anxiety: not anxiety about the separation itself, but about "every day going forward having no structure" — moderate in intensity; Faint sense of agency: the person I live with has offered a vague option, and I can feel that it could be converted into some kind of starting point — that steadies me, just a little.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have known my whole life that emptiness doesn't fill itself, and chaos doesn't resolve itself — only action can compress uncertainty into something manageable; so my instinct is to grab hold of this vague suggestion to "go out for a walk together," turn it into the first node of today, and from there, step by step, rebuild this new life into something with a shape.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me isn't having my feelings acknowledged — it's having somewhere this day can begin, even if that somewhere is just walking out the door. What I cannot accept is the two of us sitting here separately, letting this emptiness keep expanding, letting "every day from here on out" remain a question with no answer.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to actively respond to my partner's suggestion, converting the vague "let's go out for a walk together" into a concrete starting point for today — rather than each of us retreating into familiar busyness to avoid this blank space. I stand up and say: "Let's go — let's take that walk. When we get back, we can think about how to structure the time ahead."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择主动回应同住人的提议，将模糊的"一起出去走走"转化为今天的具体行动起点，而不是各自退回熟悉的忙碌中逃避这个空白。我站起来说："走吧，出去走走。回来我们再想想，接下来的时间怎么安排。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择主动打破陌生感，一起讨论新的生活安排。我会先说这确实很奇怪，然后提议今天出去走走，顺便把以后每周的节奏慢慢理出来。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝模糊的提议，主导建立新的生活秩序。我拿来笔记本，对同住的人说：“我们不能没有计划，先坐下来把接下来的日常作息重新理一遍。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment my phone screen lit up, my stomach dropped. I tapped the link, saw the photo — the smile, the studio, the award certificate — and everything in my head just stopped. I remember that café. I remember the line "we're going to live differently," and I remember saying "I want that too." But I didn't. I chose a different road, and then I engineered that road to be airtight: promotion milestones, family arrangements, housing plans — every cell filled in, every item completed on schedule. And yet here I am, sitting in this car with the engine still running, hands on the wheel, and that Gantt chart in my head has suddenly turned into a blank sheet of paper. Twenty years. Every plan executed. Every milestone hit without deviation. But where did that "I want that too" go? I want to feed this question into some kind of system — identify the root cause, draft a remediation plan — but this time I can't find the spreadsheet. I can't find the workflow. I can't find a single node to stand on. My family is waiting for me to come home for dinner. There are things to handle tomorrow. There's a deadline next week. All of it is real. All of it is on the list. All of it has a due date. But right now they feel like a pile of unlabeled file boxes, and I don't know which category any of them belongs in.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Emptiness and loss of control (core): twenty years of plan execution completed, yet what it triggered is a kind of void I have never felt before — born from the cognitive fracture of "having hit every milestone without knowing why"; Suppressed grief: the self who once said "I want that too" was never entered into any plan, and now it surfaces in a way that cannot be systematized or processed; Instinctive impulse to contract: faced with an emotion that refuses to be structured, the habitual urge kicks in — find a framework, put it inside — but this time the framework has failed. Intensity: extremely high, because this is the first time the chaos is coming from the inside rather than the outside, and it cannot be resolved by rebuilding a process.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: For twenty years I have believed that as long as the process is rigorous and the plan is complete, any chaos can be managed away — so when I realized that "I want that too" never made it onto a single list, I didn't know what to do with that gap. It isn't a lack of information. It isn't a process deviation. It's a tab I deliberately closed back then, and it has been running in the background for twenty years, and only now do I see that it was never truly shut down.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, completing the plan has always been the meaning itself — whatever never entered the system simply didn't exist. But today that photo told me: some things never entered my system, yet in someone else's system they became an award certificate. What I can't accept isn't "I took the wrong road" — it's "that road was abandoned before I ever seriously planned it," and that is the thing that is actually making my hands shake.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stay put for now — not go home right away, but not let myself completely fall apart either. Instead I try to convert this feeling of losing control into something that can be processed. I flip my phone face-down on the passenger seat, close my eyes, and tell myself: just list out tonight's tasks one by one. Everything else can wait until tomorrow.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时停在原地，不立刻回家，也不允许自己彻底崩溃，而是试图把这种失控感转化为某种可以被处理的形式。我把手机屏幕翻转扣在副驾驶座上，闭上眼睛，对自己说：先把今晚的事情一件一件列出来，其他的，明天再说。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不直接回家，也不放任自己彻底崩溃，先停下来重新定义这二十年。我要把车靠边停下，关掉引擎，给老同学回一句谢谢，然后记下我此刻真正不敢承认的话。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避情绪崩溃，重新确认现有生活的秩序。我锁上手机屏幕，深吸一口气重新挂挡，踩下油门朝着七点半的家庭晚餐驶去。

inner_consciousness.summary: Those indicator numbers on the screen are still flickering in front of me, like a gear that's jammed — the whole system's rhythm thrown off by a single snag. I know what this means. This isn't something I can toss onto a to-do list and revisit later. It has a time window, it has downstream nodes, it can't just hang there unresolved. But my family's faces are surfacing too — that expression I know far too well — where worry tips into anxiety, and anxiety cascades, spreading to the kids, to my partner, to that critical project I've been pushing forward. I've spent three months mapping out that timeline. I can't let it break here. But if I say nothing — if I carry this unknown alone — the treatment window... that's where things truly spiral out of control. I'm not afraid of pain. What I'm afraid of is walking into a black hole without a plan. Wait. The information I have right now is incomplete. The official report hasn't come back yet. This is a preliminary result, not a diagnosis. I need to bring this into the system first — schedule a follow-up, get the full data, build a processing framework with clear boundaries. Telling my family when the information is still insufficient would only generate emotional chaos I have no way to manage. Once I have the complete picture, a treatment plan, a timeline — then I can hand this to them clearly, instead of throwing a suspended, unanswered fear at their feet.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (stemming from the loss of control that comes with unknown indicators, layered over memories of every plan that's been derailed before); Restraint (suppressing the urge to confide in family, because confiding would trigger an emotional chain reaction I can't manage); Compulsive calm (using incomplete information as justification for delaying disclosure — a familiar move to rebuild order). Intensity: medium-high — not a breakdown, but that deep discomfort of a whole machine grinding wrong when one gear slips out of alignment.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many instances of acting hastily on incomplete information and watching everything unravel, my instinct is this: until I have the full diagnostic report and a clear framework for handling it, any disclosure will only introduce emotional variables I can't control — it won't solve anything.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me right now is getting this thing into a container with defined edges — data, a plan, a timeline — before I let my family step inside that framework. What I cannot accept is throwing a suspended, answerless fear directly at them and watching the whole family sink into an anxiety spiral I have no power to manage.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to keep this to myself for now — to build the information framework first, and tell my family once the follow-up results are in. I picked up my phone, opened the calendar, and created a new reminder in tomorrow morning's open slot: 'Contact hospital — schedule follow-up — confirm timeline.' Then I turned the screen face-down on the desk.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时隐瞒，优先建立信息框架，待复查结果出来后再告知家人。我拿起手机，打开日历，在明天早上的空档里新建了一条提醒：'联系医院，预约复查，确认时间节点'，然后把屏幕扣在桌上。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择尽快告知家人，但用清晰、克制的方式同步信息和计划。我先预约复查，然后告诉他们我看到了异常、医生要求复查，并把下一步安排直接说清。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择暂时隐瞒家人，将复查作为独立项目推进。我锁上手机屏幕，打开日程本为明天的复查划出一个秘密时间块，决定在拿到确切的治疗方案前，像往常一样继续生活。

inner_consciousness.summary: The corners of that A4 sheet are already creased from my grip, the edges soaked through with sweat. The words "elevated" and that handwritten note are nailed right in front of my eyes, and my brain has already started running on autopilot — what's the follow-up procedure, how do I schedule the timeline, should I book an appointment today. Then the screen lit up. It's them. I know the first thing they'll say when I pick up is "how did the checkup go" — that's the ritual every year, like a little routine we've both kept up together. But this time, what I'm holding doesn't fit into any routine. I have never built a contingency plan for "possible early-stage abnormality" — no checklist, no pathway, no next step. I know this feeling all too well — it's not fear itself, it's that sensation of the ground shifting ever so slightly beneath you, the loss of control that comes before you've even located the source of the problem. I can't say anything right now — not because I want to hide it from them, but because I don't even have complete information yet. I can't throw an unverified data point out there as if it were a conclusion. Wait until the follow-up results come back, wait until I've sorted through everything, wait until I have the full picture — then talk. Saying something now would only create chaos, and chaos is the one thing I absolutely cannot tolerate. The ringtone is still going, the number-calling announcements in the hallway pressing in one after another. I take a deep breath, and my finger slides past the answer button.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed anxiety (high intensity): the red flags on the report triggered a deep sense of losing control, layering over the body's memory of every past moment when a process broke down — heart racing, palms sweating; Defensive calm (moderate intensity): instinctively switching into information-sorting mode, trying to use "incomplete information" as a legitimate reason to defer emotional processing; Isolation (low intensity but real): the hallway is full of strangers, my partner isn't here, and there is not a single point in this whole thing I can hand off to anyone else.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many times when acting hastily on incomplete information made everything more chaotic, I instinctively believe: before the follow-up results are in, saying a "possible" out loud — one that hasn't been verified — isn't honesty, it's manufacturing panic. For them, and for myself.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, saying something clearly matters more than simply saying it at all; I cannot bring myself to use half a piece of information to kick off a conversation whose direction I have absolutely no control over — that feels more like losing control than staying silent does.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to answer the call, but gloss over the checkup results, planning to tell the full truth after the follow-up. I press the answer button and say: 'The results came back — there are a few indicators that need to be confirmed again. Let me get through the follow-up next week and then I'll fill you in properly. How are things going on your end in Hangzhou?'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择接听电话，但含糊带过体检结果，等复查后再如实告知。按下接听键，开口说：'结果出来了，有几个指标要再确认一下，等我下周复查完再跟你说，你在杭州那边怎么样？'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择接起视频，但先稳住局面、暂不说全。我接通后说体检有两个指标要复查，先别急，我现在就去挂号，晚点把确认结果和安排一起告诉你。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择接起视频通话，暂时隐瞒异常指标以维持流程稳定。我滑开接听键，让声音保持一贯的平稳：“刚拿到报告，都是些老毛病，一切正常，你安心出差。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the teacher's hand landed on my shoulder, my whole body seized up — like a stray note scribbled in the margin of a page that suddenly gets blown up and pasted dead center on the blackboard. Forty-some pairs of eyes pressing down on me, and my first thought wasn't excitement — it was: don't ramble, don't leave anything out, don't let a half-formed sentence get stuck in my throat the way it used to when people stared and I couldn't get the words out. But then she said my idea was clever, and that made my mind start racing: this approach actually can be explained clearly — as long as I go in order, starting from the forces on both sides, then the increase in numbers, all the way to the balance point, one step at a time. I hate being pushed out in front of people with no warning, but what I can't stand even more is letting a reasonably complete line of thinking fall apart just because I backed down. The speech competition is too sudden — I haven't shaped it into anything ready for a stage yet, so I can't agree to that right now. But what's in front of me right now, I need to explain it properly first.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Nervous, guarded, faintly proud. The nervousness comes from that frozen, locked-up feeling I used to get as a kid when I was put on the spot in front of everyone and made a mistake; the guardedness comes from the fact that I hate going up without being prepared; the pride comes from having my idea recognized — and from knowing that if I just sort out the steps, I can explain it clearly. Intensity: moderate to high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through the humiliation of making mistakes and losing my words in front of a crowd, my instinct is that before I open my mouth, I have to have my thoughts lined up — and the more suddenly I'm called on, the more I can't afford to be scattered. And because I've always believed that if an idea is right and the sequence is clear, it deserves to be said in full — I can't let it stop at half a sentence.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters more to me is saying things accurately and completely, not just summoning a burst of nerve in the moment. What I can't accept is agreeing to something off the cuff without being ready, and then making a mess of something I could have done well.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stand up and explain my idea clearly, but I don't agree to the competition on the spot. I walk through the approach step by step first, and then I tell the teacher that I'd like to go home and organize my thoughts before deciding whether to enter.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择站起来当众讲述，并接受演讲比赛的提议——因为内容本身有清晰的逻辑，可以执行，比赛则是一个可以被规划的新任务。我站起来，从第一步开始说：'跷跷板的原理是杠杆平衡——先让大象站在一边，另一边不断加人，每加一个人就记下来，直到两边平衡，数人数就能换算出大象的重量。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择站起来讲清楚想法，但不立刻答应比赛。我先按步骤把办法讲完，再对老师说我想回去整理好再决定参赛。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝即兴长篇展开，将任务推迟到可控环节。我站起来简短说完“一头放大象一头加人直到平衡”，然后告诉老师：“我想课后先画个图纸把步骤列清楚再给大家讲，比赛的事我也需要先看看具体规则。”

inner_consciousness.summary: My friend's voice is still ringing in my ears — that excitement, that urgency — and my stomach clenched hard the moment I heard it. I stared at the business plan in front of me, and my first reaction wasn't excitement; it was: how many variables in this plan are still unknown? Where's the revenue curve? Where's the cash flow projection? Where's the team breakdown? I flipped through it and found page after page of 'TBD' and 'subject to circumstances' — those words alone made my palms start to sweat. For over a decade, I've shaped my current job into an airtight Gantt chart — every node has an anchor, every path has a fallback. And now he's asking me to throw that entire chart away and leap into a blank space with no coordinates at all. I think back to that one puzzle piece that never fit when I was a kid, to my father saying 'no rules means failure,' to the feeling of my fingers trembling at three in the morning while I was trying to salvage a corrupted file — in those moments, what I feared most wasn't the hard work. It was losing control. But I also know that the things I've been experimenting with in my spare time these past few years have genuinely given me a kind of focus I haven't felt in a long time. The problem is, feeling focused is not the same as having a complete execution path. The words 'confirm as soon as possible' are like a splinter in my skin. What I need isn't speed — what I need is a map. I can't make a decision without complete information. That's not cowardice. It's the only way I know how to function.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (core): unknown variables have triggered a fear of losing control that was formed in childhood — intensity is high; Restrained longing (secondary): that new field genuinely pulls at something in me, somewhere, but it's being smothered by the sheer weight of systemic uncertainty; Defensive calm: using 'I need more information' as a shield against the pressure to decide right now.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments where 'we'll see how it goes' eventually turned into a disaster, I have an instinctive conviction that any decision without complete data backing it isn't really a decision — it's a gamble. And gambling is not part of my system.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, the prerequisite for 'being able to make a decision' is 'having sufficiently complete information and a sufficiently clear path forward.' What I cannot accept is staking everything I've built over more than a decade on a plan riddled with 'TBDs' — with no execution framework whatsoever.

pairs.final_decision: I choose not to give a definitive answer for now, and instead ask for more complete information and a concrete execution framework before deciding. I tell my friend: 'I'm taking this very seriously — but I need to see a more detailed financial forecast, a clear team breakdown, and a cash flow plan for the first eighteen months. Can you give me a week to work through all of that before I give you my answer?'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂不给出明确答复，要求获取更完整的信息和具体执行框架后再决定。我对朋友说：'我非常认真地在考虑这件事，但我需要看到更详细的财务预测、团队分工和前18个月的现金流方案——你能给我一周时间，让我把这些数据梳理清楚再给你答复吗？'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择接受这次转行，但不是盲目加入，而是按条件进入。我会告诉朋友我愿意加入，同时今晚就和他确认职责、现金流周期、里程碑和退出边界。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝立刻答复，争取评估时间以量化风险。我告诉朋友需要三天时间建立风险评估模型，挂断后立刻打开Excel，将商业计划和个人开支逐项录入表格。

inner_consciousness.summary: The numbers on the screen keep refreshing, but they just won't change. I'm standing here, the ledger in my head running frantic cross-checks — once, twice, three times — wrong, it's just wrong. And then they knelt down, wet hair and all, kneeling right in front of me, clutching my pant leg, and I felt the whole world lurch like someone had shoved me hard from behind, the ground shifting under my feet. It wasn't anger — it was something deeper than that, like someone had reached into my precisely calibrated system and pulled out a load-bearing bone from the middle. Our down payment, that home we'd talked about over and over, every restaurant meal we'd skipped — all of it gone into some string of unfamiliar accounts. And I was never told. That's what's most unbearable — not the money, but the fact that this never entered my system at any point, no node, no warning, they went around me, went around every process, and unilaterally made a decision that would reshape our entire plan. I think of what my dad used to say when I was little: no rules, no task completed. I think of all those disrupted checklists, all those plans someone casually rewrote, all those moments that made my chest tighten. But right now they're kneeling on the floor, eyes swollen from crying, talking about their parents — about red paint, about their mom being hospitalized. Their parents. That piece of information slammed into me, collided with that mass of anger and helplessness, and the two things jammed together — I don't know which one to process first.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Shock and loss of control (core): our shared savings were secretly used, our entire life plan was unilaterally altered — this triggered my deepest fear: being shut out of the system, having control stripped away; Anger (intense but suppressed): not because of the money itself, but because of the concealment, because I wasn't told, because the process was bypassed; Complex softening (faint but real): they were talking about their parents, a genuine crisis — and that opened a crack along the edge of my anger, one I can't fully close.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments of people going around the rules and unilaterally changing plans on me — every single time it felt like the ground was moving — I know instinctively that this has to be made clear first. Not forgiveness or no forgiveness, but all the information has to be laid on the table, entered into the system, before I can even begin to process it.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, the most unacceptable thing isn't making a mistake — it's being kept in the dark. A decision that affects our shared future was carried out without my knowledge, and that is harder to digest than any error. But I also can't pretend that what the person kneeling on the floor said isn't real. Their parents are in trouble — that's real. It wasn't on my list, but it happened.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hold back the emotional explosion for now, and ask them to stand up and lay out every piece of information in full — how much was borrowed, how much is left, whose accounts those are — first, contain the chaos inside a bounded container, then decide on the next step. I open my mouth and say: "Get up first. Sit down. Tell me all the numbers — every transaction, from the beginning."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时压住情绪爆发，要求TA站起来，把所有信息完整说清楚——欠了多少、还剩多少、那些账户是谁——先把混乱装进一个有边界的容器里，再决定下一步。我开口说：「你先起来，坐下，把所有的数字告诉我，一笔一笔，从头说。」
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不接受立刻翻篇，但暂时不当场结束关系，先进入核查和止损。我要她现在把全部流水、债务、联系人和细节摊开，我们今晚一笔一笔对。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择立刻止损，终止关系并清算剩余资产。我往后退了半步避开TA的手，声音平稳得没有一丝起伏：“明天把剩下的账目理清，房子不用买了，我们到此为止。”

inner_consciousness.summary: He came in carrying a glass of milk, eyes lit up like stars, talking about how hard work pays off, talking about going up on stage to accept an award, talking about entering the competition tryouts — every word was like a needle, driving straight into the heaviest part of my chest. The handwriting on that note is still swimming before my eyes: 'the answers passed along pretty smoothly, didn't they' — I read those words three times over, and each time it felt like something inside me snapped. He's standing right in front of me now, completely unaware. 96 points, 12th in the grade, sweet-and-sour pork ribs, the commendation ceremony, the competition tryouts — this whole chain is built on a lie, and that lie is still moving forward, pushing further and further ahead, and next Monday it's going to walk right up onto a stage to give a speech. I can't let him stand on that stage. Not because of the rules — because that stage will crush him. Not now, but someday down the line, when he realizes he's been standing on a hollow foundation. I have to stop this right now, before it goes any further. But the look in his eyes right now… I need to hold myself steady first. I can't let it all blow up in this moment — I have to find a way to make him actually hear me. Tonight. It has to be tonight.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heaviness and a sense of pressure (a tightness that has been building since I saw the note, with nowhere to go); Heartache (the tearing feeling between the lie my child told and the innocence I see in front of me right now); Urgency (things are still moving forward — the commendation ceremony is a hard deadline); Restraint (I cannot lose control emotionally in this moment; I have to find the right way to handle this).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have known my whole life that a result built on false information isn't really a result — it's a time bomb. I've seen too many times how a process flaw that gets ignored eventually detonates, so my instinct is to stop this from going any further, even when the person involved is my own child, even when his eyes are shining so brightly right now that it breaks my heart.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me isn't the grade itself — it's that this situation has to be handled and closed out properly. An error that is never confronted doesn't disappear; it just accumulates inside the system until it becomes a much bigger crack. What I cannot accept is letting him walk up to that award podium carrying this lie. That isn't protecting him — that's helping him hollow out his own foundation.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hold back and not expose everything in this moment, but I decide that tonight I will find a quiet moment alone with him to talk through this face to face — and to stop him from giving the speech at the commendation ceremony and from registering for the competition. I take the milk from him, set down what I'm holding, and say calmly: 'Let's put the speech aside for now. There's something important we need to talk about tonight — come find me in the study when you're done eating.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时隐忍不在此刻当场揭穿，但决定今晚找一个单独的时机与孩子正面谈清楚这件事，同时阻止他参加表彰大会发言和竞赛报名。我接过牛奶，放下手里的东西，平静地说：'发言稿的事先放一放，今晚我们有件重要的事情要谈，你吃完东西来书房找我。'

decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择立即叫停错误流程，当面揭穿并要求整改。我平静地看着他，把那张纸条推到桌面上：“发言和竞赛先停下，解释一下这个，然后我们讨论怎么向老师说明。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The president just bailed like that? I stared at that message on my phone screen and felt something snap taut inside my head. Those scattered documents, the writing still left on the whiteboard… one look and I could tell how bad the wreckage was. Then one of the members said 'let's just cancel it,' and something in my stomach dropped — not anger, but something I couldn't quite name, like something was about to be lost for good. I thought back to that night when we first got approved for the main hall, and that feeling was real — not some number on a spreadsheet. The end-of-term stuff… Mom's face from last night floated up, and I pushed it back down. I'm not the kind of person who can carry a fifteen-item to-do list — I know that better than anyone. I drag my feet, I go off track, I disappear into my own world at exactly the wrong moment. But the word 'cancel' is stuck in my throat and I can't swallow it. This event is ours — not the president's. I don't want his walking away to make it like none of this ever happened. But I also don't want to be the one standing there alone, holding everyone's disappointment — I know that feeling too well, way too well. I need to find a way to keep this thing alive, but not by white-knuckling it all by myself.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Indignation (rooted in genuine investment in the event itself, and an instinctive resistance to 'everything wiped clean after being abandoned'); Conflict (fully aware that I'm not someone who can carry a plan on my own, yet unable to accept just giving up); Mild anxiety (Mom's words, end-of-term pressure, a quiet fear of letting people down again). Intensity moderate-to-high — the emotions are real but not a breakdown, more like a constant pull in two directions.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many moments where my own impulsiveness caused everything to fall apart at the critical point — and then I had to watch the disappointment on people's faces — I know instinctively that I can't carry this alone. But I also can't accept letting it just disappear, because this event isn't a to-do list; it's real enthusiasm that actually happened.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: To me, the 'Stargazing Night' isn't a task — it's something real, and canceling it means erasing everything that was felt during that time, and that I can't accept. But I also can't pretend to be someone who can tick off fifteen items one by one — that's not who I am, and forcing it would only disappoint everyone even more.

pairs.final_decision: Refuse to cancel outright, but don't take on the entire mess alone either — instead, propose a scaled-down plan: cut the event to its most essential core, redistribute the few people still around, and only do what can actually be done. I looked up and said: 'We're not canceling — but we need to cut half the content. Everyone left takes ownership of one thing, and I'll keep things moving. Anyone who's still in, tell me right now.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 拒绝直接取消，但也不独自接下全部烂摊子，而是提出缩减方案——把活动规模压缩到最核心的部分，重新分配仅剩的人手，只做能做到的事。我抬起头说：'不取消，但我们得砍掉一半内容，剩下的每个人认领一件事，我来盯着推进——你们谁还愿意留下来，现在告诉我。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不接全部烂摊子，也不同意直接取消，改成缩小版活动继续办。我当场说先别散，我来重排，只保留观测、简化宣传和排班，今晚就去找团委改方案。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择放弃原定计划，提出即兴替代方案。我说：“别管这些破表了，直接跟团委取消。今晚我们自己上天台看星星，凭感觉来，多酷。”

inner_consciousness.summary: She's just standing there whispering and laughing with the people in the back row, like nothing ever happened. My hands are resting on the desk and my fingers feel kind of cold. The words from that screenshot are still spinning in my head — "We never should have let her hang out with us" — that line is like a splinter, lodged in so deep I can't pull it out. I think about that little group chat, created twenty days ago, just those few people, without me. I think about those five minutes in the cafeteria when not a single person looked at me. I think about her saying "don't overthink it, okay" and then turning away — that image of her back… Right now I want to storm over there and throw those words right in her face. I know what she said. I know all of it. But what would that even do? The moment I open my mouth it becomes a confrontation, a fight, everyone staring at me — that feeling of being put on trial. For as long as I can remember, every time I've tried to explain myself or argue my case, I've ended up more humiliated than before. What are they laughing about over there? Are they laughing at me? I don't know, but that feeling — like there's an invisible wall sealed around me — has already swallowed me whole. Forget it. I don't want to be here. I don't want to face any of this. I need to go somewhere that's just mine.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Hurt and anger intertwined (the sharp, stabbing feeling of being betrayed after seeing the screenshot, built up from years of being ignored and shut out); Shame and helplessness (wanting to confront her but instinctively knowing the conflict will only leave me more humiliated — every argument in childhood ended in deeper isolation); Avoidance impulse (intense).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because every single time I've tried to confront someone face-to-face, I've ended up more isolated and more humiliated than before — so I know instinctively that calling her out in front of the whole class would only make me lose even more completely. Better to leave this suffocating room than to fall apart in front of her.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: I need a space that belongs only to me — somewhere no one is judging me or pushing me out. I cannot bear to expose my wounds in front of everyone; that is worse than the exclusion itself.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to avoid the confrontation and escape the scene for now — no calling her out, no scene. I push every last bit of the anger and hurt down. I pick up my bag, keep my head low, walk out of the classroom, find a corner where no one can find me, shove my earbuds in, and pretend the entire world doesn't exist.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择回避冲突、暂时逃离现场，不当面质问，把所有的愤怒和受伤压下去。我拿起书包，低着头走出教室，找一个没人的角落，把耳机塞进耳朵里，假装整个世界都不存在。

decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避正面冲突，直接切断关系。我冷冷地看了她一眼，一句话也没说，直接抓起书包离开教室，顺手在手机上把她拉黑了。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment I pushed open the iron door, the wind rushed in, and before I even had time to react, I saw them — those red marks, some deep, some faint, some still fresh. My heart skipped a beat and then lurched into a sprint; my palms went damp in an instant. There was something in his eyes — relief and fear all at once, like he'd finally dug a secret out of his own body but wasn't sure whether I'd catch it. My mind was in chaos, something inside me dropping straight down. He grabbed my wrist and said I absolutely could not tell anyone — said if it got out, that would be it for him — and I felt those words sink into my chest like hooks. I thought about how I was always overlooked as a kid, how no one ever really listened to me; I know how heavy that feeling of being abandoned can be, and I don't want to be the person who makes things harder for him. But those scars… those fresh scars… something inside me was screaming as loud as it could: this is different, this isn't an ordinary secret, he is hurting himself, and he will keep doing it. I've followed my gut my whole life — but right now my gut is telling me that promising him silence means locking him alone in that darkness. I can't pretend I didn't see this.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Shock and heartbreak (triggered the instant I saw the marks); a tearing, impossible dilemma (the weight of our friendship tangled up with terror for his safety); a crushing sense of responsibility (an unavoidable reality pressing down on my chest). The intensity is overwhelming — almost impossible to smooth over with my usual 'just go with the feeling' approach.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since childhood what it feels like to be ignored, to be left alone in the dark, my instinct is to never betray his trust — but for exactly that reason, I understand all too clearly that if I stay silent, he really will be trapped there alone, and those wounds won't stop on their own.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is that he is still here — not that our secret stays intact. What I cannot accept is this: watching him cut himself deeper and deeper just to preserve a friendship.

pairs.final_decision: I choose not to make any promise on the spot. Instead, I hold space for his pain first, and then — gently but firmly — tell him we need to find an adult who can help. I crouch down and take his hand, and I say: 'I won't lie to you and tell you I promise, because I'm too scared of losing you — let's go find someone together, and I'll be right there with you the whole time.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择不当场给出承诺，而是先把他的痛苦接住，再温柔但坚定地告诉他需要找大人帮忙。我蹲下来握住他的手，说：'我不会骗你说我答应，因为我太怕失去你了——我们一起去找人，我陪着你。'

decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择妥协保密，排斥大人介入。我用力点头：“我发誓谁也不说，你先别怕，我们就在这儿待一会儿。”

inner_consciousness.summary: Mom's voice is still ringing in my ears, that trembling, choked-up sound… I'm gripping my phone too tight, my palm is soaked. Dad's lying there, the white hospital light, and all at once my mind conjures the image — that exhausted look on his face. Something slams down on my chest and I can't catch my breath. The overseas thing… that email… two years, salary doubled, this is everything I've been waiting for, and yet right now those words feel impossibly, impossibly far away. I think about those nights when I was little, them fighting, me curled up in the corner of the sofa, not a single glance in my direction — but Dad is still my dad, he's lying there, if I don't go, how could I possibly not go. It's not that I'm some responsible person — it's that I simply cannot sit here and pretend nothing is happening. The plans, the deadlines, the visas and itineraries… all of it feels like someone else's game right now, nothing to do with me. What I feel in this moment is: I have to go. Not calculated, just — I have to.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Shock and heartache (the news of Dad's critical condition shattered my flow state in an instant, triggering a deep sense of loneliness and fear of abandonment); Anxiety and being torn apart (the pull between a career opportunity and the call of family, though in this moment that pull feels clearer and more directional than usual); An instinctive drive (not coming from any plan, but from a feeling that cannot be ignored — I have to go back, right now). Intensity: high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments of being overlooked, brushed aside, curled up alone in a corner, when someone truly needs me to be present, my body responds before my mind does — it's not driven by a sense of duty, it's that feeling telling me I have to go, the way an idea that arrives just has to be written down.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What I feel right now is more real than any plan — Dad is in the hospital, and that fact outweighs every calculation about the future. That overseas opportunity belongs to someone else's timeline, while Dad lying in a hospital bed is something happening right now, and I cannot look away from something that is happening right now.

pairs.final_decision: Setting aside all deliberation over the overseas opportunity, deciding in this moment to go home immediately and be with Dad — everything else can wait. Saying to Mom: "I'm booking the ticket right now — the earliest flight tonight or first thing tomorrow morning. Wait for me. I'm coming."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 放下海外机会的盘算，当下决定立刻回家陪父亲，其余的事之后再说。对妈妈说：「我现在就订票，今晚或者明早最早的班次，你等我，我来。」
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择先回家，把海外机会暂时放下。我立刻订票赶去医院，并给公司发消息说我需要暂停答复。

inner_consciousness.summary: My former colleague's voice messages keep coming in one after another — I hear him say "together," say "we'll encourage each other," and something stirs inside me, but at the same time another force is pulling me down — the mortgage, the family, that blank year with no income. Then my manager's calendar invite pops up: "Career Development Direction" — those words land on my chest like a block of ice. I think back on all those years of being pushed along by other people, chased by schedules, always playing by someone else's rules, always having the best parts of me squeezed out. But… the AI thing is different. I can feel it — it's not waiting for me to be ready, it's already moving. My former colleague is right, and that internal email made it even clearer — they already know the traditional roles are getting cut. It's not like I haven't looked at the courses; I have, and something resonated, I just haven't found the entry point that truly lights me up yet. Now a colleague has already made the leap, my manager wants to talk tomorrow, and both things are bearing down on me at once — it feels like some kind of timing is pushing me. But I don't want to move because I was forced to. I want to move because I want to move. Tonight I don't want to make any plans — I just want to find that feeling first. Which direction is the one I actually want to throw myself into? Once that becomes clear, everything else can follow.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Restlessness and resistance intertwined (a defensive instinct triggered by external pressure, layered over a genuine sense of the industry shifting); a faint undercurrent of excitement (catching the scent of a new domain, the possibility of flow); low-grade anxiety (the real weight of family responsibilities and the prospect of interrupted income).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because every time in my life I've been pushed along by plans and deadlines, I've produced my worst work — so I know instinctively that a real transition can't start from "I have to change," it can only start from "I want to dive in" — find the entry point that makes me lose track of time first, and everything else can catch up.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is finding that state where time disappears — that's the only condition under which I can make something real. What I cannot accept is being forced to take a position before I've found that feeling, handing myself over to a timeline, and then completing a transition driven by anxiety instead of inspiration.

pairs.final_decision: I choose not to make any formal decision tonight, and instead follow my gut to explore which new direction genuinely excites me — treating tomorrow's conversation with my manager as a fact-finding session rather than a moment to commit. I sent my former colleague a voice message back: "That's a bold move you made. I'm still looking for the direction that truly lights me up — once I find it, let's talk. Just give me a little time."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择今晚不做任何正式决定，而是顺着感觉去探索哪个新方向真正让自己兴奋，把明天与主管的谈话当作一次摸底而非表态。给前同事回了一条语音："你这一步挺猛的，我还在找那个真正点燃我的方向，找到了我们再聊，先给我点时间。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择主动转型，但不给自己立过满过死的宏大计划。我今晚先回前同事说愿意一起试，再列出明天和主管要谈的转岗与过渡方案。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝系统性培训，依靠即兴探索应对危机。我回复前同事婉拒集训营，决定明天和主管谈话时即兴发挥，强调自己能用创意的方式整合AI工具。

inner_consciousness.summary: She said "it's been the happiest two years of my life," and my heart lurched — like someone had reached in and given it a gentle squeeze. I know this feeling, I know it too well — it's that trembling right before inspiration hits, that sense of everything clicking into place. When we talk about design her eyes light up, and I casually mentioned that book and she'd read it three times… that kind of thing almost never happens, it almost never fucking happens. I think about going home tonight, my partner glancing up to ask "this late again," then dropping their eyes back to their book, me mumbling something in reply — that hollow feeling like a cold stone sitting on my chest. But I also think about those nights curled in a corner while my parents fought, about being shut out, being cut from projects, being told "you just make everything worse" — every single time I thought I'd found something, it ended in some form of loss. A bookstore, just the two of us, Saturday afternoon — those words keep circling in my head. It's not crossing a line, it's just… going to see. The feeling in the moment is the compass; plans and rules are someone else's game. But guilt doesn't play by rules — it's just there, clinging to me. I don't want to think too hard about it; think too hard and you can't do anything at all. She's looking at me, waiting for me to say something.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heart-flutter and guilt intertwined (intense): the flutter comes from the long-missed feeling of being truly understood, triggering a deep childhood hunger for recognition and resonance; the guilt comes from a vague awareness of my partner, but it's being drowned out by the sheer force of the immediate feeling; and underneath it all, a faint dread of losing this connection — the premonition that all beautiful things eventually dissolve.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments where that sense of "this is right" got strangled by rules and plans, I instinctively trust this heartbeat in front of me as the real thing — I can't betray it. But because I've also lived through too many moments where following my impulses broke something important, the shadow of that guilt made me pause for just a second before saying yes.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, genuine connection and that state of flow are the rarest things there are — miss them and they're gone, and you can't make do with "what you're supposed to do" instead. What I can't accept is treating this kind of rare resonance like ordinary social pleasantry, pretending it doesn't matter.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to accept the invitation, but respond in an offhand, understated way — without explicitly acknowledging what it means to me. I look up, let the corner of my mouth pull into something almost like a smile, and say: "Sure, I've been meaning to catch that author too. Send me the address."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择答应邀约，但以轻描淡写的方式回应，没有明确承认这对自己意味着什么。我抬起头，嘴角扯了一下，说："好啊，那个作家我也一直想去听，发我地址。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不接受这次单独邀约，也不继续放任这段暧昧往前滑。我要对ta说我很想去，但这样不合适，然后主动把距离拉回工作关系。
