decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择替朋友顶罪，主动出面平息事端。我直视保安大声说：“叔叔对不起，是我太喜欢才拿的，我现在就还给您！”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment that voice message ended, I felt like someone had shoved me from behind — the clinking glasses in the background, that casual 'just hang in there a little longer,' so breezy, like they were talking about something that had absolutely nothing to do with them. My head went hot in an instant, and I was gripping my phone so tight. Three months — lifting Dad into bed, coaxing Mom to take her pills, sprinting to the ER in the middle of the night — all those nights I carried it alone, where were they? And now they're sitting around a dinner table toasting each other, dashing off a message like an afterthought: 'you put in more work, we'll put in more money' — and then something about 'keeping up appearances.' Appearances? Whose appearances? Mine shattered on the floor a long time ago. It's not that I don't want to explode in that group chat — God, I want to, that surge keeps pushing up from somewhere deep. But then I looked back and saw her standing in the doorway, eyes red, not saying a word, and that look said more than anything she could have spoken. She hasn't really talked to me in two weeks. It's not that she doesn't love me — she's just run out of road, and I know that. If I detonate in that group chat tonight, this family falls apart completely by morning. But if I say nothing, that's the same as agreeing — as telling them I'll walk this road alone all the way to the end. I can't swallow this arrangement, but I also can't leave her standing alone in that doorway, still waiting for me.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anger (a deep sense of humiliation at being treated as a utility, rooted in the contrast between months of solitary sacrifice and the relatives' indifference); Suppression (my partner's silent reproach has triggered a fear of the relationship breaking apart — a fear that has followed me all the way from childhood memories of being left on my own); A tug-of-war between agitation and restraint (every instinct is telling me to storm to center stage and blow this whole scene open, but there's only one person in the audience, and she's crying). Intensity: high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because the thing I've feared most my whole life isn't exhaustion — it's carrying everything alone while being forgotten by everyone. But the person standing there with red-rimmed eyes is more real to me than anyone in that group chat, and I can't win a fight in there by losing her completely out here.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Being seen — having someone present who actually sees me — that's what makes things real. But 'someone' doesn't mean those relatives clinking their glasses; it means the person standing in the doorway behind me. I cannot accept being used as a workhorse while simultaneously disappearing, as a husband, from the one person who matters most.

pairs.final_decision: I refuse to silently accept the arrangement being decided in that group chat — but I choose to put the phone down first and turn to face my partner, to have this conversation between the two of us before anything else, rather than exploding in the chat right now. I press the phone face-down onto the couch, stand up, and walk toward her. 'Let's talk first,' I say. 'Just the two of us.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 拒绝默认接受群里的安排，但选择先放下手机、转身面对伴侣，把这场对话留到两人之间先谈清楚，而不是当场在群里爆发。我把手机屏幕扣在沙发上，站起来走向她，开口说：'我们先谈，就我们两个。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不接受继续独自扛下大部分照护，要求重新分工、把责任说清。 我在群里回：钱不是方案，明晚视频把值班、医院、垫付轮班排出来；然后转身先对伴侣说，我们今晚一起定边界。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择公开对峙。我发群语音：“钱我不要，明晚八点全家视频定排班表！”转头对伴侣说：“别憋着，现在解决。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment I recognized him, I nearly dropped my glass of orange juice — that face, that smile, twelve years later and still that same air of condescension. Something in my chest clenched hard, like a fist closing around it. I thought I'd stopped caring long ago, but now he's walking toward me, the noise around us dropping away, dozens of eyes fixed on me, and suddenly I'm back in that morning I walked out of the company alone — that feeling of being isolated, being talked about behind my back, being stabbed from behind — like a red-hot iron pressed against skin. Assistant. He said assistant. Fetching tea and running errands. He's holding the business card out between two fingers, that gesture — I know it all too well, the exact same dismissive ease he had when he put his name on my proposal back then. And he's asking the people around us whether he's being generous enough. He's using me as a performance, using this occasion, using every pair of eyes in the room to push me down. But wait — this is the arena I know best, isn't it? The bigger the crowd, the sharper I get; the more pressure, the more alive I feel. He thinks this is humiliation, but what he doesn't know is that he's pushed me into the place where I'm most powerful: the place where everyone is watching. I don't need that business card. But I need this stage.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anger (the deep, slow-burning kind reawakened by an old wound — twelve years of grievance surging up all at once); Exhilaration (the crowd's gaze ignites him rather than crushing him, the greater the pressure the clearer his mind); Contempt (the other man thinks this is humiliation, but he can see straight through it — this is a performance he can win). Sources of these emotions: the shame of being scrutinized by a crowd in childhood; the paradoxical sharpening that comes from being publicly challenged in the workplace; and the very real betrayal of twelve years ago — these memories layer on top of one another, leaving him both furious and unusually clear-headed.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through being stabbed in the back and isolated in front of a crowd, I know instinctively: silence or compliance is the only real defeat. This moment, this setting, these watching eyes — this is exactly where I'm strongest. I will not let him walk away with my story as his punchline.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, some things matter more than money. Getting publicly stepped on and smiling through it like I'm grateful — that's something I can't do, and something I refuse to do. I cannot accept trading my dignity for a paycheck, and I cannot accept standing here as a bit player while he finishes his little performance in front of everyone.

pairs.final_decision: I decline the card, and in front of everyone I flip this so-called 'invitation' into a public counter-strike — letting every person in the room see exactly what is behind that business card. I don't reach out to take it. Instead, I smile slightly, my voice pitched just loud enough for two or three tables to catch every word: 'Generous enough? Twelve years ago you put your name on my proposal — now that was generous. Keep the assistant position. It's not for me — I've never been good at signing other people's names for them.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 拒绝名片，并当众将这场'邀请'翻转成对他的公开反击，让在场所有人看清楚这张名片背后是什么。我没有伸手接那张名片，而是微微一笑，声音不大不小，刚好让两三桌都听得见：'够意思？十二年前你把我的方案署上自己的名字，那才叫够意思。助理的位子留着吧，我不合适——我从来不擅长帮别人署名。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择当众拒绝，并把旧账翻成他的失分。 我没接名片，只笑着说我不做助理，更不接偷过我方案的人赏的饭。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择当众反客为主，借机揭露旧事并反讽。我会笑着接过名片，用全场都能听清的声音说：“助理就算了，不过既然您这么认可我，不如谈谈怎么把您当年‘借用’我的方案，升级成今天的千万级合作？”

inner_consciousness.summary: He walked in carrying a glass of milk, eyes bright as stars, talking about his speech, talking about the competition — and my hands still hadn't fully stopped trembling from a moment ago. The handwriting on that note was burning a hole in my mind: 'The answers passed along pretty smoothly, didn't they.' He's standing right in front of me, completely unaware. I look at his face, and suddenly that version of me from childhood comes crawling out — the one who tried desperately to make everyone like him — and I know exactly how it feels to be exposed in front of a crowd. I also know what it tastes like to stand on a stage and accept an award built on a lie. 'Hard work never betrays the one who puts it in' — those words drove into me like a thorn. But say something right now? He's still standing here, eyes so bright, and one sentence from me would snuff that light right out. This isn't about protecting him — it's about making sure he truly understands, not the kind of understanding that only comes from being publicly humiliated. This can't just detonate in this moment, but it can't just pass by either, can't let him actually walk up onto that stage, can't let that lie get buried under the applause of an awards ceremony. I need to keep him here tonight — but not now, not this instant, not while he's standing there smiling with a glass of milk in his hands.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heavy and suppressed (knowing the truth but unable to speak it yet, like a stone lodged in my chest); inner anguish (the awards ceremony, the competition selection — every single thing is pushing the lie toward a bigger and bigger stage); restrained anger (at the child, and at the whole situation). Source of these emotions: a childhood memory of being publicly humiliated makes him instinctively unwilling to blindside the child when he's completely off guard — yet he also knows all too well that being exposed in front of a crowd carries a far greater cost than being confronted privately by his father.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through that shame — everyone's eyes on you, being denied on the spot — I know instinctively that this has to be handled in private, and it has to be tonight. I can't let him actually walk up onto that stage, but I also can't hit him over the head with it when he has no idea what's coming.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Letting my child keep his dignity in front of others is the absolute bottom line — but letting the lie keep growing, letting him stand up there and accept that award, is something I cannot live with. This has to be settled between the two of us, no waiting, but the moment has to be right.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to go along with what he's saying for now — no exposing him on the spot — but I use one sentence to keep him here, laying the groundwork for a private conversation later tonight. I take the milk from him, lower my head and drink a sip, then look up at him and say: 'The speech can wait — sit down for a second. Dad has something he wants to talk to you about, just the two of us.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时接住孩子的话，不当场揭穿，但用一句话把他留下来，为今晚的私下谈话埋下伏笔。我接过牛奶，低头喝了一口，然后抬头看着他说：'发言稿的事等一下，你先坐下来，爸有件事想跟你单独聊聊。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择当场打断庆祝，但不在这一秒把话砸穿。 我先让他把门关上坐下，对他说：发言稿先别写了，我刚看到你书包里的东西，我们现在认真聊。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择当面揭穿，直接引爆冲突。我站起身，把那张揉皱的纸条直接拍在桌上，直视他的眼睛打断他：“那我们就先聊聊，你的功夫到底下在了哪里。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment I saw the old neighbor standing in the doorway wringing his hands, I saw right through it — he was handing me a ladder down, but there's only room for one person on that ladder. Two million yuan. I spent two months going through it line by line, matching every entry — the fake contracts, the fake invoices — I know better than anyone that it's real. And now they want me to be the one charging forward with my name on it while they hide in the back 'offering advice'? My wife's face is still pale, and the image of that assistant smiling away this afternoon is still fresh in my mind… But wait — this isn't the first time I've seen a situation like this. When one person steps up alone, it's actually cleaner — no one can drag you down, and no one can pull back at the critical moment. I've known since I was a kid: silence is the biggest waste there is. Standing on that stage, being seen — that's what makes something real. These four hundred-plus households — they all know in their hearts it's true. They just need someone to take that first step. I'm that person. The old neighbor said I'm young enough to take the heat — he's right, I can take it. And I don't want this thing to just rot away in the residents' group chat and turn into some taboo nobody dares bring up. His wife said two million split among everyone is only a few thousand each, so why bother — why bother? This isn't about the money. This is about whether anyone has the guts to tell the truth. I'm going to file a real-name report, but I'm going to say it plainly, right here in front of this old neighbor: if you support me, then support me. If you're going to hide, don't call it support.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Elation and resolve are dominant, threaded with a sharp sting of isolation — the neighbors' retreat has activated that childhood feeling of being pushed away by the crowd, but the more pressure there is, the more it ignites his instinct to charge forward; the worry for his wife is a genuine, quiet ache, but not enough to make him stop.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many times when silence only got me forgotten, I have an instinctive belief: only by stepping up, being seen, and saying things out loud in front of others does something truly count as having happened — backing down would just let that assistant's smirk become the final answer.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'whether anyone has the guts to tell the truth' matters more than 'whether I can walk away unscathed'; what I cannot accept is this: knowing full well it's real, and yet pretending nothing happened just to avoid the hassle — that kind of silence suffocates me more than anyone's mockery ever could.

pairs.final_decision: I've decided to file a real-name report with the Housing and Urban-Rural Development Bureau in my own name, and to make my position clear to the neighbor face-to-face: if you support this, that support needs to be real action. I understand if you're not willing to co-sign, but don't use 'why bother' to strip this thing of its meaning. I say to the old neighbor: 'Brother, I'm going to file the report — putting my name down alone is fine. But I want you to remember: tonight, you said with your own mouth that none of this was a problem. If anyone asks about it down the road, I hope you'll tell the truth. Two million yuan is not a small thing. I'm not doing this for a few thousand bucks — I just don't want the people in this complex to think that if someone steals, they steal, and nobody does a damn thing about it.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 决定以个人名义实名向住建局举报，同时当面向邻居表明态度：支持就要有真实的行动，不愿联名可以理解，但不要用'何必呢'来消解这件事的意义。我对老邻居说：'大哥，我会去举报，名字写我一个人没问题。但我想请你记住，今晚你亲口说这些东西没问题——如果将来有人问起，希望你能说实话。两百万不是小事，我不是为了几千块，我是不想让这个小区的人觉得，贪了就贪了，没人管。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择实名牵头举报，但不替他们的退缩粉饰。 我会答应自己署名去住建局，同时当面说清：你们能暗中帮材料，就别再劝我算了。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 拒绝单独实名举报，决定将维权行动彻底公开化。我看着邻居的眼睛提高音量说：“老哥，躲在暗处赢不了！我这就去业主群开语音，咱们当着全小区的面把账算清楚，要签大家一起签！”

inner_consciousness.summary: That line of text on my phone screen hit me like a rock thrown straight into my skull — the president had quit. I stood at the doorway of the activity room, staring at the scattered documents, the task-assignment chart still scrawled on the whiteboard, and felt a heaviness pressing against my chest. The astronomy club is one of the few places I've genuinely cared about since my first year of high school — not to impress anyone, but because those telescopes and star charts made me feel like I existed inside something larger and more ordered. And now this wreck of a situation… The other members stood there with their heads down, murmuring 'maybe we should just cancel,' and I could feel them waiting for me to nod, waiting for me to hand them a dignified way out. My mom's words from last night were still ringing in my ears — finals, summer camp, top hundred — each one pressing down on me like another weight added to the pile. If I take this over, I'll be completely swallowed by coordination and logistics for the next ten days: phone calls to sponsors, meetings with the student union, scheduling volunteers — every single one of those things is exactly what I'm worst at, exactly what drains me most. And yet I've polished those telescopes so many times on my own, revised the stargazing observation plan over and over again… just cancel it? I can't make myself say 'fine.' But I also know, clearly, that trying to single-handedly hold up all the social coordination work will break me. I need to find a way to push this forward without being the one standing at the center of it all.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heaviness and resistance (an instinctive aversion to being dragged into social coordination, layered over anger at seeing years of effort so easily abandoned); stubborn defiance within the contradiction (unwilling to concede to 'cancel,' yet clear-eyed about where my own limits are). The intensity is fairly high — especially that tearing feeling of being pushed to the front while simultaneously knowing I can't just do nothing.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many breakdowns from being forced to stand in front of a crowd, forced to coordinate and speak up, I know instinctively that I can't carry all the external communication on my own. But the astronomy club is one of the few things I've ever truly invested in — letting it be cancelled would mean personally destroying something I built with my own effort, and that is harder to accept than any amount of social exhaustion.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'something I genuinely care about cannot be allowed to disappear just because others ran away' matters more than 'avoiding all social hassle.' But 'being forced to become the central figure for all external coordination' is something I cannot accept — I need to find a way to keep the event alive without being pushed into the very eye of the social storm.

pairs.final_decision: I decide not to cancel the event, but I propose a compromise: split the remaining tasks, explicitly assign the parts that require external communication (confirming sponsors, contacting guest speakers) to whichever members are still willing to stay, and take on the parts I can handle independently myself (the venue layout plan, organizing the volunteer schedule). I speak up: 'We're not cancelling. Whoever can make phone calls, go make them — I'll handle the scheduling and the venue plan. We work separately. Ten days is enough.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 决定不取消活动，但提出折中方案：将剩余任务拆分，把必须对外沟通的部分（赞助商确认、嘉宾联络）明确分配给还愿意留下的社员，自己负责可以独立完成的部分（场地布置方案、志愿者排班表整理）。我开口说：'不取消。你们谁能打电话就去打电话，我来整理排班和场地方案，分开做，十天够了。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不直接取消，但把活动大幅缩减到我能独自控住的程度。 我会说先别报取消，我今晚重排清单，砍掉赞助和复杂环节，只保住观测和基础布置。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择顺水推舟，同意取消活动。我暗自松了口气，低声说：“那就取消吧，我去跟团委写申请，大家都回去复习。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the teacher's gaze landed on me, it was as if all the air had been sucked out of the classroom — every head swiveled in my direction, and I know that feeling all too well, like needles, like being pinned in place. My palms started sweating immediately, and I'd been gripping the corner of my notebook so hard it had gone a little soft. But… but this time is different. Something is churning in my head — the sources in that book, those primary documents, the ones I'd gone through page by page at the library last week, the records right there in black and white — the causal relationship the teacher just described simply doesn't hold up. This isn't me guessing wildly; this is documented. I don't want to stand up, I really don't — the memory of being forced to the front of the room, the laughter, the stares, all of it is stirring in some corner of my mind, restless and ready. But if I just nod along and say 'I understand,' what does that make all those words I read so carefully? I'm not trying to perform — I just… I just know he got it wrong. Can I say just a few sentences, sit back down when I'm done, nothing more, no elaborating, just point out the contradiction and let it end there? I'll stand up — my voice might shake — but the evidence is real.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (rooted in the traumatic memory of being repeatedly forced into public speaking, high intensity); yet alongside it, an unstoppable intellectual drive — because I've confirmed that what I know is reliable, and that drive keeps the anxiety from completely overriding my reason.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through so many moments of being forced to speak and coming up empty — all that shame — my instinct is to shrink back; but this time I have my notebook, page numbers, citations, things I built up carefully in my own solitude. If I can't even say this, then what was all that time alone worth?
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, genuine knowledge and things I've read with real care cannot just be nodded away — they are what I've built, with intention, in my own space. What I cannot accept is being turned into a performer. I only want to say those few sentences, put the facts out there, and then disappear back into my own seat.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stand up, but only to state the facts — no drawn-out argument — and sit back down the moment I'm done. I rise, my voice a little dry but as steady as I can keep it: 'Professor, last week I came across an academic monograph on this period of history, and the primary documents it cites describe this causal relationship differently — there's one point that doesn't quite match what you've said. I'm not sure which account is more accurate, but I wanted to raise it.' Then I lower my head and sit down, one hand pressed flat on my notebook.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择站起来，但只陈述事实、不展开争论，说完立即坐下。我站起来，声音有些干涩但尽量稳住：'老师，我上周读到一本涉及这段历史的学术专著，里面引用的原始档案对这个因果关系有不同的表述，和您讲的有一处出入，我不确定哪个更准确，但想提出来。'说完低头坐下，手按住笔记本。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择谨慎表达异议，不正面顶撞老师。 我站起来低声说我查到过另一种说法，想课后把资料页码给老师看。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避冲突，掩藏真实观点。我僵硬地站起身，把笔记本合上一点，低声说：“没问题，我只是在补抄板书。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The footsteps in the hallway echo one by one against the terrazzo floor, like someone counting down how many steps I have left to stall. The moment that person smacked the back of my head, my neck locked up — that familiar feeling, being made a target, being watched, everyone around hearing it and nobody saying a word. Now the door is half-open, the fluorescent light is a harsh, eye-stinging white, and he's sitting there waiting for me to speak. I know what he wants. I also know what that classmate wants. Both sides are using me — one holding a disciplinary action over my head, the other holding… that thing he said. It's not like I've never been in this kind of situation before. My whole life — being surrounded, being watched, being pressured to take a side — every single time it felt like having a layer of skin peeled off. But this time is different. He said 'accomplice.' He said a disciplinary notice mailed home. That image flashes through my mind — my mom's face as she opens the envelope. It's not that classmate I'm afraid of. What I'm afraid of is being treated like an object, a chess piece, squeezed from both sides at once. But what I'm even more afraid of is standing here, with this director staring at me, asking and asking, waiting and waiting. I just want to get out of this room. The fastest way out is to give him an answer. But I don't want to become that kind of person — not out of any sense of loyalty, but because I don't want to be remembered by anyone, I don't want to get dragged in any deeper, I don't want to walk into that classroom every day afterward and have to face those looks. If I say something, the whole thing will never be over.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Oppression (core): being pressured from two sides has activated a deep, long-accumulated fear of being watched and forced to take a stance; Anxiety (moderate-high intensity): the threat of disciplinary action triggers a vivid imagining of the consequences at home; Resistance (moderate intensity): an instinctive aversion to being used as a tool, exploited by both parties; Exhaustion (underlying tone): the social drain compounded by the immediate high-pressure situation — every part of me wants to escape this scene as quickly as possible.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because every single time since I was little that I was pushed to the front and stared at while people waited for me to speak, it ended in nothing but humiliation and getting pulled in even deeper — so I know instinctively that in moments like this, the less I say and the vaguer I am, the faster I can get out. It's not about protecting anyone else. It's about protecting myself from being dragged along by this thing any further.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is not being pulled along by anyone else's business, not becoming a piece in someone else's game. What I cannot accept is that because of something I say or don't say, I end up spending every day from here on out living in the aftershocks of that one moment.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to give a vague, minimizing response — neither directly identifying the person who brought the contraband, nor explicitly denying that I saw anything — in order to end this conversation as quickly as possible and get out of here. I say to the director: "I was reading at the time. I didn't notice who brought it — I really couldn't make it out."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择给出模糊而最小化的回应，既不直接指认带牌人，也不明确否认看见了什么，以求尽快结束这场谈话、脱离现场。我对主任说："我那时候在看书，没注意是谁带的，真的没看清楚。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择有限度地如实说明，先自保，不替任何人遮掩。 我只说我看到是谁带的牌，也补一句那句威胁话，请主任别再把我牵进去。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview:
选择回避冲突，以不知情为由拒绝站队。
我低下头，盯着自己的鞋尖，声音发着抖说：“主任，我当时在想题，真的没看清是谁拿出来的牌。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The note is in my hand — the handwriting is round and bubbly, with a little sun drawn on it — and my heart slams hard against my chest, then immediately I feel this indescribable, creeping panic. I've written about the way that person smiles in my diary, written about it for so long, and I've never told a single soul — not even her, sitting right beside me. But she likes the same person. That realization lands on my chest like a block of ice, and I don't even know whether I should feel happy or terrified. I hadn't even managed to put the note away before she was already leaning over — my fingers instinctively curled it into my textbook, but it was too late. She wants to see. If she sees it, what will she know? What will she think? My mind is a complete mess, and all of it comes flooding in at once — the feeling of being watched, the feeling of being forced to explain myself, the feeling of every pair of eyes in the room converging on me. I don't want to explain. I don't want to be asked. I don't want to stand at the center of anyone's gaze. Meeting up at the field after school — that means standing under that big banyan tree, facing that person, having to talk, having to be seen. Just thinking about it makes my fingers start to tremble. I just want to tuck this note away and sit with all of this alone.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Panic (the helpless disorientation after my heart slammed — the frantic alarm of having my secret crush suddenly touched): ; Defensive withdrawal (years of being forced into the open have made me instinctively want to hide this, to keep it from anyone's eyes); Conflicted suppression (there are real, genuine feelings for that person, but showing up means exposing myself, and that thought makes it almost impossible to breathe). The intensity is high — especially the moment my best friend leaned in close.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many moments of being forced into the open — the shame, the helplessness of it — I've come to treat anything that might make me the center of attention as a threat by instinct, even when that thing is something I actually long for.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is being able to handle my own affairs on my own terms, without anyone else's gaze pushing its way in. What I cannot bear is being pressured to take a stance — or being seen through — before I've even had a chance to figure out what I feel.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hide the contents of the note and refuse to let my friend see it; at the same time, I'm deeply resistant to the idea of showing up at all, and I'm leaning toward not going. I flip my textbook over, press the note underneath it, look up, and give a small smile: 'It's nothing — just a classmate asking about homework. Nothing interesting.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择遮掩纸条内容、拒绝好友查看，同时内心对赴约极度抗拒，倾向于不去。我把课本往上一翻，把纸条压进去，抬头笑了一下说：'没什么，就是同学问作业的事，没意思的。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 先隐瞒纸条内容，放学后独自赴约。我把纸条迅速合上收进课本，只说“没什么”；放学后我会去大榕树。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择隐瞒纸条内容并彻底逃避邀约。我猛地把纸条揉成一团死死捏在手心，僵硬地对好朋友说：'没什么，别人传错的废纸。'放学后我打算立刻回家，绝不去赴约。

inner_consciousness.summary: My name came flying out of the microphone, clear as a bell, and the air in the entire auditorium seemed to freeze for one second — then every head swiveled toward me at once, applause surging over me like a wave, and all I wanted was to sink through the floor and disappear. I stumbled up to the stage, my feet like they were sinking into cotton, camera flashes popping against my face, and that feeling of being pinned by hundreds of pairs of eyes — it collapsed on top of every other feeling I'd ever had: being dragged out into the hallway by a teacher when I was little, being surrounded by relatives who forced me to draw for them, being called up to the piano in front of everyone and hitting the wrong notes. I took the trophy, my palms soaked in sweat, and the icy cold of the crystal only made me more sharply aware that I was standing dead center in the spotlight. Mr. Zhao held the microphone out to me, hundreds of pairs of eyes waiting quietly below the stage, the red light on the camera in the corner still blinking. My mind went completely blank — not because I didn't know what I'd written, but because I simply could not get words out in front of this many people. My throat felt like something was blocking it; the sound just wouldn't come. It's not that I'm not grateful. It's just that… the award is real, but this moment is torture for me. Can it please be over soon — can I please get out of here, back to somewhere no one is staring at me.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Panic (an unexpected honor triggers not joy but the terror of being exposed); Shame (the gaze of hundreds of eyes activates every painful memory of being forced to stand before people); Numbness (extreme anxiety short-circuits my thinking, making it impossible to form words); A quiet, aching grievance (this is supposed to be a good thing, and yet it only makes me feel awful)
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have lived through too many moments of being forced to stand in front of people — voice trembling, mind blank, then laughed at — my body already knows, instinctively, in the second that microphone is held out to me: I cannot get a single complete sentence out. It's not that I don't want to speak. It's that my body has already collapsed ahead of my will.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, creating is something that belongs to myself alone — when I wrote that story, it was just me, and that was real; being propped up on a stage and put on display like this feels like someone has forced open the door to my room. I cannot bear the thought of myself standing in front of hundreds of people, rambling incoherently, being recorded by a camera.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to force myself to open my mouth anyway, but I squeeze out only a handful of words and make a hasty exit — unable to truly share anything at all. I lean into the microphone and say quietly: 'It's just… something I wrote on a whim. Thank you.' Then I hand the microphone back to Mr. Zhao and retreat to the side, head down.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择硬撑着开口，但只挤出极短的几个字草草收场，无法真正分享任何内容。我对着话筒低声说：'就是……随便写的，谢谢。'然后把话筒还给赵老师，低着头退到一旁。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择极简发言，尽快结束这个被围观的时刻。我接过话筒，只说谢谢老师和评委，灵感来自平时随便想的东西，然后把话筒还回去。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the wind rushed in I saw them — those red marks, some deeper than others, some still fresh — and it was like something slammed into my head, left me standing there completely frozen. She was trembling. Her eyes held something I recognized, that feeling of being crushed until you can't breathe — I know it, I know exactly what that feels like. Then she grabbed my wrist and said I couldn't tell anyone, said if I did, that would be the end of it. My palms started to sweat. I thought about all those times I was forced to stand in front of everyone, that terror of being betrayed, of being exposed — I know how unbearable that is. But this is different — this isn't reading a script on a stage, this is her cutting herself with a blade. Those marks aren't an ordinary secret. It's not that I don't care about her — it's precisely because I do that I know I can't carry this alone. I have no idea how to help her; I'm just someone gasping for air the same way she is. If I promised — if I promised and then one day something happened to her… that thought sent my stomach lurching. I can't pretend this is something I'm capable of keeping by myself.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Fear (born from the shock of seeing the wounds, and a clear-eyed awareness of my own limits); a crushing dilemma (my love for the friendship pulling against my fear for her safety); a quiet, creeping guilt (the sense that no matter what I choose, she'll be hurt). The intensity is high — but it isn't panic. It's that feeling of something pressing down on your chest, where you're completely lucid and yet completely weighed down.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many moments of being forced into exposure, of being betrayed — I know how devastating it is when trust is broken. But I also know that carrying something like this alone only pulls you deeper. Those marks aren't something I can protect with silence. Promising her would only drag us both under.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: To me, truly caring about someone doesn't mean giving them what they want — it means not letting them disappear from this world. What I can't live with is this: I kept a promise, and then one day something happened to her, and I did nothing.

pairs.final_decision: I refuse to make the promise of secrecy — but not with coldness. I tell her clearly that I can't agree to this one thing, because I'm too afraid of losing her. I look down, my voice a little hoarse: 'I can't promise you that… not because I don't care about you — because I do care about you. I can't carry this alone. We need to find someone who can actually help you.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 拒绝做出保密承诺，但不是冷漠地拒绝，而是明确告诉她我没办法答应这一条，因为我太怕失去她。我低着头，声音有点哑：'我没办法答应你这个……不是因为我不在乎你，是因为我在乎你，我一个人扛不住这件事，我们需要找一个真的能帮你的人。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不答应绝对保密，但先站在他这边稳住他。 我先说我不会立刻去告诉所有人，再劝他和我一起找一个可靠的大人。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择当场妥协保密，但内心决定拉开距离。我僵硬地点点头，低声说“我答应你”，慢慢抽回手，在接下来的日子里本能地躲避与ta的单独接触。

inner_consciousness.summary: My friend's words land in my ears, and I'm standing in the hallway feeling like the floor has turned to cotton beneath my feet. The wave of 'wows' from a moment ago hasn't quite faded, but the chorus of agreement inside the classroom rings even clearer — no rehearsals, bring food, just sing, how convenient. I know what that means. I spent three evenings on this: mapping out the lighting cues, imagining the glow of each era falling from a different angle… all of that feels like a stack of scrap paper now. My friend says to go talk to her, merge the two proposals. The image flashes in my mind immediately — walking over, smiling, saying 'can we collaborate?' — and then being looked over by her whole crew with that politely indifferent gaze, until my work gets diluted into 'food plus karaoke' and there's nothing left of it. I've seen this happen before. I know exactly how that tastes. And as for canvassing votes — going up to people one by one to explain what a 'time tunnel' even is — just imagining myself standing in front of some classmate trying to make the case, my throat already feels dry. That's not something I'm capable of doing. Honestly, what I want most right now is to go home, put those sketches away, and never look at them again. If the proposal loses, it loses — that's still better than sacrificing myself in the process. I don't want to force myself into someone I'm not just for a class party.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Exhaustion and withdrawal (core): the crowd's easy chorus of agreement has stirred up years of feeling swallowed by the group; Mild resentment: my proposal was brushed aside so effortlessly, my creative work treated as raw material anyone can freely absorb; A vague, nameless sense of loss: not because of losing, but because those three evenings of work were never truly seen.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many cycles of pushing myself into the crowd only to come out worn down, I know instinctively that whether I go negotiate a merger or spend the weekend canvassing votes, what gets spent is something I simply don't want to spend — and that proposal existed in the first place because it made me happy to make it.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: To me, that proposal is something entirely my own. The moment I take it into a negotiation or use it to solicit votes, it stops being what it was. I can't accept turning myself into someone who has to go around selling themselves just to win a vote.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to step away from the competition — I won't go talk to her about merging, and I won't spend the weekend canvassing votes. I'll let the proposal lose on its own terms. I tell my friend: "Forget it — if everyone likes her proposal, they should go with it. I'll keep mine for myself."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择放弃竞争，既不去找对方谈合并，也不花周末拉票，让方案随投票结果自然落败。我对朋友说："算了，她那个方案大家喜欢就用吧，我的东西留着自己用。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我不去主动谈合并，也不挨个拉票硬拼。周末我把方案整理成一页更清楚的展示稿，交给想看的人看，周一让投票自己决定。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择放弃竞争，主动退出方案之争。我摇了摇头对朋友说：“算了，我不去拉票，也不想合并，他们爱选什么选什么吧。”然后转身独自离开。

inner_consciousness.summary: The sound of that hard drive hitting the corner of the desk is still rattling around in my ears, and my stomach feels like someone's grabbed it and won't let go. Six months. Staying up until two, three in the morning every night — all those source files, all those assets I revised over and over again, everything was in there. And now a stack of cash gets slapped down on the coffee table — 'It's just a hard drive with some files on it.' He has absolutely no idea what that was. I stare at that stack of bills and feel something rising in my throat. The drawing that got torn up when I was a kid, being shoved to the center of a crowd, the sound of my father shutting off the computer — that feeling of having something that matters to you casually destroyed and then paid off with money, I know it far too well. My mom's voice message is still playing in my head: 'Don't make people think our family is petty.' I'm not being petty. It's that those six months simply cannot be converted into cash. I know that if I speak up right now my voice will shake, and I'll be treated like I'm making a big deal out of nothing, and someone will say 'young people overthink things.' But I also know that if I just nod and take that stack of money, then all those nights with the lights on at two in the morning will have meant absolutely nothing. I don't want to explode — I'm too exhausted for that — but I also can't pretend none of this happened.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed rage (core — six months of effort casually priced out with a stack of bills, triggering the accumulated wound of 'creative work / personal space violated and met with indifference' that has repeated since childhood); deep grief (that hard drive wasn't just data — it was the one quiet world that belonged entirely to me); intense humiliation (being placated like a child who doesn't know any better); exhausted restraint (years of social wear have made him instinctively want to hold it down rather than explode on the spot). Intensity: extremely high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because over and over again, my whole life, I've watched things I cared about get casually destroyed and then brushed off with 'it wasn't on purpose' or 'it's not a big deal' — so I know instinctively that that stack of money isn't compensation, it's hush money. And I can't bring myself to sell those six months out with my silence.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Those late nights over six months were real — they actually happened — and they are not 'files' that can be converted into a few hundred-yuan bills. What I cannot accept is this: the thing I cared about most was priced in the most dismissive way possible, and then I'm supposed to be grateful for his generosity.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to speak up and name the true weight of what was lost, to refuse the money — but to say it in as steady a voice as I can manage rather than letting it become an explosion. Not because I expect my uncle to understand, but because I can't let myself stay silent. I push the stack of bills back toward him, my voice a little dry but not trembling: 'Uncle, I can't take this. What was on that drive was six months of my work — it's not something a few bills can bring back.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择开口说出损失的真实重量，拒绝接受那叠钱，但用尽量平稳的语气而非爆发来表达——不是为了让大伯理解，而是不能让自己沉默。我把那叠钱推回去，声音有些干，但没有颤抖：'大伯，这钱我不能收。那里面是我六个月的工作文件，不是几块钱能买回来的东西。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择当场撕破表面的客气，明确拒收这笔钱并追究。我要把钱推回去，直接说这不是赔几百块的事，请你们现在离开，之后按数据恢复和损失正式谈。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝和解，直接下逐客令并物理隔离。我没有碰那叠钱，径直走过去捡起地上的硬盘残骸，指着大门低声但坚决地说：“拿着你的钱，带着他，现在就从我家出去。”

inner_consciousness.summary: Lin Xiaoyu's tears and those eyes locked onto me, refusing to let go… I felt the entire café pressing down on me. She was trembling, and the moment her phone screen lit up her face went completely white — that wasn't performance, that was real fear. My first instinct was to run, to tell myself 'this isn't my problem,' to retreat back into my shell. But I couldn't retreat. She came to me not because we're close, but because she had no one else to turn to. That senior colleague… the power dynamics are complicated… and tomorrow there's a one-on-one meeting. My mind is a mess — that feeling of being cornered against a wall with nowhere to move, I know it all too well — the teacher in the hallway, the boy by the pool, all those moments when the words just wouldn't come out. But right now she's in more danger than I ever was. I'm not good at this kind of thing, I don't know what to say that would actually help her, I don't even know what happens once I get involved. But there's one thing I do know: that meeting tomorrow cannot just happen. She can't carry this alone. I'm allowed to hate this — but I can't pretend I didn't see that look on her face.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heaviness and unease (being dragged into a social crisis triggers an instinctive urge to flee, but witnessing genuine fear and danger activates a deep moral sense — the two pulling against each other in intense internal conflict); Oppression (memories of being cornered in a hallway as a child, surrounded with no way to speak, resurface as bodily sensations and resonate with Lin Xiaoyu's situation). Intensity: high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I know what it feels like to be trapped, to have no voice, to have no one step up — I can't choose silence when she needs someone most, even if getting involved will drain every last bit of my energy, even if I have no idea what I'm doing.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Solitude and being left alone is my default state, but there are things I can't look away from — the cost of that would be too much to bear. Her safety matters more than my comfort. There's no way around that.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to intervene and offer real, concrete protection — but on my own terms, through deliberate action rather than impulsive confrontation. I tell Lin Xiaoyu: 'Don't go to that meeting alone tomorrow — I'll figure something out. I'll use project coordination as a reason to get myself inserted into that meeting arrangement, so you won't have to face him by yourself. As for reporting it, we can take that one step at a time — but tonight, write down the times, the places, and what happened, and send it to me. Get it on record.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择介入并提供实质性保护，但以自己能掌控的方式行动，而非冲动对抗。我对林小雨说：'明天那个会议你不要一个人去，我来想办法——我会以项目协调为由介入那个会议安排，你不需要单独面对他。关于举报的事，我们可以一步一步来，但今晚你先把时间、地点、发生的事情写下来发给我，留存记录。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择有限介入，先保护她并保留证据，再谨慎推动处理。我先让她别单独赴约，陪她整理信息并由我出面取消明天单独会。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避直接冲突，提供最小卷入度的建议。我低声说：“你先找借口请假别去，把记录截图。但我没办法替你出面，你得自己决定要不要匿名发给HR。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The way he closed that report and set it at the corner of the desk — it was like shutting a door. I stared at that corner, something tightening in my chest. Those data points, those controlled experiments — I verified them alone, over and over, late into the night. They weren't thrown together carelessly. But he flipped through two pages and put it down, just like the time my teacher took my drawing and pinned it on the board, and the whole class started laughing. I heard him say 'postponement,' 'basis for collaboration' — those words landed on my eardrums like blunt instruments. I know exactly what he's doing: he's using time and his signature to pressure me. But I also know that if I just pack this up and walk away, those systematic biases won't disappear because of his signature — they'll blow up at the final checkpoint. He said he's supervised many cohorts and never had a problem — but the issues flagged in those independent team reports are real. I didn't fabricate them. I'm not good at getting my words out clearly under this kind of face-to-face pressure; my throat is already starting to tighten. But this time I can't do what I always do — lower my head and walk away. I need him to understand that I'm not trying to provoke him. I just can't pretend I haven't seen a known risk.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed anger (triggered by having my evidence and effort dismissed — an accumulation of countless times my work was ignored or invalidated); defensive resolve (the data I verified in solitude is the only ground I have to stand on); mild fear (the threat of postponement and losing his signature is real, not something I can afford to ignore); intense discomfort (face-to-face confrontation costs me an enormous amount of energy — my body is already resisting). Intensity: medium-high; rationality is still intact, but the edges are trembling.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: I've lived through too many times of putting my work away, then discovering in solitude that I was actually right — so I know instinctively: if I stay silent this time, I will spend countless nights afterward staring at those skewed data points and regretting it. I'm not questioning his entire academic career. I simply cannot look away from a risk point that has already been confirmed by multiple independent teams.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is this: something I verified on my own cannot become wrong just because the other person has authority. That is the only thing I truly trust. What I cannot accept is seeing a problem clearly and then pretending it doesn't exist for the sake of clearing a single milestone smoothly — that feeling is worse than a delay, worse than being laughed at.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hold my ground and present the core evidence without directly escalating the conflict, while offering a limited compromise — and I do not voluntarily withdraw my materials. I speak up; my voice comes out quieter than I expected, but it doesn't waver: "Professor, I understand the pressure of the timeline, and I'm not trying to overturn the entire existing plan — what I want to ask is: would it be possible, while the main line of work moves forward, to allow me to run an alternative procedure as a controlled comparison at one specific stage, and document the risk point on record? That way, even if the final conclusion doesn't change, there's data showing we considered this issue."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择在不正面激化冲突的前提下，坚持陈述核心证据，同时提出有限度的折中路径，不主动收回材料。我开口，声音比预想的要低，但没有颤抖："老师，我理解时间的压力，也不是要全面推翻现有方案——我想问的是，能不能在主路线推进的同时，允许我在某个具体环节用替代流程做一个可控的对照，把风险点记录在案，这样即使最后结论不变，也有数据说明我们考虑过这个问题。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择坚持风险判断，但提出折中验证方案。我会说我不主张立刻全盘推翻，只请求保留既定节点，同时加做一组小规模对照验证。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择当面回避冲突以求脱身，但绝不放弃自己的替代方案。我默默把桌角的书面说明收回包里，低声说一句“我先回去了”，然后迅速转身离开办公室。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment my phone lit up, something sank in my chest — like the stifling air pressing down one more notch. She said in a hoarse voice that she was exhausted, that she wasn't sure how much longer she could hold on. My chest tightened, and what rose up first wasn't the urge to rush to her side — it was a suffocating feeling of being backed into a corner. Like someone standing in the doorway again, pushing me to walk in, pushing me to take a stance, demanding that even my quietest inner space be handed over as collateral. It's not that I don't care about her — it's precisely because I do that I can see it so clearly: if I go there, enroll in a program that doesn't really fit me, and try to hold my life together in an unfamiliar city through the sheer force of this relationship, I'll be distracted and slowly sinking the whole time, and in the end I'll lose both my work and the relationship anyway. She's waiting for an answer, and I know that staying silent tonight would be even crueler. The pain is real. The guilt is real. But in the end I can only hold onto the one thing I still have a grip on: stay, take the path at my own school, and then tell her honestly — I can't prove my love by sacrificing the direction of my own life.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppression, guilt, resistance, and clarity. The suppression and resistance come from that old feeling of being asked, again and again, to give up my own space for the sake of a relationship, a group, or someone else's expectations. The guilt comes from not wanting her to keep enduring things alone. The clarity comes from knowing — and I've known it for a long time — that once my creative work and my rhythm are bent out of shape by outside forces, I slowly lose all my energy. The intensity is very high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I know myself too well: the moment I leave the quiet environment that suits me best and force my life to swerve in order to accommodate a relationship, I won't become better at loving someone — I'll just be hollowed out first. So my instinct tells me that trading my future and my working life for companionship isn't saving this relationship; it's burying it ahead of schedule.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is holding onto the path that is truly mine — preserving the kind of life where I can work quietly and keep creating. What I cannot accept is being stuffed into a position that was wrong from the very beginning, just to prove that the relationship matters.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stay at my own school, and I accept that this may bring the relationship to an end. I text her back, tell her I'll be staying in my own program, and am honest with her: I love her, but I can't sustain what we have by rewriting the direction of my own life.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择今晚不给出最终答案，但诚实地告诉她自己内心的撕裂，不假装一切都好。发语音回复她：'我听完了，我没办法今晚就告诉你我决定了什么，但我想让你知道我没有在逃避这件事——我只是真的不知道去了那边我能不能成为你需要的那个人，而不是一个更空的自己。我需要一点时间，但我会认真想的。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择留在本校，也接受这可能让关系走到尽头。我回她消息，说明我会读本校，并坦白我爱她，但不能靠改写自己的人生方向来维系我们。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择留在本校深造，并主动结束这段关系以止损。我打字回复她：“对不起，我决定留在本校。我给不了你需要的陪伴，我们还是分开吧。”

inner_consciousness.summary: When my senior slid that financial forecast across the table, I felt the air in the café suddenly thicken. 8% equity, CTO, taking a leave of absence — these words came at me one after another, like the moment a roommate yanks my headphones off without warning. My first reaction wasn't excitement; it was that burning feeling of being stared at, my palms already starting to sweat. I know the numbers he's talking about might be real, and I know my proposal from that competition wasn't bad. But what does co-founder actually mean? It means meetings every day, alignment every day, having to articulate yourself in front of other people every day — that feeling of being pressed in a code review until I can't string a sentence together, that feeling of standing on a stage reading from a script with my lips trembling, all of that becomes routine. The only time I'm truly alive is when I'm debugging code alone in the middle of the night; no one can give me that state, and no one can protect it for me. My senior's eyes are lit up right now, but the kind of full-time commitment and core-team dynamic he's asking for is simply not a mode I can operate in. I'm not afraid of failing — I'm afraid of that daily drain of being pulled out into the open over and over again. I need time, not by Tuesday; I need an exit right now.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Defensive vigilance (high intensity): triggered by the prospect of a high-social-density future, rooted in the body's memory of being forced into the spotlight too many times; Resistance beneath a calm surface: the belief that solitude is the only state in which I can truly work is directly threatened by this invitation; Faint self-doubt: his recognition did touch some corner of me that wants to be seen, but that feeling was immediately smothered by fear.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments of being shoved to the front and then falling apart, I know instinctively that a startup requiring daily meetings and constant external-facing work will drain me dry rather than let me produce anything worthwhile; the only state in which I can genuinely deliver is behind a closed door by myself, and any structural arrangement that breaks that state is fundamentally incompatible with who I am.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, being able to sink into something without interruption matters more than any valuation or equity stake; what I cannot accept is handing every single day of my life over to a system that demands I perform outwardly on a continuous basis — that's not a startup, that's just another way of trapping me.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to politely but clearly communicate that I cannot accept the terms of full-time commitment, carving out space to step back rather than rejecting the opportunity outright. I tell my senior: "I genuinely find your project interesting, but the condition of going full-time and taking a leave of absence isn't something I can agree to right now — not because I don't believe in it, but because I know myself well enough to know I can't perform in a high-intensity collaborative environment like that, and that wouldn't be fair to you either. If there's ever a possibility of a technical advisor role or remote collaboration down the line, I'd be happy to talk."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择礼貌但明确地表达无法接受全职投入的条件，为自己争取退出的空间而非直接拒绝整个机会。我对学长说：「你的项目我真的觉得有意思，但全职休学这个条件我现在没办法答应——不是因为不看好，是因为我知道自己在那种高强度协作环境里根本发挥不出来，这对你也不公平。如果将来有技术顾问或者远程合作的可能，我愿意再聊。」
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不当场答应，先明确表达保留和谨慎。 我会说我对方向有兴趣，但休学全职太激进，我需要回去认真想，也想先了解具体分工与协作方式。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择暂时回避正面冲突，尽快脱身并计划异步拒绝。我含糊地说休学是大事需要仔细考虑和跟家人商量，迅速离开咖啡馆，决定回宿舍后发微信正式拒绝他。

inner_consciousness.summary: The smell of disinfectant, the hum of the fluorescent lights overhead… I just want to pick up my prescription and go, my arm still aching. Then that curtain gets yanked open, the monitor starts screaming, and the young man slides down onto the floor — I saw it, of course I saw it. He crawled over and grabbed my pant leg, and my whole body locked up; that feeling of being grabbed, being stared at, being needed shot through me like a current, made me want to back away. Three thousand yuan. The trip. That route map flashed through my mind — the tickets already booked, the money already paid, the one thing that's kept me going through all of this. But his father is in there, the doctor is pressing, everyone else in the hallway has their head down, nobody's moving. I want to look down too. I want to look down too… but his hand is still gripping me, his voice is shaking, 'if it gets any later there really won't be time.' I think of all those times I've felt trapped — surrounded by crowds, stared at, forced to stand up in front of everyone — and nobody reached out then. I'm not that kind of person. I don't want to become the kind of person who keeps their head down. Money can be earned again, the trip can be rescheduled, but that man's father… I pull out my phone, check my balance — three thousand, just enough. I don't want to talk, don't want to explain, don't want to be thanked; I just want to get this done and get out of here.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Pressure and inner struggle (being touched and stared at activates a deep social discomfort, like every time I was forced to stand at the center of a crowd layered on top of each other); quiet resolve (not a hot-blooded impulse — it's a certain calm, stubborn refusal to be 'the one who looks away,' moderate in intensity but unwavering).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through the isolation of no one reaching out, over and over again, I know instinctively what it feels like to be trapped with nobody caring — I cannot be the one who looks away here, even though every part of me desperately doesn't want to be pulled into this.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Money and travel are things that can happen again, but someone being turned away from the operating room for lack of funds — right in front of me — is something I have no way of pretending I didn't see. I don't need to be thanked. I just need to finish this and leave.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to lend him the three thousand yuan, but I don't want to talk more than necessary — I want to get it done as fast as possible and go. I hold out my phone so he can see the balance, and say quietly: 'I'll transfer it to you. Give me your payment code. Doesn't matter whether you write an IOU or not.'

inner_consciousness.summary: Those blue underlines burned into my eyes like a branding iron — the browser search results were a 100% match. This wasn't some vague suspicion; it was an ironclad fact. My heart started racing, but not entirely out of anger — it was more that panicked feeling of being dragged into something against my will. Three days of research, nearly four thousand words, something I dug out alone in the quiet, and now it's going to be handed in tied to this plagiarized garbage. Grade zeroed — the professor made that crystal clear on the very first day. I don't want to face him, I really don't — the moment the door handle moved, my stomach just dropped. That post he put up flashed through my mind again: 'I'm barely holding on.' I know he's under pressure, but what does that have to do with me? I'm not his life raft. Say it to his face? Just imagining the moment he walks back in — me having to open my mouth, him having to defend himself, the whole atmosphere going rigid in this cramped little dorm room — my whole body starts to tighten up. Contact the professor? That's even more of a mess: explanations, dealing with everything that follows, and he'll know it was me. I just want to cut this thing off me and be done with it. The safest move is — screenshot those blue underlines in his document, then quietly close his laptop, wait for him to come back, find a way that doesn't require a face-to-face confrontation, send him a message, forward him the search result screenshots, tell him it's due the day after tomorrow and he can figure out what to do himself. I don't need to be the main character in this story.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (core): the threat of a zeroed grade triggers a deep fear that three days of my own work will be dragged down and destroyed alongside his — intensity is very high; Avoidance impulse (intense): the mere thought of a face-to-face confrontation makes my body tighten up — this is an instinct carved into me over years of being worn down by social friction; Calm self-protective awareness (underlying drive): it isn't anger — it's an almost mechanical clarity of 'I need to move this risk off of me.'
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because every single time in my life I've been forced into someone else's chaos, I've paid a price for it — so my instinct is to handle this at the lowest possible social cost. Not silence, not a face-to-face showdown, but using screenshots and a text message to toss the problem back to him, let him carry it himself, while I don't have to show up at any confrontation scene.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Three days of my work are real, and I can't let someone else's recklessness drag them into the mud — that's something I simply cannot accept. But I also don't want to be the person who exposes someone to their face and manufactures a dorm-room standoff; that kind of drain costs me too much. A text and some screenshots are enough to say everything that needs to be said.

pairs.final_decision: Choose to deliver the evidence asynchronously, hand the decision back to the roommate, and keep myself out of any direct confrontation. Quietly screenshot and save the search results, close his laptop, and once he walks in, send him a message: 'I accidentally saw your section just now — ran a search, sending you the screenshots. It's due the day after tomorrow. You decide what to do with it.'

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the pill bottle rolled to my feet, my heart skipped a beat. I picked it up — almost empty. Sleeping pills. I stood there, fingers wrapped around the bottle, my mind completely blank. He said 'just kick it back' — that voice, hoarse, like a machine that hadn't been used in a long time. I wanted to kick it back. I really did. This isn't my business, I'm not good at this kind of thing, I never know what to say, and every time I'm forced to open my mouth it's a disaster. But that bottle was almost empty. Two weeks — the curtains never opened, takeout boxes piling up, those sounds in the night… I kept telling myself it was none of my concern. But now this bottle is in my hand, and I can't pretend I didn't see it. Not because I know what to do, but because if I kick it back and go on sitting here, I don't know what's going to happen tonight. I don't need to say the right thing. I just need to not walk away. I take a deep breath, turn around, and walk toward that bed curtain.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heavy dread (not social anxiety, but an instinctive fear of something irreversible); a compelled urge to act — not because I'm capable, but because I cannot stand by and do nothing. Intensity is high, yet not chaotic — it's an oppressive, crushing clarity.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been ignored too many times, treated like I wasn't even there, I know what hides inside that kind of silence — so when I saw that nearly empty bottle, I instinctively knew this was not a moment I could let pass with 'it's none of my business.'
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Solitude is the most important thing to me, but some things matter more than my comfort zone. What I cannot live with is this: I was right there, and I pretended nothing was happening.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to intervene — I don't return the bottle, I walk toward the bed curtain and speak, while preparing to reach out for outside help. I grip the pill bottle in my hand, walk to his bedside, and say quietly: 'I'm not leaving — I just don't want to. I'm holding onto this bottle for now — how are you doing right now?'

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the director walked over, I knew it wasn't good news — I recognized that smile, the same one my homeroom teacher used to wear before calling me in for a talk. The second he finished speaking, my first thought wasn't about the proposal — it was that high-speed rail ticket. Tomorrow morning. Dad's stomach. Mom can't manage on her own. I've been carrying those words for three days now. But then he said, 'Just between us — if you can't pull this off, I can't bring anyone else in,' and it hooked into something. I'm not naive about what that means. I know exactly what it means: he's loading the full weight of this thing onto me alone. That's my worst fear — being singled out, being needed, and having no way out. But then there's Dad's endoscopy results, Mom's back, the promise I made them. I'm not good at speaking up in moments like this — there's always something lodged in my throat. I don't care about the year-end performance award, but if the proposal falls apart and the client walks — that kind of failure, I can't stomach that either. Both things are unbearable, and I only have one weekend. I need to say something clearly, but I don't know how to start. Every time someone's eyes are on me, I just want to shrink back.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (a suffocating, pinned-in feeling — pressure bearing down from two directions at once); Guilt (I promised my parents and now I'm on the verge of breaking that promise — the sense of betrayal is heavy); Resistance (being singled out, being made the sole person responsible — it triggers my deepest discomfort); Struggle in silence (I want to speak up and explain, but my throat feels blocked).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since I was young that once someone picks you out and keeps their eyes on you, it's very hard to walk away clean — so my instinct is to say something about the situation at home first. Not to dodge the work, but because it's the only way I don't have to lie about one thing or the other.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, a promise is a promise — especially to my parents. That means more than any KPI ever could. But at the same time, being the one person visibly holding a failed project while everyone watches — I can't accept that kind of failure either. I can't let go of either thing, so I have to say what's actually going on, even if getting the words out is hard.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to honestly explain the situation at home while proposing a middle-ground arrangement — such as handling part of the work remotely — rather than simply agreeing or outright refusing. I keep my eyes slightly down, voice low but as steady as I can manage: 'Director, my father has a follow-up appointment at the hospital this weekend — I've already promised my family I'd go back to be with him. Could we do it this way: I push as far as I can tonight and before I leave tomorrow morning, I stay reachable remotely on the way there, and for the final draft Sunday night I'll do my best to either get back in time or go through it with you online. Would that work?'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择如实说明家中情况，同时提出折中方案（如远程完成部分工作），而非直接答应或直接拒绝。我低着头，声音不大但尽量稳住：'总监，我父亲这周末要去医院复查，我已经答应家里回去陪他，能不能这样——我今晚和明天早上出发前尽量多推进，路上也可以远程，周日晚上的终稿我尽力赶回来或者线上对，你看这样行吗。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择坚持回老家，但提出远程补救和交接方案。我会直接说明父母复查必须回去，今晚先梳理清单，路上和周末远程补材料。

inner_consciousness.summary: Everyone is looking at me. I know this feeling all too well — it's like being called up to the front of the classroom as a kid, like standing at a podium in the auditorium with my hands shaking over a script, like being cut off mid-sentence in one of those brainstorming sessions and having everyone pretend it never happened. My file is right there on the screen, I know what's in it, and I know I'm right — that plan is genuinely going to run into trouble down the line with maintenance, and this isn't just a feeling, I actually worked through it. But everyone has already signed off on it now. Someone is jotting down task assignments, the lead is waiting for me to say something, time is tight, the atmosphere is tight. What happens if I speak up? They'll frown, they'll say 'you already brought this up last time,' they'll sweep me with that impatient look. My voice has always come out small in situations like this — whatever I manage to say always ends up drowned out or cut off. But if I don't say anything… this plan really is going to cause problems later, and that'll be an even bigger mess. I'm not trying to show off, I just know there's something wrong with it. But my throat is already starting to tighten, and my palms are a little damp. Maybe I can say just one short thing — just one sentence — and then let them decide. Or I could send the file over, without having to say it out loud. Without having to say it out loud.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Conflicted tension (a certainty in my own technical judgment pulling against an instinctive dread of speaking in front of others; intensity moderate-to-high); suppressed anxiety (accumulated from countless memories of being ignored, interrupted, and pinned under the spotlight — all of it activated right now).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Every time I open my mouth I get drowned out or cut off — that feeling is like being held down — so my instinct is to pull back and go quiet. But this time I actually have something concrete in my hands, not just a feeling but a real plan, and that makes it impossible for me to stay completely silent.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: I can live with not being understood, but I can't live with seeing a problem clearly and saying nothing, then watching it go wrong right in front of me. What I worked out on my own, in private — that shouldn't stop counting just because people are watching me.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to speak up briefly — raising my concern without trying to hard-sell my alternative — and leave the file as a written supplement for them to decide on. I say: 'I still think there could be some strain when it comes to scaling later on. I'll write up the plan and send it to everyone — whichever way you go is up to you.'

inner_consciousness.summary: The hallway lights flickered on and off, and the moment that cigarette butt was brought close to my face, I felt my entire body lock up — not the first time, this feeling of being cornered with nowhere to run, it started in that dark stairwell basement, it started the day my water gun was snatched away by the pool. His foot was planted on my suitcase, two guys behind him tapping baseball bats, this was not a place for reason — there was a voice in my head saying: screaming won't do anything, he knows people at the local precinct. But the deposit — how hard it was to scrape that money together, the price I paid to finally have a place of my own. My hands were shaking, not because I was afraid of him, but because it was freezing cold, and because my anger had nowhere to go. I thought about that morning I was falsely accused in the tech club, I thought about the version of myself who slipped away that night at the debate team — every time I've gone head-to-head, what I've lost isn't the argument, it's my body. Three people, a baseball bat, the middle of the night, not a single neighbor opening their door. I have no leverage. The only thing I can do right now is get out of here, keep myself safe, write down everything about tonight — the contract terms, the time, exactly what he said — and then figure out my next move. This isn't surrender. It's a strategic retreat. My studio is still there, my code is still there, and as long as I'm still standing, I still have cards to play.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed fear (the physical threat has activated memories of being trapped and cornered in childhood — fear intensity is high); suppressed rage (having the deposit forcibly taken and my space invaded cuts right to my deepest sense of boundaries, but the rage is crushed down by the immediate danger); a cold, hollow powerlessness (the three-against-one imbalance snuffs out every impulse to fight back before it can even reach my throat).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since I was small that going head-to-head only leaves me more humiliated and more hurt, my instinct is to put 'stay alive, fall back first' ahead of 'get justice on the spot' — that's not cowardice, it's the one thing I can still control.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, physical safety and preserving my ability to keep working matter more than in-the-moment dignity; but I cannot accept walking away as a total defeat — leaving is not the same as giving up, and documenting everything so I can pursue legal recourse later is the only form of fighting back that's actually within my reach.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to comply and leave for now, without confronting anyone on the spot — but I make a silent decision to pursue the deposit through legal channels afterward. I keep my head down, my voice steady but very quiet: 'I'm leaving now, but I'm keeping this contract, and we'll settle the deposit matter another time.' Then I bend down to gather the clothes scattered on the floor, pick up my suitcase, and walk out of the hallway.

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment my ex took my hand, I felt something shift inside my chest — not quite a flutter of feeling, more like an old drawer being pulled open, all those quiet stretches of those two years, the afternoons we spent tucked away in some corner together, suddenly rushing back up. But… I'm with someone now. And honestly, these three months with my current partner have been one of the rare relationships where I don't feel so drained — she knows how to give me space. Every word my ex said — travel, plans, starting over — each one means more socializing, more expectations, more emotional output I'd have to produce on demand. My heart was racing, but I couldn't tell whether that was longing or panic. He said, "Have you really let me go completely?" — and no, I haven't completely let go, but so what? Whether I've let go or not isn't a reason to go back. I thought about all those times I'd been pushed into that position, forced to stand under the spotlight, forced to speak, forced to give an answer. I don't want to be pushed around by this question. I need to say what needs to be said, and then leave, and go back to my own space. As for telling my current partner — I don't want to hide it; that would turn into a weight hanging over my head, more exhausting than just saying it out loud.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Calm with an undercurrent of quiet wavering (triggered by the brief resurfacing of old memories), alongside an instinctive resistance (the pressure of being pushed toward the center of an emotional decision). The wavering is moderate in intensity but quickly suppressed; the resistance is the more dominant force.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many moments of being pushed along by other people's expectations, of being forced to stand in the spotlight and suffocate under it, I instinctively resist any invitation that asks me to open myself back up, to take on emotional responsibility again — even when the person asking was once genuinely part of my life.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is that state of being where I'm not depleted — where I can truly be alone without feeling abandoned. What I cannot accept is pushing myself back into a relationship that demands heavy emotional output and is full of uncertainty; that exhausts me more than any social situation ever could.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to turn my ex down — gently but clearly, leaving no room for ambiguity — and at the same time I decide to tell my current partner honestly what this meeting was about. I speak up: "I haven't completely forgotten what we had, but I'm in a new relationship now, and I'm not going back. Thank you for telling me all of this." When I get home, I'll tell my current partner directly what happened today.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择温和但明确地拒绝前任，不给任何模糊的余地；同时决定如实告诉现任这次见面的内容。我开口说："我没有完全忘记我们，但我现在有了新的感情，我不会回头。谢谢你告诉我这些。" 回去后，我会直接告诉现任今天发生了什么。
