decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择拒绝前任，对现任保密以避免引发沟通消耗。我抽回手说：‘过去了，我喜欢现在的安静。’随后起身离开。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment he spoke, it was as if all the air had been sucked out of the open office — everyone stopped what they were doing, eyes shuttling back and forth between him and me. I know that feeling all too well. Every time I was pushed into the center of a crowd growing up, every suffocating moment at the podium, every pair of eyes waiting for me to fall apart… and here it is again. His arms were crossed, that little curl at the corner of his mouth — I could read it perfectly: this isn't a real question, it's a performance. He's testing whether I'll panic. I felt my back tighten slightly, my palms go a little damp, but at the same time something was pushing back up from inside. This time is different. I'm not a kid being forced onto a stage — I'm the team lead, this is my meeting, and this question is my territory. That unfinished module — I went through the schedule over the weekend, I know exactly how to handle it. He wants me to unravel, but I can't let him see me unravel. Take a deep breath. I don't need to beat him — I just need to hold myself steady, say what needs to be said clearly, and let everyone else see that I know what I'm doing.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Taut but suppressed calm (a defensive instinct triggered by countless memories of being challenged in public); low-intensity alertness (the way he's baiting me lets me recognize this as a test, not a straightforward technical question); faint but genuine stubbornness (a hard-won, solitary sense of knowing my own work).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments of falling apart under the gaze of a crowd — I know exactly what that shame feels like — my instinct this time is to refuse to repeat it. Not to beat him, but to keep myself from collapsing in front of everyone all over again.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is being able to stand on solid ground in my own work, even if that just means quietly saying what needs to be said; what I cannot accept is losing my grip on my own work in front of the whole team because I panicked — that kind of exposure is harder to bear than any criticism.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to meet the challenge head-on, respond with facts, neither dodging nor snapping back, and reclaim control of the meeting's rhythm with a steady tone. I hold his gaze, keep my voice even: 'I can handle it. I went through the unfinished module from last quarter over the weekend — we'll slot it into today's team meeting. If you have any specific concerns, say them now and we'll go through them together.' Then I turn to the rest of the team and signal everyone to sit down and get started.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择正面接住质疑、用事实回应，不回避也不反唇相讥，以平稳的语气重新掌控会议节奏。我直视他，声音放平：'接得住。上季度未完成的模块我周末看过了，今天组会我们就把它排进去，你有什么具体的顾虑现在可以说，我们一起过一遍。'随即转向全组，示意大家坐下准备开会。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不接情绪性的挑衅，直接把组会拉回排期和分工。 我看着他说：我先过季度目标和未完成模块，风险我来接，总体拆分现在开始。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 回避情绪博弈，强行降级为异步沟通。我盯着屏幕说：排期已发群里，有具体问题稍后一对一过，现在散会。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment my collar was grabbed, the whole world felt like someone had hit fast-forward — my phone screen was still lit up with the work group chat, I hadn't done anything, I hadn't done anything, and yet that person's screaming had already nailed me to the spot. A phone camera was pointed at my face, the viewer count was ticking up, phones all around me were recording too, and that feeling… it was like being dragged up to the front of the class in kindergarten, like being surrounded on the playground and mocked for going mute, like standing at a microphone with my mind gone completely blank — every single one of those moments stacked on top of each other and came crashing down at once. I wasn't taking secret photos. I was just exhausted, and there was nowhere to put my hands — but what good is explaining that now? Nobody's listening, nobody's looking at the screen, there's only lenses and shouting. The middle-aged man was blocking the exit, my heart was pounding so hard it felt like it might burst, but I knew that the more I panicked the worse it would get — yelling would get edited into 'violent reaction,' running would get filmed as 'fleeing the scene.' I'm not the kind of person who can speak up loudly in a crowd. I never have been. But this time, if I stay silent, my face is going to be frozen in that livestream forever. I need to let the phone screen do the talking — not my mouth.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Panic layered with humiliation — every memory of being watched, of being forced to stand at the center of everyone's gaze, all activated at once, intensity extreme; alongside that, a thin sliver of ice-cold clarity, because every time I've tried to explain myself in the past no one believed me, and this time I have to find evidence that doesn't depend on words.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments where explaining myself only brought louder ridicule, I know instinctively: in a situation like this — where you've been set up as the spectacle — your mouth is the most useless weapon you have. The only thing that might break through is making them look at the facts on the screen.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, solitude and the right not to be intruded upon are the most basic conditions of existence; being forcibly thrust into the center of everyone's attention is the hardest thing I can bear. But what I can bear even less is being stuck with a label that can never be washed off — that destroys my space more completely than any social torment ever could.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to break the situation with physical evidence rather than verbal argument, forcing myself to speak but taking the shortest possible path straight to the proof. I raise my phone screen high and turn it toward the people filming around me, and with everything I have I squeeze out one sentence: 'Look at the screen — work group chat, timestamp, I was scrolling the group, I wasn't photographing you. See for yourselves.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择用物证而非口头辩解打破局面，强迫自己开口但以最短路径指向证据。将手机屏幕高举转向周围拍摄者，用尽全力挤出一句话：「看屏幕——工作群消息，时间戳，我在刷群，不是拍你，你们自己看。」
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择克制自保，不和围观者正面冲突，立刻固定证据并要求报警。 我先高声说我没偷拍、请别碰我，同时举起手机录下现场并要求到站找乘警。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择防御性退缩与消极否认，首要目标是切断物理接触并逃离注视。我用力掰开对方揪住我衣领的手，把亮着工作群的手机屏幕朝向她，低下头死死避开所有镜头，声音发紧地挤出一句：“我没有，我在看工作。”然后拼命往车门方向退。

inner_consciousness.summary: The bank notification buzzed like a needle jabbing into my chest. The numbers jumped up on the screen, cold and indifferent — mortgage, automatic deduction, balance reduced. I stared at that figure while my mind simultaneously overlaid it with all those "under 35" filters on the job-search apps — each one like a door swinging shut in my face, my hand still raised mid-knock. She took my hand, and I felt the warmth of her palm, but I didn't know how to respond. I heard everything she said — Dad's follow-up appointment, the house, whether the company still had any options — every word landing like a stone on my chest. It's not that I didn't want to speak. I just couldn't get the words out. My whole life, whenever I was backed into that corner, pinned by everyone's eyes, I'd go mute. But she isn't those people. She's the one who stayed. I was afraid to say what I was really thinking — those thoughts are too dark. I don't know where the next job is. I don't know which doors a résumé at forty-eight can even reach. I've even thought about whether I should scrap everything and try a completely different path, do something on my own, even something small. But the moment those words leave my mouth they become weight — weight that lands on her. She's already exhausted enough. She's still holding my hand. The sound of running water has stopped; from upstairs, the faint rustle of the kid turning pages drifts down. I take a slow, deep breath and decide to tell her part of the truth — not all of it, but no longer just silence.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppression (the tearing sensation between a lifelong habit of solitude and the forced exposure of this moment); Fear (the "age 48" label turns every job-listing page into a mirror reflecting a sense of helplessness — the same feeling as being left outside the crowd as a teenager); Shame (being seen in my own vulnerability, like that moment standing at the podium reading from a script); a faint pull toward closeness (the act of her taking my hand pierced through the defenses I usually keep up). Intensity: medium-high — suppression and fear are dominant; the pull toward closeness is a subtle but real crack in the wall.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have spent my entire life using silence and solitude to keep the outside world from breaking me, my instinct right now — even facing her — is to hide the worst of my thoughts. But the second she took my hand I realized: this is not the bleachers beside the basketball court, not the spotlight in the assembly hall. She won't laugh at me. And if I keep going silent, I'm using the same old method to hurt the one person who is still here.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is not letting my own unraveling become someone else's burden — but what I cannot accept is using silence to shut her out too. Because if I do that, this room truly has only me in it, and that kind of loneliness feels exactly the same as standing there all those years ago with everyone watching, unable to say a word.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to speak — to tell her part of the truth, to acknowledge how hard things are, while setting aside for now the most drastic idea (scrapping everything to go independent) and focusing on steadying what's right in front of us. I turned my hand over and held hers back, and said quietly: 'The options really are narrow right now. I haven't figured it out yet — but I don't want to hide it from you. It's harder than I expected.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择开口说出部分真话，承认处境艰难，但暂时搁置最激进的想法（彻底转型独立开发），先稳住眼前。我把她的手反握了一下，低声说：'现在的方向确实很窄，我还没想清楚，但我不想瞒你——比我预想的要难。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择坦白现状，并放弃原有职位与薪资底线。 我先对伴侣说我得现实一点，再把不限年龄和过渡性工作都投出去。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择隐瞒困境并拒绝卖房。我抽出手，关掉屏幕低声说：“还没到卖房那步，我能接外包，复查的事我来安排。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment he finished speaking, the living room suddenly felt like it had shrunk. Not a blowup, not a collapse — more like a quieter, harder-to-escape kind of loosening. 'Redefining boundaries' — those words made my stomach clench, the way it did when I was little and accidentally read a letter I wasn't supposed to see, the way it did all those times I later discovered that relationships could warp, could lose their original shape. I didn't want to argue right away — arguing is too loud, and the phone kept lighting up, jabbing at my attention like a needle — but what I couldn't stand even more was this ambiguity: saying he wasn't leaving, yet wanting to crack the door open just a little. I know I need space; I've always lived by boundaries. But that's for the sake of quiet, not to turn a commitment into something elastic. It became suddenly very clear to me: if I go along with it now and say fine, I'll spend the rest of the time living inside a suspended, low-grade noise. So I had no choice but to press the phone face-down first, look at him, and put this thing to rest once and for all.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Emotional tone — Tightness, disappointment, resistance. The tightness comes from old memories of losing control in a relationship; the disappointment comes from years of stability being rewritten; the resistance comes from my longstanding need for clear boundaries. The intensity is very high — it nearly drowns out every other thought in the moment.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've watched relationships quietly rot in silence, and because vague commitments are what I fear most, my instinct tells me that the moment a boundary gets called 'flexible,' what's left is nothing but endless, grinding erosion. My solitude needs a clear door — not a relationship that can't be named for what it is.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters more to me is a relationship that is clear, quiet, and trustworthy — not one that keeps up the appearance of a home while hollowing out its core. I can accept space for each of us. What I cannot accept is using the language of freedom as cover for making a commitment vague.

pairs.final_decision: Refuse the proposed redefinition of the relationship's boundaries; hold to the original commitment. I press the phone face-down first, then tell him plainly: 'I can't accept this kind of redefinition — we need to talk this through properly.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时搁置手机、直面对话，但以防御性的沉默和反问代替直接回应，试图弄清楚她说的"弹性"究竟意味着什么威胁。我把手机翻过去扣在桌上，开口说：'你说的弹性……是指什么？'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 拒绝关系边界调整，坚持原有承诺。 我先把手机扣下，只对他说我不能接受这种重新定义，我们得把话说清楚。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 拒绝关系弹性，坚守简单承诺。我倒扣手机，直视她：“我不需要弹性，复杂只会让我疲惫，保持简单就好。”

inner_consciousness.summary: Empty. I'd felt it all last night — something had shifted in this place — and waking up this morning only made it more obvious. That silence was a different kind of silence: not the clean quiet of being alone in my studio, but the hollow left behind when something gets pulled out. He walked in, still half-asleep, and said 'let's each just do our own thing,' and I almost nodded, because that's the rhythm I know best, the mode I'm most fluent in. But the moment his voice trailed off, I suddenly thought of myself crouched on the bathroom floor, thought of that line in the email — 'you make people feel no warmth from you' — thought of the sweat in my palms when my therapist asked, 'what if solitude becomes a forced choice rather than a chosen one?' I've always told myself that being alone recharges me. But this emptiness right now isn't one I chose — it's one I've been left inside. He's here too, and he doesn't know what to do either. The two of us are standing in the same hollow. I don't want to pretend today is like any other day, because it isn't — I know it, and he knows it. I'm not good at saying things, but I can at least not run.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Loss with a faint tightening (from the hollow feeling after the goodbye, and the habitual defensiveness around the boundary of solitude); layered with an unusual softness (from years of accumulated self-reflection — those questions about whether solitude is a choice or an escape feel unusually lucid right now). Moderate intensity — no collapse, but a genuine, real wavering.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many moments of pushing people away with 'let's each do our own thing,' only to realize later that they were already gone — so this time, instinctively, I don't want to fill this silence with more silence, even if finding words feels clumsy.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Solitude is real and true for me — but the emptiness right now isn't my solitude; it's a loss we're both facing together. I can't bring myself to pretend everything is normal and paper over something that has genuinely happened.

pairs.final_decision: I choose not to sidestep this shared strangeness — to gently acknowledge that it exists, rather than each of us retreating back into the familiar busyness. I look down at the table and say: "This does feel strange. How about… we have a cup of coffee together first? No need to think about what comes next."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择不回避这个共同的陌生感，轻轻承认它的存在，而不是各自躲回熟悉的忙碌里。我低头看着桌面，开口说："这感觉确实奇怪。要不……先一起喝杯咖啡？不用想之后的事。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择轻度打破陌生感，但保持低刺激和边界。我会说“出去走走吧，不用太久”，然后先去拿外套，和他安静出门。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避情感共建的试探，退回各自熟悉的工作轨道中。我避开了对方的视线，低声说：“还是各忙各的吧，我手头还有个项目要赶进度。”然后站起身，快步走回了自己的书房。

inner_consciousness.summary: The light from my phone screen falls on my face, and that number is still spinning in my head. Abnormal indicator. I stare at the lamp in the study, at their faces in the photo, at the stack of code documentation beside it. Tell them — and then what? They'll come rushing in, the calls won't stop, and their eyes will be full of that suffocating worry, and my whole rhythm will shatter completely. I know that feeling all too well — being surrounded, pinned down by everyone's gaze, all my focus torn to pieces. But if I say nothing… I carry this alone, keep sitting here typing code, pretend nothing happened? That indicator is pressing against my chest like a stone I can't quite name. It's not that I don't understand the risks of delay — I'm not an idiot. But what I'm more afraid of is that loss of control — not the body losing control, but my space, my rhythm, everything I've worked so hard to build, all of it swamped by other people's anxiety. I've carried so much alone. This time… maybe I can too. Get the follow-up done first, figure out what this actually is, then we'll see. It's not deception — it's waiting for confirmation. I don't want to drag them into this while things are still uncertain, turn everyone into a panicked mess. I'll handle it myself first, and once I have the results, I'll decide whether to say anything.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed fear (the health threat has activated a deep-seated fear of losing control); Defensive self-enclosure (the decades-long instinct to use solitude as a shield against outside intrusion); A faint undercurrent of guilt (the family photo is right there in front of me — I know there's a cost to keeping this hidden). Intensity: medium-high, but held in check by habitual suppression — no breakdown, just a heavy, settled weight.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through too many moments of being swamped by other people's worry and scrutiny, my instinct tells me that bearing this alone — before anything is even confirmed — is a way of protecting myself and protecting them too. Even if that logic looks like self-deception to anyone else.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, keeping control of my own rhythm and space matters more than almost anything — being drowned in other people's emotions is something I simply cannot accept. And yet I can't entirely ignore that photo, can't pretend my family doesn't exist. It's just that I need to get things clear first, before I can decide how to bring it up.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to keep it to myself for now, using 'waiting for the results to be confirmed' as my reason to move forward with the follow-up alone, leaving the decision of whether to tell my family suspended. I reply to the doctor's text to confirm the follow-up appointment, then flip my phone face-down on the desk, reopen the code documentation, and tell myself: wait until the results come in.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时隐瞒，以'等待确认结果'为由独自推进复查，将告知家人的决定悬置。回复医生短信确认复查时间，然后把手机屏幕扣在桌上，重新打开代码文档，对自己说：等结果出来再说。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择先立即推进复查，再有限度地告诉家人。我要先联系医生约最快时间，然后对家人说我发现异常、已安排检查，但先别过度猜测。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择暂时隐瞒异常指标，独自承担复查压力以维持日常节奏。我锁灭手机屏幕，把体检短信设为已读，然后深吸一口气，重新将目光投向电脑屏幕上的代码。

inner_consciousness.summary: He's sitting across from me, eyes lit up in a way that isn't right — that brightness isn't joy, it's some kind of fervor that's been ignited by something. I flip through the PPT and with every slide my stomach sinks another inch — tiers, recruiting heads, doubling your money in three months, I know these words, I know this whole logic, it's that kind of thing. But his face… he actually believes it. Flashes of those college nights when we'd talk until dawn — back then he'd argue with me until his voice gave out over the design of a single game level. Now he's sunk eight hundred thousand into this. I want to say something, but before the words are even out he turns on me — jealousy? I'm jealous of him? Something ignited in my chest in that moment, not anger, but a very deep sadness, like watching something in the middle of breaking apart. I'm not good at moments like this, I've never been good at them — every time someone forces me to speak up I freeze, but this time is different, this time it's him. I can't pretend I didn't see it, and I can't just nod and walk away to preserve some surface-level calm. Now he's using this to shut me down — saying I'm jealous, saying I can't recognize an opportunity — but I can't stay quiet just because I'm afraid of losing this friendship. I have to say something. Even if he gets angry, even if he thinks I'm tearing him down, I have to say it.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Grief (a deep sorrow born from watching an old friend fall into a scam, layered over a genuine care that is rarely stirred in a life spent mostly alone); Anger (a recoil at being slapped with the label 'jealous,' drawn from a lifetime's accumulation of being misread and pushed away since childhood); Tension (an instinctive resistance to being forced into a high-conflict social situation — my body is already running hot); but one thread cuts through all the exhaustion — this person is worth opening my mouth for.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have spent my whole life escaping unnecessary social drain, I know better than anyone when it's worth making an exception and speaking up — and right now he has sunk eight hundred thousand into a scam, which is no longer a question of social etiquette; it's the only kind of realness I still care about.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Solitude is my default, but not because I don't care about people — it's because the people I do care about are so few and so precious. I cannot accept the false calm that comes from staying silent. If I don't say something today, by the time he's lost everything, that's when this friendship will truly be over.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to speak up directly and not shy away from the conflict, even at the risk of losing this friendship. I set down the PPT and look up at him, my voice low but steady: 'I'm not jealous of you — I'm scared for you. This is a pyramid scheme. I mean that in the most literal sense — recruiting heads, tiered commissions, fabricated returns. The eight hundred thousand you put in is almost certainly gone, and so is the money of every one of those dozen-odd people you brought in. You can be angry with me, but I can't stand here and watch you keep walking deeper into this.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择直接劝阻，不回避冲突，哪怕冒着失去这段友谊的风险。我放下那份PPT，抬头看着他，声音压得很低但很稳：'我不嫉妒你，我怕你。这是传销，我说的是字面意义上的传销——拉人头、层级分润、虚假收益，你投进去的八十万大概率回不来了，你拉进来的那十几个人也是。你可以生我的气，但我没办法看着你继续往里走。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择继续劝他，但不跟他争输赢。 我会把PPT放下，平静地说我不投，也请你先别再拉人，我是担心你，不是看不起你。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避正面冲突，以明确的个人边界拒绝投资并快速抽身。我把PPT推还给他，低声说：“我不懂这些商业模式，也没精力投，我还有代码没写完，先走了。”

inner_consciousness.summary: That email again. I've stared at it more times than I can count, and every time it's like something grabs hold of me and won't let go. The handwriting in those old notebooks is still floating in front of my eyes — that's what I actually wanted to do, not these go-through-the-motions days I'm living now. The door opened, and I knew it was her; I could feel the worry hiding inside that gentleness of hers, like a thin needle. I took in everything she said — 'our life together'… I know what that means. It means money, it means rhythm, it means dealing with another world all over again — the academy, colleagues, meetings, being watched. That familiar tightening in my stomach is back. But that email — that direction is somewhere I wrote down I wanted to go more than ten years ago, and then I buried it in a drawer, buried it under 'reality.' My current job makes every day feel like walking a road I mapped out long ago, and nothing — nothing — can make me feel truly awake. I don't know whether the pace of academic life will let me breathe, but I know that the way things are now is hollowing me out. She's waiting for me to say something. I'm not good at explaining all this on the spot — the words dissolve before they reach my mouth. But this time, I don't want to push this thing back down again.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed longing (a long-shelved academic passion reignited by old notebooks, caught in a tug-of-war with the inertia of reality); low-grade anxiety (an instinctive wariness toward the unfamiliar academic environment and its social demands); a rare, fragile certainty (this opportunity has touched some long-forgotten, authentic self — one I'm unwilling to abandon again).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I already know what it feels like to bury your own things in a drawer — those old manuscripts, the competitions I never entered, the ideas I talked myself out of — every time I look back it's a dull, aching pain. So this time, even with all the uncertainty ahead, I know instinctively that silence and avoidance will only carry me further down this road that's hollowing me out.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What truly matters to me is being able to find that feeling of being 'alive' in something — even if stability is the price. What I cannot accept is spending another ten years walking a path I stopped believing in long ago, only to pull out an old notebook one night and find that former version of myself still waiting for me.

pairs.final_decision: Choose to honestly express my longing for this academic opportunity — not shying away from the uncertainty — while acknowledging that her concerns are real. Say to her: 'I know this will change a lot of things, and I'm scared too, but I can't keep pushing this down anymore — I want to reply to my advisor and have a real conversation about this opportunity.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择坦诚表达对学术机会的渴望，不回避不确定性，同时承认她的担忧是真实的；对她说：'我知道这会改变很多，我也怕，但我不能再把这个东西压下去了——我想回复导师，认真谈谈这个机会。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择坦诚表达想去试试，但不立刻草率答应入职。我说我确实想认真争取这个岗位，今晚先回导师邮件，约时间进一步了解安排。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避变动，放弃机会以保全独处的安全感。我淡淡地对ta说“我没打算去，现在这样挺好的”，随后关掉邮件页面，委婉回复导师说自己无法离开现有岗位。

inner_consciousness.summary: The night outside the car window is black, the phone screen casting light across my face, that message still sitting there. I've read it a third time, a fourth time. "I suddenly thought of that point you made during the meeting today" — she remembered. She remembered something I said. When was the last time anyone remembered something I said? I can't think of it. Those two hours in the lobby that evening — like a room I thought I'd long forgotten how to open, suddenly pushed ajar. I didn't do anything wrong, we were just talking, and yet this feeling in my chest… Then my partner's message pops up: "Remember to take the kid to tutoring tomorrow." Just that one line. No name, no asking whether I'm tired. Over a decade, and it's just like that. I stare at the two messages, my finger hovering in the air. I know how dangerous this is — not to her, but to myself. I've spent a lifetime keeping the world at arm's length. A studio to myself, late nights alone — that's the real me. But that evening she said "and then what happened?" with a light in her eyes, and something inside me came loose. Like a module that had been commented out in the code for years suddenly getting switched on, starting to run, starting to consume resources. I don't know whether I should reply. What does replying mean? What does not replying mean? The phone buzzes again — my partner's message, cold, like still water. My finger finally moves.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: The tremor of being truly seen (high intensity: that evening's conversation activated a long-suppressed hunger to be understood); Vigilance and self-protection (moderate intensity: solitude is safety, connection is danger — that's carved into my bones); Numbness toward the marriage, with an undercurrent of quiet grief (moderate intensity: over a decade of stillness — not hatred, just emptiness); A contradictory pull (high intensity: wanting to draw closer and wanting to retreat back into my shell at the same time).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since childhood that being truly seen by another person is an extraordinarily rare thing — so when it actually happens, I don't know what to do with it. I only know it's dangerous. And I know it's real.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, solitude and quiet are the only states in which I truly exist. Being disturbed, being needed, being drawn in — all of that is draining. But having someone actually listen to what I say — not just going through the motions, but really listening — that matters more than I thought it did. Enough to frighten me.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to reply to my colleague's message, but keep my wording restrained and maintain distance — not crossing any emotional boundary, responding to the feeling of being seen in a way that stays safe. I write back: "Made it home. That point you mentioned — I've always found it interesting too. That bookstore really is quiet; worth a visit sometime. Good night."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择回复同事消息，但措辞克制、保持距离，不触碰情感边界，用安全的方式回应被看见的感觉。我回复道："到家了。你说的那个观点，我也一直觉得有意思。那家书店确实很安静，有机会可以去看看。晚安。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择回复，但把关系收在安全距离内，不继续暧昧升温。我回“到家了，晚安。书店有机会再说，最近先忙家里和孩子的事。”
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避情感试探，退回安全的孤独中。我给伴侣回了句“知道了”，然后退出微信，没有回复那位同事的消息，将手机倒扣在桌面上。

inner_consciousness.summary: When he slid that proposal across the table, I felt the surface suddenly recede. The sound of his finger tapping the cover — it reminded me of high heels clicking down a hallway many years ago, one tap after another, brooking no argument. The whole room went quiet, and my colleagues' eyes landed on me. I held it together in the moment, but that feeling of being pinned in place — I know it far too well. I've known it since I was six. Now it's just the two of us, and he says 'this isn't a conversation about whether it can be changed.' When I heard that, something tightened in my chest. I built those processes — every single step has a reason for existing. It wasn't laziness; it was deliberate. He's younger than me. He doesn't know. But I also know that pushing back head-on in the moment is pointless — I'm not good at real-time confrontations. Words scramble on their way out, and I end up like that kid standing at the front of the class, voice trembling, letting everyone see exactly what I least want them to see. But I can't say nothing. I can lay out the execution risks clearly, in language he can actually receive — not as a rebuttal, but as information. What I need isn't to win this conversation. It's to have my judgment on the record.

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment Mom finished saying that, my first thought wasn't the amusement park — it was that set of keys on my desk. The little cloth pouch, the teacher handing it to me in front of the whole class, the mimosa just repotted with its roots not yet settled — those words are nailed into my head. I nodded solemnly when I accepted that responsibility. That was a promise I made. I don't like being watched, I don't like being in crowds, but what I really can't stand is that feeling — promising something and then not following through. That hollow would just sit there. The amusement park… indoor climbing, go-karts, my cousin, a whole day of noise and laughter and people looking at me. Just imagining it makes it hard to breathe. It's not that I don't want to go — it's that a place like that would hollow me out completely. But Mom is waiting for my answer, and there's expectation in her eyes. I don't want to disappoint her, and I don't want to explain why I'd rather go to school to water a plant. I'm running the numbers in my head: school is close to home, I could go in the morning, water everything, feed the tadpoles, and maybe still make it to the gathering in the afternoon? But I know myself — once I'm back home, I won't want to go out again. What matters most is that mimosa. I promised. That has to come first.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Calm with an undercurrent of resolve (rooted in a fixation on keeping promises, drawn from the deep satisfaction of having completed tasks on my own many times before); mild resistance (an instinctive aversion to a full day of social gatherings, shaped by countless experiences of being drained by crowds); a small twinge of guilt (not wanting to disappoint Mom, though this guilt is far outweighed by the discomfort of breaking a commitment). Overall intensity is low to moderate — no panic, just a clear-eyed weighing of things.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I made a promise to the teacher — that responsibility was entrusted to me — and if I don't see it through, the unease will linger. And a place like that amusement park, for a whole day, would wring me dry; even if I went, I wouldn't really be there.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, a promise made is a promise kept — that matters far more than where I go to have fun. The cost of being surrounded by crowds all day is one I simply can't accept, even when the person involved is my own cousin.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to take care of the watering and tadpole-feeding at school first, then decide afterward whether to head over to the gathering — but I'm not committing to showing up. I tell Mom: 'Tomorrow I have to go to school first to water the plants. The teacher gave me the keys — the mimosa was just repotted and it can't wait. Once I'm done I'll see if I can make it over, but I might not be in time. Can you let Uncle know for me?'

inner_consciousness.summary: Empty-handed. He's standing in front of me empty-handed. I spent two full weeks on this — alone — researching, writing the script, rehearsing. That model was built piece by piece with my own hands: I wired the LED lights myself, I shaped every single clay planet myself… and now it's all gone. He's trembling, eyes red, and I can feel that his fear is real — his mom is sitting in the third row, and I know what it feels like to be backed into a corner. I've known that feeling my whole life. But he's asking me to lie. He's asking me to stand in front of the homeroom teacher, in front of everyone, and tell a lie I have no way of holding together. I think back to all those moments of standing at the center of a crowd with every eye on me — that suffocating feeling, the heat rising in my face, the way my throat locks up and I can't get a single word out. That's my worst fear, that exact thing: being pinned down by every stare and then saying something that can't hold up, and then being exposed, and then everything getting even worse. The homeroom teacher is already watching. If I lie now, there will be more questions in a moment, more eyes on me, more time trapped in this spiral. I can't do it. I can't put myself in that position and lie. All I want is to end this as fast as possible — even if it looks bad, even if I'm the only one left holding it, at least once I've told the truth it's over. No unraveling later. No longer agony.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Oppression (the fear of being forcibly exposed before everyone's eyes, drawn from countless memories of being pushed onto a stage); Anger and grievance (two weeks of work destroyed in an instant, the accumulated weight of having my effort betrayed); Lucidity within exhaustion (lying means more time under scrutiny — an instinctive drive to find the fastest escape from the spotlight). Intensity: medium-high, with oppression as the dominant force.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments of being trapped between a lie and a roomful of staring eyes, unable to move, I know instinctively that lying only drags out the agony of being watched — telling the truth, however humiliating, at least ends it fastest.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What I find most unbearable is being trapped in a situation that demands constant performance and damage control — that's worse than losing face on the spot. Speaking the truth, even if all I have left is my voice, is the last thing I can still control.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to tell the homeroom teacher the truth about the missing model — I won't go along with the lie, but I also won't actively assign blame to anyone; I simply state the facts and ask to continue with a verbal presentation instead. I say quietly to the homeroom teacher: 'The model was lost on the way to school and didn't make it here — would it be alright if we just walked through the content verbally?'

inner_consciousness.summary: The tiles are ice-cold, and I'm standing in the hallway, the chill seeping up from the soles of my feet. That blue-and-white porcelain vase is shattered — I've known it my whole life, and now it's scattered across the floor, sharp light bouncing off the fragments. Two people saw me at the same moment, and then one hand grabbed me, trembling, fingernails digging into my palm — it hurts. The other hand pulled me behind them, voice low and hard. I'm caught in the middle, both of them waiting for me to speak, just like those times I was pushed to the center of a crowd and every pair of eyes landed on me at once — my throat would lock up completely. It's the same now, except a hundred times heavier. This isn't being asked to sing or skip rope; this is being asked to choose who stays and who goes, and choosing means I'm the one who shattered something even bigger. I don't want to choose. I have no way to choose. That red suitcase is the one we used when we traveled together — I remember the sound of its zipper. I don't want to hear that sound disappear out the front door. But I also don't want to leave the other person alone, the way I was alone waiting outside the kindergarten gate until it got dark. My feet are still ice-cold, my heart is pounding, and I can't think clearly about anything — I only know that I cannot open my mouth, because the moment I do, something will shatter completely, into even smaller pieces than the porcelain on the floor.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Fear (extreme): both caregivers pressing in on me at once has triggered my deepest social terror — being forced to become the center of a decision, forced to speak, forced to choose; Helplessness (extreme): I have no path out, no option that doesn't break something; Numbness (as protection): my mind is going blank as a shield against the unbearable weight of the moment. Countless memories of being pushed to the center of a crowd as a child — being demanded to take a side, to speak up — are all flooding back at once, but this time it is heavier than any of those times, because this is home, the one place I thought was the only safe place.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have lived through too many moments of being forced to speak, forced to take a position — that suffocating feeling — I know instinctively that right now any single word I say will become the force that crushes something. Not something belonging to anyone else, but the last intact piece of my own inner self.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'not being dragged into the middle of it' matters more than 'making the right choice'; what I cannot accept is becoming the reason that breaks someone — whichever person that might be.

pairs.final_decision: I choose silence and freeze in place, refusing to open my mouth or make any choice at all, letting the stillness of my body stand in for words. I sink down into a crouch, bury my face in my knees, and can't make a single sound — only shaking.

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the teacher's hand landed on my shoulder, my entire back went rigid. Forty-some pairs of eyes — I could almost feel them stabbing into me like needles. I only said one thing to my seatmate, just one thing, so how did it come to this. My mind flashed back to that family gathering, when they pushed me into the center of the living room to draw something, and I ended up escaping to the bedroom and sliding down against the door to sit on the floor; and that time at the flag-raising platform, my voice so small it was barely a whisper, my legs so weak they felt like cotton. Every time I get pushed to the center, it's this same feeling — like a layer of skin has been peeled off me. I heard what the teacher said: speech competition, representing the class, "very creative" — those words kept echoing in my ears, but all I wanted was to shrink back, back into that tiny space that had just been mine and my seatmate's. I know the idea is right — the seesaw principle, I'd thought it through. But thinking something through is one thing; standing up and saying it in front of everyone is another thing entirely. That's not something I do. I don't want to stand up, I don't want all those eyes on me, I don't want to enter some competition, I don't want to become anyone's idea of a "little scientist." That feeling of being watched — it would crush me.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Panic (a stress response triggered by being suddenly thrust into the spotlight, compounded by countless layered memories of being forced into the center of attention); Resistance (an instinctive, high-intensity aversion to public display); Mild sense of grievance (a good idea has been "requisitioned" — it was only ever meant to be said in private)
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because every time I've been pushed into the center of everyone's gaze, it has been an ordeal of exhaustion and shame — my body has memorized that feeling — and so I know instinctively: no matter how good the idea is, the act of standing up and speaking is itself a cost I cannot bear.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, thinking and creating are things that belong to myself alone — they're not performances put on for others. Being stared at, being judged, being pushed onto a stage — that is something I cannot accept, even if what I'd get in return is praise.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to quietly decline standing up to explain, and respond to the suggestion about the speech competition with a vague, evasive shake of my head. I lower my gaze and say in a very small voice: "I… I can't really explain it well — teacher, you go ahead and tell them."

inner_consciousness.summary: The envelope is in my hands, the edges of the paper a little creased, and I'm staring at those words — examination records, follow-up recommendations — something tightening inside my head. For weeks I thought it was work stress, his moods, me not being sensitive enough. Turns out it wasn't any of that. The door opened, he came in, and in that split second something flickered across his face — I saw it, even though he thought I hadn't. 'It's nothing serious' — the way that line landed was wrong, too light, light in the way things are when they're covering up something heavy. I think about all those late nights I'd find him sitting there, staring into nothing, and I told myself he was just tired, I never asked, because asking isn't something I'm good at either. But this is different now. This isn't just his business — this is his body, and it's our business. He reached out to take the envelope back, the gesture hesitant, his eyes drifting away — I know that move, I've done it myself, it's the move you make when you're trying to push something far away. I don't want to corner him; I've never liked being cornered myself. But what I can stand even less is being kept in the dark, being treated like someone who doesn't need to know. That feeling is too familiar, too lonely — I am not going to sit by the door again, waiting for an answer that may never come.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety and suppression (born from the collision between a long-held habit of solitude and being suddenly forced to confront a crack in the relationship); a sense of being shut out (being treated by a partner as someone who doesn't need to know the truth, triggering a deep, old memory of loneliness); a low-intensity resolve (not anger — more a quiet, accumulated determination, a refusal to keep giving ground).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because the hardest thing in my life has never been fighting — it's been being left out, being treated as someone who doesn't need to know. That kind of loneliness drains me more than any amount of noise ever could. So this time, even though speaking up is hard, I can't just go along with him, let him put the envelope away, and pretend nothing happened.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: I can live with quiet. I can live with distance. But I cannot live with being kept in the dark about the things that matter most — that isn't protecting me, that's pushing me further away. His body and his fears are things I deserve to know about, because we are people who share the same space.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to make clear that I've seen the report and ask my partner to be honest with me about what's going on — I won't follow his lead into avoidance. I set the envelope down gently on the table, didn't hand it back to him, and said quietly: 'I saw it. Can you tell me what's going on?'

inner_consciousness.summary: He came in carrying the milk, eyes bright enough to hurt. My hand was still resting on the table, the words from that note still searing through my mind like a branding iron — "the answers passed along pretty smoothly, didn't they." He said "hard work pays off," said he'd be giving a speech at the assembly, said he wanted to try out for the competition… every word felt like more weight pressing down on something that had already cracked. I looked at his face — that genuine, completely unguarded happiness — and my stomach turned. I thought about the note. I thought about next Monday's award ceremony. I thought about the image of him standing up there saying "hard work pays off" while I sat in the audience knowing the truth. No. But I also thought about what that face would look like if I said something right now — I know that feeling far too well, the feeling of being exposed in front of everyone, that burning shame, the weak knees, the desperate wish to disappear. Not now. Not like this, not charging in like that. But letting him go up and accept that award, letting him stand there and say those things… I took a sip of the milk. Warm. Slightly off. I need to hold this down for now — but I can't just let it go. Tonight, once he's calmed down, I have to talk to him. Just the two of us, not in anger — I need him to say it himself.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heaviness and suppression (from the shock the note brought and the disappointment in my child's behavior); restrained anxiety (from an instinctive dread of public humiliation, and from anticipating what is about to happen at next week's award ceremony); and a quiet, cold resolve (the habit of solitude means I won't act impulsively at the peak of my emotions). Intensity is moderate-to-high, but held firmly in check by the self-suppression that comes naturally to an introvert.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I know all too well what it feels like to be exposed in front of everyone — that kind of shame crushes a person, it doesn't actually help them understand where they went wrong. So I'm not going to charge over and shatter everything at the moment he's happiest. I'll wait until it's just the two of us, and let this be worked through in private.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters to me is that he genuinely understands where he went wrong — not that he breaks down under humiliation. What I cannot accept is letting him stand on that stage carrying a lie. And I equally cannot accept just saying nothing and letting it all pass.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hold back for now, not to expose him in this moment — but inwardly I've already decided that tonight I will sit down with him privately and talk this through. I take the milk from him and say quietly: "We can talk about the speech later. Go wash up first — tonight, let's have a real conversation."

inner_consciousness.summary: After the old man upstairs finished saying all that, I stood in the doorway and my mind went blank for a second. Then that familiar feeling came — being pushed forward by other people, being used as a shield. I spent two months going through those numbers line by line, not to be some kind of hero, but because I just can't stomach that kind of blatant lie staring me in the face. And now what they're saying is: you're young, you can take the hit, so you go. I think back to all those times — being shoved up to the podium in class, being dragged out to speak at exhibitions — every single time it was someone else deciding I 'should be able to handle it,' then pushing me into a place I never wanted to be. That feeling of being used isn't any different from being bullied. They say they'll help behind the scenes, but they won't put their names on the letter — that's not support, that's letting me carry all the risk alone while they stand back and watch. My wife's face went pale. I saw it. But those two million are real, those forged contracts are real, and if I don't report it, this whole thing just rots where it sits. It's not that I'm not scared — I just can't stand this feeling of being played for a fool even more. One name, just one name — but I'm going to see this through.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anger (triggered by a sense of being used, compounded by layered memories of being pushed into unwanted social situations over the years) + Isolation (no one truly standing beside him; the neighbors' retreat confirms his distrust of social bonds) + Calm resolve (when faced with a clear moral judgment, his introversion actually makes him more capable of acting independently — he doesn't need anyone else's validation). Intensity: medium-high — anger is the driving force, isolation is the undertone, and resolve is where it all lands.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: I've been pushed out as a target too many times — I know exactly what that feels like. But this time is different. No one forced me into this; I'm the one who dug it up, this is my own matter. So I won't back down just because no one's with me. If anything, going alone feels cleaner.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is this: is what I'm doing true, and does my judgment hold up? What I cannot accept is knowing something is right and then shrinking back just because no one's standing beside me. That would make me feel more disgusted with myself than anything else.

pairs.final_decision: He chooses to file a real-name report in his own name alone, refusing to let the neighbors' retreat or his wife's pleas change his position — but he won't push anyone to join him either. He says calmly to the old man upstairs: 'I understand your difficulties. I won't press anyone on the joint letter — but I'll go to the Housing and Construction Bureau myself. I've already got the materials ready.'

inner_consciousness.summary: The teacher's gaze landed on me, and the whole class turned to look — that feeling is all too familiar, like needles, like being held over an open flame. I kept my eyes down on the dense notes in my notebook, the arguments from that book still printed clearly in my mind. I knew there was something wrong with what the teacher said — genuinely wrong. But… if I speak up, what will he think of me? What will my classmates think? The laughter, the looks that say 'there you go interrupting again,' the taste of being publicly shot down — it all came rushing back at once. It's not that I don't want to tell the truth; it's that I know all too well what happens after I do — the teacher might refute me on the spot, my classmates might think I'm showing off, and just like that, the harmony of the whole classroom gets shattered by me alone. But he called on me by name. I can't pretend I didn't hear. My palms are starting to sweat. Maybe… maybe there's a way to have it both ways? Instead of saying 'you got that wrong,' I could frame what I read as a 'question' — that way I'm not contradicting him, just asking for clarification, and no one has to feel embarrassed. Yes, that's it — keep it gentle, leave room for everyone, don't break the atmosphere in here, and don't swallow all those genuine doubts back down either.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (the exposure of being called on by name, triggering memories of countless moments of being watched and dismissed in class); Conflict (knowing clearly that the historical account is flawed, yet instinctively wanting to preserve harmony); Mild self-suppression (wrapping the impulse to 'directly point out the error' inside the form of 'humbly asking for guidance'). Overall intensity: moderate-to-high, with anxiety as the dominant note.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments where speaking the truth only ended up damaging a relationship, I've come to instinctively believe that even when I disagree, I have to find a way to say it that everyone can accept — rather than confronting anyone head-on.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, not letting this classroom become awkward because of something I said matters more than proving I'm right; but staying completely silent and swallowing every genuine question I have is also something I can't accept — that would feel like a betrayal of the book I read, of the careful notes I took.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stand up and, framing it as 'seeking guidance' rather than 'making a correction,' gently raise the different account I came across in my outside reading, packaging the discrepancy as a question. I stand up and say quietly: 'Teacher, I was reading a book last week that cited some primary archival sources, and the way it described this period of history differs a little from what you've explained. I'm not entirely sure whether I've misunderstood something on my end — could you help clarify it for me?'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择站起来，以"请教"而非"纠正"的姿态，温和地提出自己从课外阅读中看到的不同说法，把分歧包装成疑问。我站起来，轻声说："老师，我上周读到一本书，里面引用了一些原始档案，对这段史实的描述和您讲的有些出入，我不太确定是不是自己理解有误，可以请您帮我解释一下吗？"
