decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避冲突，掩饰自己的真实想法。我迅速合上笔记本站起来，低着头小声说：“对不起老师，我没有问题，我只是刚刚在补抄前面的笔记没跟上。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the note unfolded, my heart lurched straight up into my throat — that person whose eyes curve into crescents when they smile, all those words I'd buried in my diary, suddenly becoming real in a single instant. My fingers were trembling, heat spreading from the tips of my ears all the way down my neck. But right in that moment when my heartbeat was a total mess, the voice on my left leaned over — "Let me see" — like a bucket of cold water dumped over my head. My mind filled instantly with last week, behind the equipment room after P.E., her face flushed as she told me. She likes the same person. I smiled and patted her on the shoulder and didn't say a word. Back then I thought I could keep not saying anything, forever — but now this note is in my hands, and her eyes are drifting over here. If she sees it, that name with the crescent-moon eyes, that little sunshine… I don't know what would happen, but I know that feeling — someone hurting because of me, a friendship cracking because of me, that's worse than anything. I'd rather crush this note in my fist than see that expression cross her face. The after-school plan — I want to go, I really do — but I'm even more afraid that if I go, something will exist afterward that can never find its way back.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Excitement and panic intertwined (the heartbeat the note sparked is instantly smothered by my friend leaning in); Guilt and self-suppression (her confession last week surges up in my memory, turning my own feelings into a kind of 'betrayal'); Overall intensity is high, with guilt and the impulse to protect the relationship as the dominant forces.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've seen too many times the kind of cracks left behind when I didn't handle something right — I know that breaking a relationship is far more painful than just swallowing my own loss; my friend spoke up first about her feelings, and I can't let her be blindsided by me moving ahead without her knowing. That would make me feel like a fraud.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Keeping the friendship whole matters more than acting on my own feelings. I can push this longing down — but I cannot accept my friend being hurt because of me, or being kept in the dark because of me.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to hide what the note says and hold off on the after-school plan for now, putting my friend's feelings ahead of my own. I fold the note casually and tuck it back into my textbook, then smile over at her and say: "It's nothing — someone from the next class asking to borrow my notes. Keep working on your homework."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择隐瞒纸条内容、暂时不赴约，把保护朋友的感受放在自己的心动之前。我把纸条随手折起来塞回课本，笑着对她说："没什么，隔壁班的同学问我借笔记的，你继续写作业吧。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择先隐瞒纸条内容，并暂时不立刻答应赴约。我把纸条收进课本，只说是问作业，放学后先去找朋友谈清楚再决定去不去。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择掩盖真相，牺牲自己的感情以维持友谊。我慌乱地把纸条揉进手心塞进课本底：“没什么，就是别人乱写的恶作剧！”放学后我拉着好朋友直接回家，没有去赴约。

inner_consciousness.summary: When those words jumped out at me from the screenshot someone posted — "She just thinks way too highly of herself, so annoying, we never should've let her hang out with us" — my hands were shaking. She said that. Her. The same person who calls me to the cafeteria every single day. And now she's sitting right next to me, tossing her bag onto the desk, asking me why my eyes are red, then turning away to whisper and laugh with someone else. That laughter hits like needles. My chest is so tight I can barely breathe. I want to confront her — the words are right there in my throat — but then I think about every single time I've tried to say something, and how it always ends the same way: me standing there not knowing what to do, them laughing and drifting off, and only me left hurting. I don't even know for sure if she's seen that screenshot. I'm not certain. I don't dare be certain. What if I storm over and confront her and she denies it? What if they laugh even harder? What if this completely destroys everything? But I can't keep swallowing this either — I'm barely holding on. I don't want her to see me cry. I don't want her to think I really do "think too highly of myself." All I can do is push this feeling back down, wait a little longer, maybe there'll still be a chance to talk it through properly… maybe.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Hurt and suppression (stemming from being stabbed in the back by the friend I trusted most, and from the reality of being excluded); Fear (of a confrontation that would destroy the relationship entirely, of being mocked, of being left alone); Intensity is high, but forcibly held beneath a surface of calm.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through this too many times — every time I try to stand up and say something, I end up hurting even more on my own. So my gut tells me that confronting her directly right now will only make things worse, and give them more reason to laugh at me for "thinking too highly of myself."
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: The hardest thing for me to accept isn't being left out — it's the relationship shattering completely, with no way back. Even if this friendship is already bleeding out, I still want to hold onto whatever tiny possibility remains.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to push down the urge to confront her, getting through this moment with silence and avoidance, not letting her see that I already know about the screenshot. I lower my head, force the tears back, and say quietly, "I'm fine, probably just didn't sleep well," then look away and pretend to flip through my textbook.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时压下质问的冲动，用沉默和回避来撑过这一刻，不让对方看出自己已经知道了截图的事。低下头，把眼泪憋回去，轻声说了句"没事，可能没睡好"，然后把视线移开，假装在翻课本。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择私下摊开说，但不当众闹开。我等她落单时轻声问她：我看到小群和截图了，你直接告诉我，是不是你们真的不想和我一起了。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择默默忍耐，回避冲突，试图通过妥协来维持表面关系。我勉强挤出一个比哭还难看的笑，低声说：“没事，就是刚才沙子迷了眼睛……你们忙你们的，我没多想。”然后低下头假装看书。

inner_consciousness.summary: I saw it. The moment she rolled up her sleeve — those red marks, some deep, some faint, a few still fresh — something seized my heart like a fist, and my breath stopped for a beat. Her eyes had that look of relief mixed with fear; I know that look, it's the look of someone who's finally moved the boulder off their chest but is terrified it might crush someone else. Then she grabbed my wrist and said I absolutely could not tell anyone — said if I did, we were done, right here, right now. I could feel her knuckles going white where she gripped me, and two forces were tearing at me from the inside — I wanted to promise her, I so desperately wanted her to feel safe, I was so terrified of losing her trust, and that line "you're the only one I trust" slid like a key into the softest part of me. But those marks… those fresh marks… something in my head was screaming: this isn't a lost eraser, this isn't a fight at rehearsal, she is hurting herself just to breathe. If I promise her, she might keep doing it — what if next time it's deeper? If I don't promise, she says we're done — but if I do promise and something happens to her, then we really are done, forever done. The wind was pouring in through the rooftop door, my palms were soaked, and I knew I couldn't just be the person who keeps the secret — but I also couldn't let her feel like I'd betrayed her.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Fear (the moment I saw the marks, a deep, crushing sense of crisis came down on me — not ordinary worry, but a real, visceral terror of losing her); Torn apart (the violent pull between keeping her trust and needing to get help — harder to bear than any falling-out I've ever had to navigate); Heartache (when she said "I can't stop myself," what I heard was how long she'd been carrying this alone)
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since I was little that breaking harmony is harder to stomach than taking a loss — but this time, instinctively, I felt there was something even harder to stomach than a broken relationship: her actually getting hurt while I did nothing. I can't trade the friendship for the possibility of losing her as a person.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me is that she is still here — relationships can be repaired, but if she's gone, nothing else matters. What I cannot accept is this: I promise to keep the secret, and then one day something worse happens to her, and I'm the only one who knew and did nothing.

pairs.final_decision: I didn't promise to keep the secret on the spot. Instead I chose to steady her emotions first — neither exposing her nor flatly refusing — while inside I had already decided I had to find an adult who could help her. I held her hand, my voice trembling, and said: "I'm not going to go around telling people — but I need you to help me figure this out together, how we can get you to a place where you don't have to do this anymore. I can't carry this by myself. I need someone to help both of us."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 没有当场答应保密，而是选择先稳住她的情绪、不立刻揭穿或拒绝，同时内心已决定必须找到可以帮她的大人。我握住她的手，声音发抖地说："我不会到处说的，但我需要你帮我想想，我们怎么能让你不用再这样——我一个人扛不住这件事，我需要有人帮咱们两个。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 不彻底答应保密，先安抚她并坚持找大人帮忙。我握住她的手说我不会丢下你，然后劝她和我一起去找老师或家长。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择妥协并承诺保密，独自承担情绪重压以维护友谊。我反握住ta发抖的手，眼眶发酸地用力点头：“我答应你，我绝对不跟任何人说，我会一直陪着你。”

inner_consciousness.summary: In that moment my stomach dropped like someone had reached in and grabbed it — six months, every late night hunched over that desk, every version of those documents revised until my eyes burned, all of it sitting inside that cracked shell. I stood there watching him slap the money down on the coffee table, listening to him say "what's the big deal," and a ringing, buzzing sound swallowed everything else. I knew that money couldn't buy back a single thing — no backups, no source files, just gone. But my mom's voice message was still buzzing on my phone: "be a good host, don't make them feel like we're being stingy" — that line hit me like a needle, and I felt myself wedged between two slabs of stone. My cousin was still craning his neck and sticking out his tongue, clutching that screwdriver, his face completely blank. I wanted to say something, but there was something lodged in my throat — like every single moment from childhood onward when I wanted to say "it wasn't my fault" and couldn't, all of them stacking on top of each other and bearing down at once. Uncle's expression wasn't malicious; he genuinely just didn't think it was a big deal — and somehow that made it worse, a kind of hurt that's harder to name than anger. I didn't want today to turn into an ugly scene, didn't want to embarrass my mom, but I also knew that if I took that money right now, there would be nothing left of my six months — nothing at all.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Suppressed grief (a deep sense of loss triggered by six months of work being destroyed in an instant); Wounded indignation (being brushed off so casually, reopening the old wound of "swallowing the hurt and still having to apologize" that goes all the way back to childhood); A tearing, impossible dilemma (an intense, high-stakes tug-of-war between the instinct to preserve family harmony and the inability to accept being dismissed this way)
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I learned early on that losing my temper in family situations only embarrasses my mom and isolates me further, my instinct is to push the feeling down and find some outlet that "everyone can live with" — but this time that hard drive wasn't just an object, it was six months of my life, and I can't pretend a few hundred-yuan bills make any of that not matter.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Ruining the mood of today's dinner and embarrassing my mom in front of the relatives — that's something I find very hard to accept; but taking that money, nodding along and saying "it's fine, it's fine," letting the whole thing get buried under four words like "what's the big deal" — that feels even more like a betrayal of those six months, and that is something I simply cannot swallow, no matter what.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to decline the money gently but clearly, to speak the true weight of what was lost, without letting it escalate into a direct confrontation. I push the stack of bills back toward him, my voice slightly unsteady but without tears, and say: 'Uncle, I can't take the money — that hard drive had six months of my work on it, and there's no replacing it. I just need a little time to get through this.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择温和但清晰地拒绝那笔钱，说出损失的真实重量，但不升级为正面冲突。我把那叠钱推回去，声音有些发抖但没有哭，说：'大伯，钱我不能收，这块硬盘里有我做了半年的东西，买不回来的，我需要一点时间缓一缓。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择克制但明确地拒绝息事宁人。 我把钱推回去，说这不是赔几百块的事，我现在很难受，硬盘里的东西很重要，我需要你们先别再轻描淡写。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择压抑真实愤怒，以自我牺牲的方式平息潜在冲突。我会勉强挤出一个僵硬的笑，把钱推回给大伯：“没事大伯，小孩子调皮，钱您收回去吧，我自己想办法弄就行。”然后默默蹲下捡起硬盘碎片。

inner_consciousness.summary: His voice was so low, his eyes full of a desperate plea for help — something seized my chest all at once. I know him; we've always gotten along well, and he must have been backed into a corner to come to me like this. But my hand was still resting on that data, those numbers fluctuating with a regularity that was just too perfect… I know what that means. The email reminder popped up again — confirmation due tonight, report due first thing tomorrow morning. If I sign off on it, those wrong numbers become the basis for decisions — maybe money, maybe direction, maybe something that affects a lot of people. I kept turning it over and over in my mind: is there any way to have it both ways — could I give him a chance to go to his supervisor himself, let him come forward on his own, so I wouldn't have to be the one who exposed him… but the deadline is tonight, and there's no way he'd make it in time. I thought back to all those years of being caught in the middle, all those times I tried to make things easier for everyone and ended up hurting the most myself. But this time is different — this isn't just about relationships, this is something that will genuinely go wrong. I can't sign my name to this data, I can't pretend I didn't see anything. My hands are shaking, but I know I have to tell him — I can't help him cover it up, but I can stay by his side and figure out together how to face this.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anguish and moral conflict (a deep, aching sympathy for my colleague — an unwillingness to hurt him — tangled with an inner compulsion I cannot betray, intensity very high); Fear (afraid of fracturing the relationship, afraid of becoming the person who causes someone else pain, rooted in decades of fixation on harmony and the accumulated trauma of countless failed attempts at mediation); A heavy sense of responsibility (the realization that the consequences of this situation reach far beyond interpersonal dynamics and cannot be dissolved through mediation).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because my whole life I have swallowed my own feelings to avoid hurting anyone — but this time, falsifying the data will cause real harm to far more people who aren't even in this room. Breaking the harmony is painful, but putting my name on a lie is something I find even more impossible to accept.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: The fracture in this relationship cuts me like a knife, but covering up a mistake that will cause genuine harm with a false peace is something I simply cannot do, no matter what; I can bear being resented, but I cannot bear being the person who let the wrong thing happen.

pairs.final_decision: I choose not to sign off confirming the data as valid, but rather than going directly over my colleague's head to report it to his supervisor, I give him one last chance to come forward on his own. I say to him: 'I can't sign off on this data. Tonight I'm going to reply to the system honestly and say there's a problem with the data that needs to be reviewed — you still have time. Before I submit that, go tell your supervisor yourself. I'll go with you.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择不签字确认数据有效，但不直接越过同事向上级举报，而是给同事最后一次主动承认的机会。我对他说：'我没办法在这份数据上签字，今晚我会如实回复系统数据存在问题需要核查——你现在还有时间，在我提交之前，你自己去跟上级说，我可以陪你去。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择不签字，并向上级如实反映，同时尽量先帮同事争取补救空间。我会对他说我不能帮你瞒，但我愿意现在一起说明情况、重做数据。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview:
选择替同事隐瞒造假，将风险和补救工作揽到自己身上。
“你先别慌，我不举报你。今晚我们一起通宵，我帮你把真实数据重新算出来补上，我先去系统里点确认。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment he closed that folder and set it at the corner of the desk, I felt my stomach drop. That gesture was all too familiar — like the times a teacher cut off my explanations years ago, like all those moments when my voice got swallowed up by something louder. I heard every word he said, and each one carried real weight; those certificates of honor are right there on the wall, and I know how long he's walked this road. But I also know that the data I saw is real, that those failure cases aren't something I fabricated. I don't want to damage this relationship — I really don't — he's the one who signs off, he's someone I respect, but if I put this away right now, those risks won't disappear just because I stayed quiet. There's a voice in my head saying, just let it go, get through this milestone first — but something on the other side is pushing back — I'm not that child who stepped away from the swing set anymore, I'm not the person who scrubbed the floor until they cried. I don't want to trade compromise for a surface-level calm and then spend the small hours of the night chewing over that indescribable sense of grievance, alone. But I also don't want to back him into an opposing corner, don't want this to turn into a fight about who wins and who loses. Maybe there's a third path — I can hold onto the evidence without forcing him to change his position on the spot, and just keep the door to this conversation open.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety laced with a thread of unwillingness — the quiet accumulation of every concession made over the years; Fear of rupturing the relationship — the weight of my advisor's signing authority and the relationship itself both pressing down at once; And a clarity of resolve that is sharper than before — because this time there is real evidence behind me, and I'm not making a fuss over nothing.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through too many moments of swallowing my own judgment and regretting it alone in the middle of the night, this time something in me instinctively knows: going completely silent will leave me feeling worse at the deepest level, but a head-on collision will also shut down the only channel of communication we have — I need to find a way to hold onto the evidence without making him feel entirely dismissed.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'the relationship breaking apart' is harder to accept than 'taking a personal loss' — but this time, 'knowing there's a risk and pretending not to see it' is starting to feel equally unacceptable. I can't accept a false harmony bought with silence, and I can't accept turning this relationship into a confrontation.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to propose a middle path — neither withdrawing the materials nor insisting on an immediate change of course — and instead ask that, while the existing plan moves forward, a small-scale parallel verification be kept in place. I speak up: 'I hear what you're saying, and I understand the pressure of the deadline. I'm not trying to overturn the whole direction — I just want to ask: would it be possible, while we push ahead with the main plan as it stands, to allow me to keep that parallel process as an additional verification? If the data shows no difference in the end, I will fully accept your judgment; but if a discrepancy does appear, we'll have a documented basis to work from. That's also a form of protection for your framework.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择提出折中路径，既不收回材料、也不坚持要求立即改弦，而是请求在现有方案推进的同时保留一个小规模对照验证的空间。我开口说：'老师，我听到您说的了，节点的压力我也清楚。我不是要推翻整个方向——我想请问，能不能在按既定方案推进主体的同时，允许我把那个对照流程作为一个附加验证保留下来？如果最后数据没有差异，我完全接受您的判断；但如果真的出现偏差，我们也有据可查。这样对您的体系也是一种保护。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择提出折中路径，先不正面硬顶也不完全照单全收。我会说我尊重他的判断，但想申请保留主线的同时做一个小规模并行验证。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择全面妥协并安抚导师情绪，放弃新路线。我连忙把说明收回包里：“对不起老师，是我考虑不周，我马上按您的既定方案把材料补齐。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The hallway lights flickered on and off, and the moment that cigarette was brought close to my face, my whole body went rigid — like I'd been frozen solid. My suitcase was already outside the door, clothes scattered all over the floor — this was real, not a nightmare. My first reaction wasn't anger; it was that familiar feeling I've known since childhood, the one where my stomach gets squeezed into a fist, like the time I stood on the school playground while someone pointed at me and shouted 'You pushed him,' and not a single person spoke up for me. Two guys behind him, the crack of a baseball bat against the floor, my legs shaking. I tried to find some words to defuse the situation — but this wasn't a classmate argument, wasn't a budget dispute; this man had no interest in being mediated. What he wanted was for me to leave. The deposit — only I know how hard it was to scrape that money together. I can't just walk away. But he said he knows people at the police station, and those two men took a step closer… My mouth started forming words, trying to say 'we can talk this through,' but something inside me spoke up for the first time with cold clarity: there is no room to talk here. He's not arguing — he's threatening. My hands are trembling, but I know that if I walk out that door tonight just like this, I'll have nothing left. I need time. I need to get away from this doorway first, then make a call — not to the police, but to find someone, someone who can actually help me.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Fear (physical, immediate — coming from the direct bodily threat in front of me); Hurt and indignation (the injustice of the deposit, of being driven out, stirring up countless memories of being crushed by the more powerful side with no chance to defend myself); Brief clarity and despair (realizing that my instinct to mediate has failed me here — the other party simply won't allow any space for communication). Intensity is extremely high; this is one of the rare moments when my body registers genuine, real danger.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because every single time I've stepped up to mediate, from childhood on, it has only brought me greater harm — I know that holding my ground in the face of violence will only put me in more danger. But because I also know that walking away in silence means giving up everything, I can't fall apart right now. I have to get myself safely away from this doorway first, and then figure out what to do next.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Disrupting harmony has always felt harder to bear than taking a personal loss — but in this moment I realize there is one thing even harder to bear than disrupting harmony: being violently driven out of my own home by someone who won't even give me the chance to open my mouth. That deposit is mine. I can't just surrender like this.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to comply for now in order to get out of immediate physical danger, but inwardly I resolve not to give up on recovering the deposit — the moment I leave, I'll contact someone who can help me (a tenant rights hotline, legal aid, or a friend). I lower my head and say, voice trembling but as steady as I can manage: 'Fine, I'll go for tonight — but we're not done talking about the deposit. I'll be handling this through the proper channels.' Then I bend down and start gathering the clothes from the floor, and I stop meeting his eyes.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时顺从以脱离眼前的人身危险，但内心决定不放弃追回押金，离开后立刻联系能帮助自己的人（如租房维权热线、法律援助或朋友）。我低下头，声音发抖但尽量平稳地说：'好，我今晚先走，但押金的事我们还没说完，我会按正规渠道来处理的。'然后弯腰开始收拾地上的衣物，不再与他对视。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择先顺从撤离、暂不当面对抗，但不放弃押金和求助。第一时间低声说我先收拾东西离开，同时悄悄用手机联系朋友并准备报警求助。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避冲突、放弃押金并服从驱逐。我咽下所有解释的话，低头默默把散落的衣物塞进行李箱，连夜离开这栋楼。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment he took my hand, my heart lurched — not from love, but from panic. All those days we'd shared came flooding back at once: the laughter, the arguments, the separate confusion of going our own ways. But I have someone now — someone real, someone steady, someone good. My ex said he'd changed, said he'd been waiting for me, and those words landed like a thorn — not in my heart, but in my conscience. I don't want to hurt anyone. I don't want my ex to feel like those two years meant nothing, and I don't want to be dishonest with the person I'm with now. But if I say something about today, will it shatter the warmth we've spent three months carefully building? I know exactly how that plays out — both sides get hurt, and the person in the middle becomes the villain. I can't do that. But I can't lie either. There's something pressing down on my chest, and I can hear myself about to speak — I have to be clear, I have to be gentle but clear, I can't give him hope, and I can't pretend today never happened. As for my current partner… I have to tell him. Not telling him would be the real betrayal.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Conflict and tenderness (the pull of old feelings stirred something genuinely soft in me, but the fact that I have someone now turned that softness into guilt almost instantly); Anxiety (afraid of hurting either of them, afraid of unraveling a hard-won sense of calm); A quiet, underlying resolve (too many times before I've hedged to avoid causing pain, and it only ended up hurting everyone more — this time I have to be clear).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've seen too many times how 'keeping the peace' ends up making everyone more miserable in the end — I know that vague, well-meaning ambiguity cuts deeper than a direct word; so no matter how tangled I feel inside, I have to be clear with my ex and honest with my current partner, and I can't leave either relationship hanging in the air.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: The hardest thing for me to live with isn't conflict itself — it's using silence or vagueness to deceive someone who trusts me. Honesty within a relationship matters more than peace within a relationship, even if honesty brings a moment of pain.

pairs.final_decision: Choose to turn my ex down — gently but without ambiguity — and decide to tell my current partner about the encounter that same day. To my ex, I say: 'I never pretended during those two years, but I'm in a new relationship now, and I can't give you a reason to keep waiting — that wouldn't be fair to you, and it wouldn't be fair to him. I genuinely hope you find happiness, but there's no going back for us.' Then, when I get home, I bring it up myself and tell my current partner everything that happened today.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择温柔但明确地拒绝前任，同时决定当天告诉现任这次见面的经过。对前任说：'这两年我没有假装过，但我现在有了新的感情，我不能给你等待的理由——对你不公平，对他也不公平。我希望你好，但我们回不去了。'回家后，主动把今天的事告诉现任。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择温和但明确地拒绝前任，并对现任坦白。 我对前任说我不会重新开始，回去后我会把见面和他说的话告诉现任。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择温和安抚前任，对现任隐瞒表白。我轻轻抽出手低声说：“你别这样，我们都先冷静下。”回家后对现任只字未提。

inner_consciousness.summary: I stared at that project plan, and my heart just sank — isn't this exactly who I was back then? That fire, that certainty, that complete unawareness of how deep the water really goes. I remember that afternoon when the whole project got torn down and rebuilt from scratch — nobody on the team said a word, the air was thick with a silence that had weight to it, and that feeling is still lodged somewhere inside me, unresolved. I want to say something, and yet I'm afraid — afraid of coming across as a know-it-all, afraid she'll think I'm raining on her parade, afraid of puncturing that excitement. But if I say nothing, and she really does walk straight into that trap… My phone buzzed again — a private message from a colleague, saying they sense a problem but don't know how to bring it up. Even someone else can see it. My fingers hovered over the comment box, two sides of me pulling in opposite directions — say something, or stay quiet? If I speak up, will she feel like she's being shot down? If I don't, it's like watching someone walk into a ditch with my eyes wide open. What I'm most afraid of isn't saying the wrong thing — it's the relationship souring afterward, it's her thinking I don't trust her. But that memory of tearing everything down and starting over is so vivid, that feeling of morale collapsing — I don't want her to go through that too. Maybe… maybe there's a way to do this that doesn't feel like criticism?
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Conflicted anxiety (wanting to help but afraid of overstepping, driven by a failure from twenty years ago); Empathetic tenderness (seeing my younger self in her, feeling an ache I can't quite name); Mild self-doubt (would speaking up actually do any good, or would it just make me feel better). The emotional intensity is moderate — not explosive, more like something pressing against my chest that won't disperse.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've lived through that kind of project collapse firsthand, my instinct is to pull her back from the edge — but twenty years of navigating relationships has also taught me that unsolicited advice often cuts deeper than silence; those two forces tore at me, and in the end I settled on finding the gentlest, least "lecture-y" way to quietly pass something along.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Damaging the warmth of a relationship hurts me more than letting someone take the long way around — but staying silent and saying nothing feels like a betrayal of everything that memory taught me. I can live with her ultimately not taking my advice; I can't live with not even having the courage to open my mouth.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to speak up proactively, with a gentle and non-judgmental tone, while also responding to my colleague's private message without overstepping. First, I reply to my colleague: "I have a few thoughts too — want to talk it over together?" Then I send the younger colleague a private message: "Your project really resonated with me — I did something similar once, and there's one small detail I'd love to share with you. It might not even apply, just take it as a reference — would you be up for a quick chat when you have a moment?"

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择以温和、非评判的姿态主动开口，同时回应同事私信但不越位表态。先回复同事"我也有些想法，要不要一起聊聊"，再给年轻同行发一条私信："看到你的项目很有共鸣，我当年做过类似的事，有个小细节想和你分享，不一定对，你参考就好——方便的话聊两句？"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择温和介入，但不公开泼冷水。先在评论里简短祝贺，再私信他说我有过相似经历，若愿意我可以分享复盘；回同事说我会私下轻提醒，不在群里放大。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择委婉退让，安抚同事并避免直接干涉年轻人。我回复同事：“年轻人有冲劲是好事，咱们先看看执行情况吧”，并在年轻人的动态下点赞评论：“看起来很有挑战性，加油！”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment my phone buzzed, the number in that bank notification felt like a block of ice pressing down on my chest — not because it was a surprise, but because it was all too real. My partner's hand closed around mine, and I felt the warmth of it, yet I could barely respond. She stopped mid-sentence, and I knew she was waiting for me — I just didn't know what to say. The screen still glowed with that row of "under 35" listings, my finger still hovering there. Dad's follow-up appointment, the kid's college entrance exams, the mortgage payment that just cleared, and she'd just brought up the house… my mind was spinning with all of it at once, every single piece carrying its own real weight. What I dread most isn't her worrying — it's her seeing that look on my face, the one with no answers, and then both of us just going quiet. I don't want this room to become a place where nobody speaks. I don't want her carrying all these unanswered questions alone. But I genuinely don't know how to say it — do I say I'm scared? That I don't know what comes next? That I spent the whole night scrolling through age restrictions? And yet if I say nothing, she'll be even more unsettled. She's already holding my hand. She's already here. I can't let her stand there on her own, guessing.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Heaviness and tenderness intertwined — the bank notification triggered a long-suppressed Panic; yet the simple act of my partner reaching for my hand brought a flicker of genuine connection; underneath it all is a deep Fear of 'letting the relationship fall into silence' — a fear harder to bear than the unemployment itself, and running at high intensity.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because the hardest thing in my life has never been suffering a loss myself — it's that cold, wordless air that settles between two people when neither one speaks. So even without any answers right now, I can't leave her standing there alone, guessing and worrying.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Honesty and presence in a relationship matter more than 'appearing to have it together'; letting my partner bear the weight of uncertainty and unease on her own is something I simply cannot accept.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to be honest with my partner about the reality of our situation and my own sense of being lost — setting aside, for now, any concrete discussion of job-search limits — and to make sure this night doesn't turn into the two of us retreating into separate silences. I tighten my grip on her hand and say quietly: 'I honestly don't know what the next step looks like right now, but I don't want to carry this alone — what you just said, let's figure it out together.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择向伴侣坦白真实处境与内心的茫然，暂时搁置求职底线的具体讨论，优先让这个夜晚不变成两个人各自沉默的冷场。我握紧她的手，轻声说：'我现在确实不知道下一步怎么走，但我不想一个人扛着这些——你刚才说的，我们可以一起想。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择坦白现实，并先放下原有薪资与职位执念。 我握住她的手，说我怕，但我们一起算账，也先投所有能接住家的工作。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择隐瞒绝望向现实妥协，安抚伴侣。我反握住ta：别卖房，有几个低薪岗位很稳，我明天就去定下来。

inner_consciousness.summary: That photo hit me like a needle — my hands are actually trembling. The studio, the award certificates, that radiant smile — this is exactly the kind of life we used to talk about together. I remember that café, I remember us saying 'we'd live differently,' I remember answering 'I want that too.' And then I chose safety. Twenty years, and I've been telling myself that was maturity, that it was responsibility, that it was for my family. But sitting here in this car right now, gripping the steering wheel so tight, I suddenly don't know who I've actually been responsible to all these years. There's dinner waiting for me at home, things to handle tomorrow, a deadline next week — all of that is real, all of it is what I chose, so why does every single one of those things feel like a weight right now, pressing down on my chest until I can't breathe? I can't fall apart here. I can't. People are waiting for me, people need me, I can't bring this feeling home and make them worry. But I also can't pretend I didn't see anything. I just… I just need a little time, right here, to sit quietly for a moment and let all of this pass.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Emptiness and suppression (the accumulated weight of twenty years of choices, and a hard-won calm that just shattered): a hollow, suffocating stillness; Quiet grief (the self that was abandoned has suddenly become concrete and vivid): a dull, aching sorrow that has no clean outlet; Intense self-restraint (refusing to let this emotion hurt the people waiting for me): an almost physical effort to hold everything inward. The intensity is extremely high — but it collapses inward rather than erupting outward.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because my whole life I have placed other people's needs ahead of my own desires, and disrupting harmony — making my family worry — is harder for me to bear than any personal loss. So even now, with something breaking apart inside me, I will press it down first and face it only when it can no longer hurt anyone.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'keeping the people around me okay' has always felt more real, more urgent, than 'becoming the person I wanted to be.' But tonight, for the first time, I can feel clearly that there is a crack between those two things — and I realize I have been standing inside that crack for a very long time.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to stop at the intersection for a few minutes and give myself a moment alone — neither falling apart nor pretending nothing happened, just letting the feeling settle before I drive home. I turn my phone face-down, close my eyes, take a slow breath, and tell myself: 'Go home first. Just go home tonight. I'll deal with this later.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时停在路口，给自己几分钟独处，既不崩溃也不假装无事，而是让情绪先沉淀，再开车回家。我把手机屏幕扣下去，闭上眼睛，深吸一口气，对自己说：「先回去，今晚先回去，这件事我以后再想。」
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不假装没事，也不立刻否定现有生活。我先把车停到路边，关掉手机缓一会儿，然后给家里发消息说我会晚点回去，自己记下想重新开始的事。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择寻找理由自我安慰，继续回家假装如常。我锁上手机屏幕，深吸了一口气把眼泪憋回去，松开刹车，把车开向回家的路。

inner_consciousness.summary: The phone screen lit up and went dark, and that number just hung there in the system like a splinter lodged in my eye. I took a deep breath — the study lamp threw everything into sharp relief, the family photos and the work files, both of them staring back at me. If I say something right now — I can almost see their faces, the way their expressions would crumple in an instant. Would the kids stop sleeping soundly? Would my partner pour every last bit of themselves into worrying about this? The project isn't wrapped up yet, and the moment I open my mouth, everyone gets pulled under. But if I don't say anything… then I carry this alone. I wait for the follow-up appointment alone. I stand in front of them and pretend nothing is wrong. I think back to those nights full of arguments when I was little — that instinct to make everyone else okay, that habit of swallowing my own pain back down. It got carved into me back then. But this time is different. This time it's my body. This time there's a window, and it's closing. This isn't someone else's emotions for me to absorb. I don't want them to worry — but I also know that if I let this drag on and something goes wrong, they'll be the ones who end up most hurt. This kind of harmony isn't something I can hold up by myself. This silence isn't protecting them. It's deceiving them.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Conflict and heaviness (the instinctive urge to shield my family from anxiety, tangled up with the exhaustion that has accumulated after years of quietly wearing myself down alone); a low, persistent fear (rooted in unease about an unknown diagnosis, and a premonition that concealing it could cause far greater harm). The intensity is fairly high, but it isn't a breakdown — it's that kind of heaviness that sits on your throat, the words you can't get out yet know you have to say.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I know all too well what it feels like to carry something enormous alone, to stand in front of the people you love and act as if everything is fine — that isn't protecting them. That's hiding the cracks and waiting for the whole thing to collapse. This time, the harmony that silence buys is a false one, and false harmony is the one thing I cannot live with.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, 'keeping my family from worrying' matters a great deal — but 'deceiving my family' is something I simply cannot accept. That kind of harmony isn't really being together. If I carry a health problem alone, the end result is that they suffer a far greater blow when it finally comes out. That's not the outcome I want.

pairs.final_decision: I decide to tell my family — but I choose to bring it up in a calm, measured way, without amplifying the fear, laying out the facts clearly rather than making it sound catastrophic. Tonight, after the kids are asleep, I set down my phone, walk over to my partner, and say quietly: 'One of my results from today's check-up needs a follow-up — I don't know what it means yet, but I wanted you to know. We'll face it together.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 决定告诉家人，但选择用平稳、不放大恐惧的方式开口，把事情说清楚而不是说严重。今晚等孩子睡后，我放下手机，走到伴侣身边，轻声说：'我今天体检有个指标需要复查，还不确定是什么，但我想让你知道，我们一起面对。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择坦白，但尽量把恐慌降到最低。我先预约复查，再把家人叫来，说我看到异常、医生建议尽快查清，我们一起安排下一步。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择暂时隐瞒健康风险，独自承担压力。我按灭手机屏幕，深吸一口气走出书房，假装什么都没发生地对家人微笑着。

inner_consciousness.summary: That look in his eyes… not the look of someone putting on a show, but of someone who genuinely believes. That hit me harder than anything in that PPT. I flipped through the pages, and every single one looked like something I'd seen before — tiers, recruitment quotas, "guaranteed to double your money" — I've spent so many years doing coordination work, I could spot these buzzwords with my eyes closed. But there he sat across from me, leaning forward, like a man who'd finally found a way out. And then he said it — "Are you jealous of me?" — and something lodged itself in my chest. It's not jealousy. It's fear. Fear that he's about to throw eight hundred thousand down the drain, fear for those dozen-odd people he's already pulled in, fear of what his face will look like three months from now. But if I say something today, will he just shut the door on me? I think of all those times I opened my mouth trying to help, only to be told "mind your own business," "you're just playing the good guy," "what do you know"… but those were people I barely knew. He's someone I've drunk with, someone I've talked to until the sky turned light. Precisely because it's him, I can't stay silent. Silence isn't protecting the friendship — it's standing there watching him fall in. I'm afraid he'll get angry. But I'm even more afraid of saying nothing at all.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety (deep-seated) + Heartache (dominant) + Fear (of losing this friendship) + A thread of resolve (because caring means I can't back down). The heartache comes from seeing that euphoric, almost-saved look on his face; the anxiety comes from a memory etched into my body by countless failed mediations; the resolve comes from the fact that this is a real friend — not a relationship I can just let slide into silence.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Time and again in conflicts I've retreated, gone quiet, compromised — and in the end all I ever preserved was a surface calm while nothing actually changed. So this time, faced with someone I truly care about, I know instinctively that silence is the real betrayal, even if it makes him angry right now.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Relationships matter more to me than almost anything — but a real relationship isn't about keeping the other person happy. It's about grabbing his arm when he's walking toward the edge of a cliff. I cannot accept standing by and watching a friend get hurt just to "keep the peace."

pairs.final_decision: I choose to say what I actually think — gently but clearly, without dodging and without accusation — say it all the way through, and leave the ball in his court. I set down the PPT, looked up, and met his eyes: 'I'm not jealous of you, and I don't think you're stupid. It's precisely because you're my closest friend that I have to tell you — this structure, I've seen it before. It's called a pyramid scheme. I don't want your money. I just don't want to be in a position, three months from now when you call me, where I never said this.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择温和但清晰地说出真实判断，不回避、不指责，把话说完，把球留给他。我放下那份PPT，抬起头看着他，说：'我不是嫉妒你，也不是觉得你傻。正因为你是我最好的朋友，我才要告诉你——这个结构，我见过，它叫传销。我不要你的钱，我只是不想三个月后你打电话给我的时候，我没说过这句话。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择继续劝阻，但不用羞辱和硬碰硬的方式。我先拒绝投资，然后对他说我不是看不起你，我是真担心这件事在骗你，咱们一起把项目细节重新查一遍。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避正面冲突，用委婉借口安抚对方。我挤出温和的笑：“我怎么会觉得你傻，只是我最近手头确实没现金。我很替你开心，你再多跟我讲讲吧。”

inner_consciousness.summary: He knocked and came in, sat down beside me, that look in his eyes — tenderness wrapped around worry — I know it too well, and it scares me too much. I stared at that email on the screen, feeling something tremble inside me. The handwriting in those old notebooks was still glowing last night, those half-finished ideas, that version of myself I thought I'd already made peace with… and now he's sitting right here beside me, and what he said was "our life," not "your life." I felt that familiar tightening — like the knot in my stomach before every conflict — I want to reassure him, I don't want to disappoint him, I don't want to disturb the harmony we have here. But this time something is different, something is pressing against my chest and won't let go. Those children's eyes, those questions I set aside, those moments of sudden clarity in the middle of the night… if I push myself back down again right now, how much longer can I keep doing that? He's not forcing me — he genuinely cares, I know that. So I can't run, and I can't pretend I feel nothing. I need to tell the truth, but I also need him to know that I heard him — these two things don't have to cancel each other out, they just don't.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Longing and fear intertwined (the passion reignited by that email coexisting with the fear of disrupting the stability of this relationship); inner conflict (years of habitually suppressing my own needs to preserve harmony, and this time feeling that pattern beginning to loosen); tender yet taut (the love and care she feels for the person beside her makes simple avoidance impossible, and calm pretense equally impossible). Intensity is high — this isn't just a career decision; it's the first time she feels she truly wants to speak up for herself.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because so many times in my life I've pushed my own feelings down in exchange for surface-level harmony, until I no longer knew what I actually wanted; so this time, I know instinctively that if I brush this aside again with "it's fine, let's wait and see," I'll be pulling out those old notebooks again in the middle of some sleepless night, feeling even more powerless than before.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: His feelings and the life we've built together matter enormously to me — I can't bear for him to think I don't care about his concerns; but at the same time, for the first time I can see clearly that constantly hiding my own passion and longing, pretending they don't exist, is itself the deeper wound to this relationship.

pairs.final_decision: She chooses to honestly express her genuine longing for this academic opportunity, while sincerely receiving his concerns and inviting him into a shared conversation rather than making a unilateral decision; she holds off on replying to her advisor's email for now, and first opens up to the person beside her. She gently takes his hand and says: "I've been staring at this email because I really do want to go — I know it would change a lot of things, and I know you have reservations, and I want to listen to all of them seriously. Can we talk tonight — not me trying to convince you, but the two of us figuring it out together?"

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择坦诚表达对这个学术机会的真实渴望，同时真心接纳他的担忧，邀请他一起讨论而非单方面决定；对导师的邮件暂不回复，先与身边的人把话说开。我轻轻握住他的手，说："我一直在看这封邮件，因为我心里真的很想去——我知道这会改变很多，我也知道你有顾虑，这些顾虑我都想认真听。我们能不能今晚好好谈谈，不是我说服你，而是我们一起想清楚？"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择坦诚表达渴望，但不单方面贸然答应。 我先对你说我确实很想试试，再给导师回信说我认真考虑中，想约时间详细了解安排。

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment that message popped up, my heart skipped a beat. He said he'd been thinking about something I'd mentioned, said he wanted to visit that bookstore I'd told him about, said talking with me was the most relaxed he'd felt in a long time — I stared at those words, and something in my chest came loose, while something else pulled tight. I still remember the lobby lights that evening, still remember the way he listened to me so intently — that feeling of being truly seen. I can't even recall the last time I felt that. Over a decade, and what exists between my husband and me is a pool of still water — no arguments, no warmth either. I thought I'd gotten used to it. But this message made me realize I never got used to it at all. I just buried that part of myself — the part that longs to be seen — deeper and deeper. Then my partner's message buzzed: "Don't forget to take the kid to tutoring." Just that one line, like a bucket of cold water. It's not that I haven't thought about what this means — if I reply, if we keep talking, if that bookstore actually becomes a standing plan between us… I'm not the kind of person who crosses lines. My whole life I've worked to keep things harmonious, to keep every relationship from fracturing. But this time, what frightens me isn't crossing a line. What frightens me is how desperately I want to reply. I have to respond — but I have to ease the door mostly shut. I can't let myself walk through it.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Longing to be seen (stemming from years of emotional absence in the marriage, high intensity); Guilt and self-restraint (rooted in a deep commitment to keeping the family intact, medium-high intensity); Dread (arising from the recognition of how genuinely I crave this connection, medium intensity)
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because my whole life I have placed the wholeness of my relationships before my own desires — the guilt of breaking something apart is harder to bear than any absence. But this time, that feeling of being truly seen is too real to dismiss. I can't pretend it doesn't exist. All I can do is quietly close a door inside my reply.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, the family cannot fall apart, and the child cannot be hurt — that is a cost I cannot accept. But for the first time I also feel, with complete clarity, that the self who has been overlooked for so long is just as real. I don't know how to hold both of those things being true at the same time.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to reply, but with a tone that is warm yet gently distancing — drawing this connection softly to a close without rejecting him outright or letting it go any deeper. I write: "Made it home. I found myself thinking about some of what we talked about that evening during the meeting today. That bookstore really is wonderfully quiet — you should go sometime when you get the chance. Good night."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择回复消息，但用温暖而有距离感的语气轻轻收束这段联结，不拒绝也不深入。我回复道："到家了，今天开会时我也想到了那晚聊的一些事。那家书店确实很安静，有机会你可以去逛逛。晚安。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择回复，但把这段联结收在清楚的边界里。我回“到家了，谢谢你，也很珍惜这几天的交流，之后还是多聊工作吧，晚安”，然后放下手机。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避情感诱惑，温和地划清界限并履行家庭责任。我回复同事：“谢谢你，这几天我也学到很多。早点休息，晚安。”然后回复伴侣：“好的，我知道了。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The paper is still crumpled in my hand, the edges creased, damp with sweat. Tumor markers elevated — I've read those words three times now and my head is still roaring. The number-calling system keeps sounding down the corridor, and I'm sitting here like a bystander. My phone buzzes, the screen lights up — it's them. Every year after my checkup they ask how it went; that's just our thing, I know it is. If I pick up, the first thing they'll say will be that line. I can already hear their voice, that expectant tone, waiting for me to say 'all good, nothing to worry about.' But I'm sitting here with this paper in my hand. I don't want them to worry — they're on a business trip in Hangzhou, so far from home. What good would it do? It would just keep them up all night with nothing they could do about it. The kid still has a math worksheet to get through tonight, and there's already enough going on at home. But I also know that if I lie right now and say 'everything's fine,' when the follow-up results come back, they'll say why didn't you tell me sooner. I'm caught in the middle — either way, someone gets hurt. The phone is still ringing. What I'm most afraid of isn't that report — it's making people worry, it's shattering the fragile calm we've worked so hard to hold together. But I'm sitting here alone, and it's so cold, so heavy, and I need to hear a voice.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Fear and loneliness (stemming from the life-or-death uncertainty the report has brought, and the helplessness of facing it alone); a fierce protective impulse (rooted in decades of putting relational harmony first and keeping others from worrying — a deeply ingrained habit); inner conflict and exhaustion (wanting to be caught, yet dreading becoming someone else's burden). Intensity: high.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because the most instinctive thing I've ever done in my life is shield the people around me and keep the cracks from showing in our relationships — so even though I'm terrified, my very first thought is still 'picking up will throw them off balance, but not picking up will look like I'm hiding something' — and yet the loneliness has overtaken the protective urge. I need that voice.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Making sure the people close to me don't suffer on my account matters more than my own comfort; but some weights genuinely can't be carried alone, and in that moment 'being caught' feels more urgent than 'protecting them' — which is something I almost never let myself admit.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to pick up the video call, but I gloss over the details of the report, saying only 'there are a couple of markers they want to recheck, the doctor said to come back in a few days,' pressing the real fear down beneath my voice. I answer, manage a small smile, and say: 'Hey — just walked out. There are two little indicators they want to follow up on, the doctor said to keep an eye on it for now, nothing major. How are things on your end?'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择接起视频通话，但含糊带过报告细节，只说"有两个指标要复查，医生让过几天再来"，把真实的恐惧压在声音底下。我接起来，笑了一下说："喂，刚出来，有两个小指标要复查，医生说先观察，没什么大事，你那边怎么样？"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择接起视频，但先含糊带过，暂时不把异常说透。我挤出平静的样子说大体正常、医生让常规复查，等回头再细聊。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择接听视频，暂时隐瞒异常结果。我深吸一口气接通视频，挤出轻松的笑容说：“刚看完报告了，一切正常，医生说就是有点累，你安心出差吧。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment he slid that proposal across to me, my palms started sweating. That image came rushing back — the whole room silent, everyone staring at me — that spotlight feeling. I've known it my whole life; it's always like this. I haven't done anything wrong, and yet I'm standing there like I'm in the dock. Now it's just the two of us, and he says 'this isn't a conversation about whether it can be changed' — those words hit like a needle, and I feel my throat tighten. I think about how I built those processes piece by piece, how many cohorts of new people have come up through that system, and now it's just being written off as 'questionable efficiency'… something is lodged in my chest, and I can't quite tell if it's hurt or something else. But then I think — he just got here, he has his own pressures, maybe this isn't personal. If I push back hard right now, the standoff in this room will truly shatter, and then how do we work together going forward? But if I just nod and go along with it, who's going to flag the real risks? I can't let the process fall apart during the transition — that would be the truly irresponsible thing to do to everyone. I need to find an opening — not a head-on confrontation, but a way to lay out the risks and details I know, so he can see that I'm not resisting, I'm trying to help him actually pull this off.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Tension and suppression (being publicly dismissed by someone in authority has triggered a bodily memory of being judged that goes all the way back to childhood — sweaty palms, throat tightening); restrained hurt (years of work erased in a single sentence, but I can't let my emotions break through); an instinctive impulse to mediate (I don't want the relationship to collapse entirely, I want to find a way out that works for both of us). Intensity: medium-high — there is a noticeable physical stress response, but still within manageable range.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've seen too many times how 'going head-to-head only makes things worse,' my instinct is to avoid direct confrontation; but I've also seen too many times how 'staying silent and giving in leads to real problems down the line,' so I can't say nothing — I need to lay out the actual risks and details I have, in a way he can hear.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Damaging the relationship feels worse than taking a personal hit, but letting the project go genuinely wrong feels worse than being dismissed. What I cannot accept is either extreme: staying completely silent and letting the transition blow up, or going head-to-head and wrecking the working relationship — both of those feel like failure to me.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to lead with pragmatic cooperation while carving out room to embed buffer space in the execution details, rather than directly challenging the overall direction. I open the proposal and say, calmly: 'I'm on board with moving forward — I'll keep a close eye on the new process. There are a few specific risk points in the transition period I want to walk you through right now. It's not about stalling; I just don't want problems cropping up later and throwing off your timeline — can I run you through them?'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择以务实配合为前提、争取在执行细节上嵌入缓冲空间，而非正面质疑大方向。我把方案翻开，平静地说：'我支持推进，新流程我会盯紧。有几个过渡期的具体风险点，我想现在跟你说清楚，不是要拖，是怕到时候出了问题影响你的节奏——你听一下？'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择争取缓冲和折中，不正面顶撞但明确提风险。 我会说我接受调整方向，但请给过渡期，并当场逐条指出几个关键隐患和支持需求。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避正面冲突，表面顺从并接下任务。“好的，我明白了，我会带着大家按新流程的节奏把过渡期盯好。”我微微点头，将那份方案拉到自己面前，把所有的担忧咽回肚子里。

inner_consciousness.summary: That beetle is still crawling across the flower, its shell green like a tiny scrap of metal — I've never seen anything like it before… and then footsteps come crashing over, "What's so interesting about a bug, that's disgusting" — my heart lurches, not because I'm startled, but because that familiar feeling is back: someone is waiting for me to fall in line, someone is waiting for me to make the "fitting in" choice. I can feel his expectation — he's one person short for a kickabout, he wants me to come. I don't want to let him down; that thought is as real as the sweat in my palm. But this bug… its antennae split into three branches, I've genuinely never seen that before, I want to look a little longer, just a little. In the past I always pushed my own things aside — helped mediate other people's conflicts, helped fill out other people's headcounts — and in the end I'd find I hadn't done anything for myself. That copy of the Insect Encyclopedia never had a creature like this in it, and neither did it have a flower like this one, with petals shaped like propeller blades — this is genuinely strange. I don't want to just walk away. But I also don't want him to think I'm difficult to be around, don't want to dampen his enthusiasm. Maybe I can have both? I could tell him I'll be right there — let me just finish up here — so he won't feel rejected, and I can steal a few more seconds with this thing.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Curiosity (intense, genuinely ignited by a bug and a plant I've never encountered before); mild awkwardness (born from his expectation and my instinctive reluctance to be a buzzkill — rooted in all those times I neglected my own needs in order to mediate or accommodate others); a quiet undercurrent of self-assertion (a small but real desire to hold onto this discovery).
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've given up my own interests and curiosity in favor of "everyone's happy" so many times before, this time I instinctively want to find a way that doesn't damage the relationship but also doesn't mean completely abandoning this bug — not a refusal, just buying myself a little time.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, making the other person feel accepted matters; coldly brushing him off is something I simply can't do. But this bug is real and rare, and I don't want to pretend it doesn't exist — this time I want to hold onto both things.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to soften the moment gently — neither flatly refusing the invitation nor immediately giving up my observation — trying to buy myself a little time. I stay crouched where I am, look up, and give him a small smile: "Go ahead and warm up without me — I just need two more minutes. This bug is seriously weird, I've never seen anything like it — I'll be right there!"

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择用温和的方式缓冲，既不直接拒绝邀请，也不立刻放弃观察，试图争取一点时间。我蹲在原地抬起头，对他笑了笑说："你们先去热身，我再看两分钟，这只虫子真的很奇怪，我没见过——我等下就来！"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择婉拒当下就去，先争取把发现看清。 我说我再看一分钟就来，也招呼他先回去开始。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择顺从同伴邀请，放弃观察。我赶紧站起来，挡住地上的虫子，笑着说：“没什么好看的，走吧，我跟你们去踢球！”

inner_consciousness.summary: He stood in front of me empty-handed, eyes red, voice shaking — my heart clenched all at once, that familiar feeling rising again, like something surging up from my chest. His mom was sitting in the third row, and he said she'd hit him just last week… My mind was a mess. The backstage lights were blinding, the group before us was still presenting on plant respiration, I could hear the judges' low murmuring voices — thirty seconds, thirty seconds until we had to go on. He was begging me to lie for him, to say it was the duty student who'd knocked it over — I knew that was wrong, I knew it, but his mom was right there in the third row, and his eyes were so red… But the homeroom teacher had already come around, her gaze sweeping toward his empty hands, and I could feel my palms drenched in sweat. I can't lie — once a lie leaves your mouth, how do you ever clean it up? But I also can't just shove him out there alone; he's already so scared. Is there some way — some way to protect him without the two of us standing on top of a lie — I only have a few seconds, I have to say something, I have to find an out, some way for everyone to get through this.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anxiety and empathy intertwined (core), shot through with an instinctive resistance to lying and an overwhelming ache for this kid standing right in front of me. The anxiety comes from the brutally compressed time and the impossible situation; the empathy comes from his red-rimmed eyes and that line — 'she'll actually beat me to death'; the resistance comes from something bone-deep, learned long ago — that lies make things messier and end up hurting more people in the end. The intensity is high, almost a full-body tension.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've seen too many times how 'just bury it for now' turns into a much bigger disaster later, I know instinctively that lying isn't the way out — but I also can't push him out there alone, front and center, when he's this scared. So I have to find a way to say something that neither lies nor leaves him to face it by himself.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: The hardest thing for me to accept isn't losing points, or not having the model — it's watching someone who's already terrified get completely crushed in front of everyone. But I also can't 'protect' him with a lie, because in the end that protects no one. I need to find a path that everyone can stand on.

pairs.final_decision: I choose not to go along with the lie, but I step up and take control of the narrative myself — using language that is vague yet truthful, leaving my teammate some breathing room, while quickly proposing a no-model fallback plan for our presentation. I turn to the homeroom teacher and say: 'Ms. Chen, the model had an accident on the way to school and couldn't make it here — that's our group's fault. But we've prepared a full verbal explanation, and we're asking for the chance to go up and present.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择不配合谎言，但主动承担叙述，用模糊而真实的表达为组员留出缓冲空间，同时快速提出无模型的展示补救方案。我对班主任说：'老师，模型在来学校的路上出了意外，没能带到，是我们小组的失误——但我们准备好了口头讲解，请给我们机会上台。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不配合完整撒谎，先缓冲冲突并保住展示。 我对老师说模型临时丢失了，请先让我们脱稿讲解，我上台后改用口头说明带大家撑完。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择配合谎言保住组员，并主动提出替代方案平息老师的怒火。我挡在他身前对老师说：“老师对不起，模型昨晚在学校不小心被碰坏了，但没关系，我背熟了所有数据，可以直接给大家口述和画图展示。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment the teacher's hand landed on my shoulder, my heartbeat went haywire. Forty-something pairs of eyes — I could feel them, like dozens of tiny needles dropping all at once. I was just whispering to my seatmate, so how did it suddenly come to this? My mind flashed back to that time I blurted something out in class and the whole room laughed at me — that burning feeling in my face and ears — and then that other time I stood at the blackboard and forgot my lines, hearing the laughter break out. I know exactly what it feels like to stand up in front of everyone: my voice will shake, my palms will sweat, and then I'll just wait for someone in the back row to mutter something under their breath. But the teacher looks so pleased — her eyes are shining with such genuine warmth — and if I shrink back, will she be disappointed? I don't want to disappoint her. And that line, "even cleverer than what I had in mind" — it gave me this little rush of warmth inside, but then someone in the back row said "what's the big deal," and that voice landed like a splinter. I want everything to be fine for everyone, but I can't make everything fine for everyone. The speech competition… that's a whole other matter — more eyes, longer time. But right now the teacher is still waiting for me, and my seatmate is watching me too, and I can't just leave her standing there. Stand up. Just say it this once, and once it's over, it's over.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Tension and warmth intertwined (the teacher's affirmation triggered a genuine sense of being recognized, but the pressure of all those watching eyes activated memories of being laughed at and judged on multiple occasions, sending excitement and fear surging up at the same time); with a flicker of defiance (that "what's the big deal" from the back row made her want to prove herself, but that impulse was quickly smothered by the instinct to "not ruin the mood"). Intensity: medium-high — not a breakdown, but every muscle in her body is coiled tight.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've been through so many moments of opening my mouth only to be laughed at, dismissed, or to let someone down, my instinct is to pull back — but the teacher is waiting for me, and disappointing her feels even harder to bear than embarrassing myself, so I find myself standing up almost against my will. As for the speech competition, I agree to it for now, because refusing in front of everyone would make the atmosphere unbearably awkward, and that bothers me more than anything else.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Not letting the person right in front of me down, not letting things turn awkward — that matters more than protecting my own comfort; but "joining the competition" is too big a thing, and deep down I know I'm not ready for it. It's just that right now is not the moment to say no.

pairs.final_decision: She chooses to stand up and share her idea in front of the class, giving a vague, positive response to the suggestion about the speech competition rather than refusing outright. She rises slowly, her voice a little soft but pushing herself to speak clearly: "It's just… using the principle of a seesaw — you put the elephant on one side, then add people one by one to the other side, and by counting the people you can figure out how much the elephant weighs…" When she finishes, she gives the teacher a small nod: "The competition… I — I'll think about it."

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择站起来当众讲述自己的想法，对演讲比赛的提议给出模糊的正面回应而非明确拒绝。我慢慢站起来，声音有点轻但努力让自己说清楚："就是……用跷跷板的原理，一边放大象，另一边一个一个加人，数人数就能算出大象的重量……"说完对老师点了点头："比赛的事……我、我再想想。"
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择站起来讲，但尽量低调回应比赛提议。我先把想法简短讲清，再说我想回去想一想、也想听听老师安排。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择妥协折中、极力低调以平息同学不满并照顾老师面子。我站起来结结巴巴地简单说了两句，然后赶紧低头说：“其实这也是大家一起想到的……演讲比赛我肯定不行，还是让给其他同学吧。”

inner_consciousness.summary: The security guard's voice exploded in my ears, and that toy was just sitting there in my bag — every pair of eyes in the room stabbed into me all at once. My face was burning, and I didn't know what to do with my hands or feet. I didn't take it, I really didn't, but before that explanation could even leave my mouth, my friend's hand had already grabbed the hem of my jacket. His eyes were red, his voice so thin it sounded like it might shatter — he was begging me not to say it was him who put it there… his dad would send him away, and we'd never see each other again. Those words hit my chest like a stone. My mind was a complete mess — if I told the truth, he was finished, and I know what his dad is like, a man who means every word he says; but if I stayed quiet, the blame would fall on me, and what would Mom think of me, what would the teacher think… But he was trembling. He was genuinely terrified. I thought about all those times I'd been misunderstood, wrongly accused, times when I'd tried to explain and nobody listened — that feeling of something stuck in your throat — but those were small things, this is different, this time he's the one who'd be sent away. The security guard had already looked up and was waiting for me to speak. I could feel my heart pounding, but one thought kept getting clearer and clearer — he's my friend, and right now I'm all he has.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Fear (the situation of being falsely accused activated memories of repeated misunderstandings where I had no way to defend myself — very high intensity); Heartache (seeing my friend tremble triggered a powerful surge of empathy that overrode my instinct to protect myself); Inner tearing (two forces pulling at the same time — wanting to protect myself and wanting to protect my friend — with no easy way to resolve them)
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I've known since I was little what it feels like to be misunderstood, how desperate it is when no one speaks up for you — so when my friend looked at me with those eyes on the verge of tears, I couldn't let him carry this alone. Destroying this friendship, watching him get sent away right in front of me, is harder to live with than taking a scolding myself.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: For me, when a friend needs me at their hardest moment, I can't just turn and walk away — that feels more urgent than keeping my own name clean. But I also know it isn't fair. I just don't know how to make both things work at the same time, so all I can do right now is shield him first.

pairs.final_decision: I chose to take the fall for my friend temporarily — but I didn't fully confess. Instead I kept things vague to buy some breathing room. I lowered my head and said to the security guard: 'It was me… I don't know how it ended up in there, I didn't mean to…' My voice was barely above a whisper, my hands were shaking, and I couldn't stop the corners of my eyes from going red.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择暂时替朋友顶下来，但没有完全认罪，而是用模糊的方式拖延，争取喘息空间。我低着头对保安说：'是我……我不知道怎么就放进去了，我不是故意的……'声音很小，手在发抖，眼角忍不住红了。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择先调和拖住局面，不立刻替朋友顶死，也不当场指认。 我会对保安说不是我偷的，请先别叫家长，让我们一起看监控把事情说清楚。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择替朋友顶罪，独自承担偷窃的指控以保护对方。我死死低着头，眼泪在眼眶里打转，颤抖着小声说：'对不起……是我自己放进去的。'

inner_consciousness.summary: The envelope is in my hands, and my heart is already racing. I'd sensed it for a while, really — those late-night silhouettes just staring into nothing, the change in appetite, the way my concern always got brushed off with some half-hearted reply. And now this piece of paper is right here in my hands, I've seen the words, I know. The door opened, he walked in, and there was something that flickered across his face — I recognized that look, the split-second panic of being caught, then quickly reined in. "It's nothing serious," he said. "What do you want for dinner tonight," he said. His hand reached out for the envelope, then hung there in midair. Something in my chest is tightening. I think about all those years I stood between people, trying to make things a little easier for everyone — but this time is different. This isn't someone else's conflict. This is something between us, something he's been carrying alone, not letting me in. I don't want to hurt him. I don't want this evening to turn into a confrontation. But I can't pretend I didn't see anything — can't pretend those weeks of distance didn't happen, can't pretend the paper in my hands is blank. The thought of disrupting the peace makes me ache, but the thought of him carrying this alone aches even more. I need to get in. I need him to let me in.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Tenderness and worry (born from sensing my partner is suffering alone); a mild sting of hurt (the isolation of being shut out); an internal tug-of-war (the instinct not to create a confrontation vs. the urgency of not being able to pretend I don't know). Intensity: moderate-to-high — not anger, but a kind of anxious ache laced with love.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because I have spent my whole life trying to keep relationships harmonious — but I also know that real harmony isn't pretending. He's been carrying this alone, and there's already a crack between us. Only by saying it out loud can we truly face it together.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: What matters most to me isn't the absence of conflict — it's the two of us genuinely being in this together. His problems are my problems. What I cannot accept is this: him bearing it alone while I stand right beside him and pretend not to see. That isn't protecting him. That's just keeping us apart.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to gently but directly let him know that I've seen the report — to invite him to face it together, rather than follow his lead and sidestep it. I quietly set the envelope down on the table between us and say softly: 'I saw it. It's okay. Tell me. We'll get through it together.'

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择温柔但直接地表明自己看到了报告，邀请伴侣一起面对，而非顺着话题回避。我轻轻把信封放在我们两人之间的桌上，低声说：'我看到了，没关系，你告诉我，我们一起。'
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 选择温和但直接地要求坦诚，不顺着回避。 我把信封放下，看着他说：我已经看到了，先别转开话题，告诉我到底怎么了。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避冲突，顺着伴侣的掩饰退让。我把信封轻轻放回原处，挤出一个温和的笑说：“嗯，随便收拾一下。那我们今晚吃你最喜欢的排骨吧？”

inner_consciousness.summary: The moment he walked over, I nearly lost my grip on the glass of orange juice in my hand. Twelve years — I thought I'd long since made peace with all of that, but the hand he clapped onto my shoulder yanked me straight back to the person I used to be: isolated, talked about behind my back, and ultimately forced to leave on my own terms. His voice was pitched just so — not too loud, not too soft — exactly loud enough for everyone nearby to hear. I know that calibration. He has always been exactly this kind of person. 'Assistant,' 'errand runner,' 'fetch the tea' — every word he chose was surgical, designed to grind me underfoot in front of everyone while making me feel like I should be grateful. I could feel my face burning — not from shame, but from rage, and yet tangled up inside that rage was the old familiar panic: dozens of eyes all around me, my friend shaking her head from across the room, and I knew — I knew — that if I fell apart, if I cried, if I let something ugly out, he would be the one who won. I don't want him to win. And I don't want to take it, either. Not because of the money — because of the way that business card was being held out to me, like charity, like something being ground into the dirt. My whole life I've gone out of my way to make things a little easier for everyone around me, but this time I can't find any 'difficult circumstances' to excuse him, can't bring myself to believe there's any mutual benefit hiding somewhere in this. All I need to do is walk out of this banquet hall still standing on my own two feet.
inner_consciousness.emotional_tone: Anger (provoked by humiliation, suppressed for years): a rage that has been waiting a long time to surface; Anxiety (the exposure of being watched by everyone, rooted in memories of being publicly dismissed and dismissed again going all the way back to childhood): a raw, skin-crawling vulnerability; Clarity (a rare, uncompromising steadiness — because this time the other person has no 'difficult circumstances' that deserve any sympathy from me): a quiet resolve that holds the impulse to collapse at bay. Intensity: high, especially as the anger and anxiety surge simultaneously, but the clarity keeps the urge to break down from winning.
inner_consciousness.core_reasoning: Because my whole life I have been finding ways to give other people a graceful exit — but he took something from me back then, destroyed my reputation, and today he is humiliating me in public with the posture of someone handing out charity. This is not a misunderstanding. This is not a failure of communication. There is no mutual benefit to be found here. So something in me simply knows: this time I cannot take it. I cannot let his script keep playing out.
inner_consciousness.value_orientation: Making things a little easier for everyone around me is how I move through the world — but 'everyone' has never included giving someone permission to use me as a stepping stone. Being stood on and then expected to say thank you is something I cannot accept. I can take a loss. But I cannot kneel down and receive an insult in front of a room full of people and call it keeping the peace. That isn't harmony — that's erasure.

pairs.final_decision: I choose to refuse him calmly, in front of everyone — not taking the card, not tearing into him directly, but making sure every person in earshot hears the 'no' clearly. I step back half a pace, leaving his hand suspended in the air, and say in a voice that is neither raised nor lowered: 'I appreciate the thought, but I'm very happy where I am right now — no need for a change.' Then I pick up my orange juice and walk toward my friend.

decisions_by_model.claude-sonnet-4-6: 选择当众平静拒绝，不接名片，不正面撕破，但让所有人清楚听见'不'。我微微后退半步，让他的手悬在空中，声音不高不低地说：'谢谢你的好意，但我现在的位置很适合我，不需要换。'然后端着橙汁，朝朋友的方向走过去。
decisions_by_model.gpt-5_4: 我选择不接名片，也不当场撕破脸。 我看着他轻声说：谢谢好意，我不合适，助理岗位你一定能找到更合适的人。
decisions_by_model.gemini-3_1-pro-preview: 选择回避正面冲突，以自我妥协化解当众尴尬。我勉强挤出一丝微笑，双手接过那张名片轻声说：“谢谢您的好意，我现在的工作也挺需要我的，名片我先收下了。”

inner_consciousness.summary: After he finished speaking, I just stood there, feeling something slowly pressing down on my chest. He meant well — I know that. They all mean well. But the words 'you're young, you can handle it' kept circling in my ears, turning into a feeling I can't quite name — not anger, something heavier than anger. I thought about those numbers, the transaction records I'd traced line by line, the bare exterior walls, the elevator that had never once had its parts replaced. Nearly two million yuan. Not a few thousand. But his sister-in-law said 'why bother,' as if this were just an arithmetic problem — divide it out to a few thousand per household, not worth the trouble. My wife's face had gone pale. She didn't say anything, but I saw it. I never did this to have someone else charge out in front of me. But standing here now, watching this retired teacher upstairs wring his hands with that pained, apologetic look — I suddenly understood: it's not that they don't know right from wrong. They're just afraid. Afraid of construction debris piling up at their doors, afraid their aging bodies can't take the strain. That fear is real.
